_comment: Health-review backlog — the parking lot for MINOR SYSTEM / PIPELINE / DOC issues a session noticed but did not fix (a stale comment, a small wiring gap, a doc that needs a touch, a flaky test). Per docs/CLAUDE-RULES-CANONICAL.md the session-end routine appends here instead of letting small issues rot. The autonomous /health-review skill drains this list every run: it triages each open item, fixes what it can, and marks it resolved. Do NOT use this for live-trading incidents or anything Tier-2/3 that needs immediate attention — raise those with the operator in chat. Three-way split (2026-05-26): STRATEGY / TRADING follow-ups go in docs/claude/performance-review-backlog.json; AI/ML EXPERIMENT follow-ups go in docs/claude/ml-review-backlog.json. Item ids here: BL-YYYYMMDD-NNN.

items.source: src/prop/prop_journal.py::insert_fill — plain INSERT, no dedup. Live evidence: breakout_1 prop_fills id 15 (buy) + id 16 (long) are the SAME ETH trade (external_order_id 500139412) logged twice (the buy/long direction-synonym correction saga, BL-20260705-PROP-DIRECTION-SYNONYM-MATCH).
items.title: [prop] insert_fill is a blind append (no idempotency) — a re-reported / duplicate fill creates a second prop_fills row
items.description: prop_journal.insert_fill does a straight INSERT with no dedup guard, so re-posting the same fill (operator re-report, corrective re-report, or a relay retry) creates a duplicate prop_fills row. This is the SECONDARY double-log vector behind BL-20260706-PROP-CLOSE-MISLINK (the primary — a close linking to the wrong ticket — was fixed in #5744). Concrete residue: prop_fills 15/16 are the same trade (ext order 500139412) as two rows. FIX (Tier-2, prop DB write path): make insert_fill idempotent/upsert-ish on a natural key — (account_id, external_order_id, status) when external_order_id is present, else (account_id, ticket_id, status, qty, exit_price) — updating the existing row instead of inserting a duplicate; or at minimum flag/supersede the older duplicate in raw/notes. Prop journal is isolated from real-money/paper KPIs so this is data-hygiene, not money-at-risk. Verify with a re-post of an existing fill producing no new row.

items.source: GET /api/bot/prop/tickets?account_id=breakout_1 (via diag relay #5742/#5790, 2026-07-06): ~5 ETH-long tickets stuck in status='emitted' from 2026-07-05 (04:05/10:05/10:08/12:18), all past valid_until, never filled/expired; plus ~17 ETHUSDT order_packages in status='orphaned' (trend_donchian_eth_prop shadow-soak + a few live legs) with ticket_id null.
items.title: [prop] breakout_1 has stale never-resolved 'emitted' prop tickets (pre-suppress-guard) + orphaned ETH order-packages — cosmetic backlog
items.description: On breakout_1, ~5 ETH-long prop tickets from 2026-07-05 sit in status='emitted' past their valid_until — they predate the re-ticket-suppress guard (BL-20260705-PROP-RETICKET-WHILE-OPEN, which now suppresses new emits while a position is open) and the close-mislink fix (#5744), so nothing ever moved them to 'expired'. They're harmless (past validity; a close can no longer mis-link to them after #5744) but they clutter the tickets view and the prop reconcile 'unacted' count. Separately, ~17 ETHUSDT order_packages read status='orphaned' (mostly trend_donchian_eth_prop shadow-soak artifacts — execution:shadow, no ticket expected — plus a few live legs). FIX (Tier-1/2 hygiene): a small pass to expire the stale past-valid emitted tickets (mirroring the expiry-prompt terminal state) and/or reconcile the orphaned shadow-soak order-packages; could reuse/extend the fix-prop-mislinked-close tooling pattern. Low priority — no money impact, no open position affected.

items.opened_by: claude (order-qty-rounding-issue session; prop ETHUSD close report-back)
items.source: prop-report.yml run 28794297803 (issue #5734) comment: the ingest returned HTTP 200 (success) but the run log showed 'bash: line NN: /dev/null: Permission denied' at four points and '(no response body)' — the endpoint's JSON response ({ok,kind,status,ticket_id,...}) was not echoed back to the issue comment.
items.title: [workflow] prop-report.yml can't write /dev/null on the VM step, so the endpoint response body isn't echoed back (ingest still succeeds)
items.description: The issue-driven prop-report relay (.github/workflows/prop-report.yml, or its VM-side curl wrapper) redirects some output to /dev/null and hit 'Permission denied' four times during the 2026-07-06 ETHUSD close report-back — a symptom of the same OCI host-side /dev/null-clobbering pattern already tracked (BL-20260629-DEVNULL-CLOBBERED-LIVE-VM): /dev/null on the VM is a regular non-writable file, not the char device. Consequence is purely observability: the POST still returned HTTP 200 (so the prop_fills write + ticket-link + prop_closed notification all fired), but the endpoint's JSON response body was swallowed, so the confirmation comment reads '(no response body)' and a future reporter can't see {ok,ticket_id,...}. FIX (Tier-1, cosmetic): (a) capture the curl response body to a tmp file / var instead of relying on /dev/null redirects in the report step, and/or (b) re-assert /dev/null as a char device at the top of the VM step (the mitigation used elsewhere for the DEVNULL class). Verify by re-running a prop-report and confirming the JSON body appears in the comment. Low priority — no data was lost and the 200 is authoritative.

items.source: journalctl -u ict-git-sync.service on the live VM: every fetch succeeded cleanly through 07:08:59Z (pulled 200a536c fine), then EVERY run since 07:14:18Z fails identically: 'fatal: could not read Username for "https://github.com": No such device or address' (exit 128). Confirmed via two independent pull-and-deploy system-action dispatches (issues #5659, #5661) at 07:55Z and 07:59Z, both hitting the exact same error — not transient.
items.title: ict-git-sync auto-deploy broken VM-wide since ~07:14Z today — git fetch can't authenticate to GitHub
items.description: Auto-deploy (`ict-git-sync.timer`, fires every ~5min) has been failing on EVERY run for the last ~50+ minutes as of this writing — this blocks ALL merged PRs from reaching the live VM, not just one. The live trader process itself (`ict-trader-live.service`) is unaffected and healthy (confirmed via the liveness-watchdog journal + /api/diag/services) — this is purely the deploy pipeline. `/api/diag/version` still reports git_sha=200a536c, the last commit that landed before the failure started. This directly blocked deploying the operator-approved IB liveness-probe fix (PR #5654, merged as ee522ca) for verification. Root cause is unknown from the diag surface available (no dispatchable action exists to inspect/fix a VM-side git credential — unlike the trainer VM's full-SSH relay, the live VM only has the fixed `system-actions.yml` allowlist + GET-only diag). The 'could not read Username' signature is the canonical symptom of an HTTPS git remote with no working credential (expired/revoked PAT, or a credential helper that stopped resolving) — git falls back to an interactive username prompt, which fails headless. Timing note: this VM has an open, known pattern of an OCI host-side agent silently mutating filesystem state without warning (BL-20260629-DEVNULL-CLOBBERED-LIVE-VM / BL-20260629-DEVNULL-OCI-SOURCE-KILL) — worth checking whether the same class of interference touched `~/.git-credentials` / the git credential helper around 07:09-07:14Z, though this is unconfirmed.
items.trigger_condition: Needs VM-side inspection this repo's current tooling can't do read-only (no arbitrary-shell relay for the live VM). Likely needs either (a) the operator to check/refresh whatever git credential the VM's origin remote relies on, or (b) a system-action addition (mirroring the gateway VM's unrestricted diag pattern, scoped read-only) if this needs to be self-diagnosable going forward.
items.resolution_criteria: ict-git-sync.service fetch succeeds again; a subsequent pull-and-deploy lands ee522ca (or later) and /api/diag/version reflects it.

check_log.note: ROOT CAUSE CONFIRMED (operator): the repo was flipped from public to private this session, right around the failure window — the VM's anonymous HTTPS `git fetch` had never needed a credential before and now does. Fix prepared (PR TBD): deploy_pull_restart.sh now authenticates via an inline Basic-auth header built from a new optional VM_GIT_DEPLOY_TOKEN (fine-grained, Contents:Read-only PAT), read from .env (systemd EnvironmentFile= already exports it; a targeted single-line grep fallback covers a direct SSH/pull-and-deploy invocation). Falls back to the current plain fetch when unset, so this is a no-op until the token exists. Wired into the existing sync-vm-secrets.yml OPTIONAL_SECRETS propagation path (same mechanism as DASHBOARD_API_TOKEN). Operator is creating the token now. KNOWN GAP not yet addressed: the dedicated IB-Gateway VM has no .env by design and also does a plain `git clone`/fetch from this same repo (deploy/ib-gateway-cloud-init.yaml + the gateway branch of deploy_pull_restart.sh) — unverified whether it's currently affected; needs a separate propagation step if so. Filed as a standalone follow-up rather than blocking this fix.

RESOLVED. See resolution_note for the full two-recurrence history. Final state: global git credential set on ict-bot-arm, on-disk deploy_pull_restart.sh current (no duplicate-header bug), ict-trader-live.service restarted onto d5276f74, /api/diag/version confirms git_sha=d5276f74. Deploy pipeline is unblocked — other concurrent sessions were told they could resume.

items.resolution_note: Recurred twice after the initial fix before landing clean. (1) The per-invocation http.extraheader approach (first fix attempt) caused a duplicate-Authorization-header 400 once a global git credential was also set — http.extraheader is multi-valued, confirmed experimentally, not just assumed. Fixed by reverting deploy_pull_restart.sh to a plain fetch and relying solely on the global credential set by vm-git-credential-bootstrap.yml (PR #5678). (2) Even after that merged, pull-and-deploy failed identically again because the ON-DISK deploy_pull_restart.sh was itself still the old, broken pre-fix copy — same deadlock shape one level deeper. Fixed by extending vm-git-credential-bootstrap.yml to git reset --hard origin/main + invoke the deploy script directly when the worktree reads behind origin/main (PR #5681). Live-verified end to end: dispatched the bootstrap (issue #5682, exit 0, correctly reported the worktree behind origin/main + did the reset + ran the deploy script), caught that the deploy script itself skipped the service restart (zero diff after the outer reset) and explicitly dispatched restart-bot-service (issue #5683) to actually load the new code, then confirmed /api/diag/version reports git_sha=d5276f74 (the merged commit). ict-git-sync.timer now authenticates on every scheduled fetch.

items.title: Package watchdog marks execution:shadow packages 'orphaned' — shadow soak noise pollutes orphan metrics; give them a distinct terminal status
items.description: Tier-2 observability fix: when the owning strategy resolves execution:shadow at watchdog time (or the package is flagged shadow at creation), close the package as e.g. 'shadow_expired' (or 'shadow_logged') instead of 'orphaned', excluded from orphan-rate analytics. Keeps 'orphaned' meaning what reviews assume: an executed decision whose trade went missing.

The prop emit path does not consult open prop positions — prop fills live in prop_fills, not the trades table the netting guard / has_open_trade_for_strategy reads.
items.title: Prop emit path re-tickets while a prop position is already open — operator gets repeat tickets for the held (symbol, direction)
items.description: DECISION FOR OPERATOR (Tier-2/3, changes emission behaviour): should ticket emission for (account, symbol, direction) be suppressed while an unclosed prop_fills position exists (mirroring the netting-guard monocle), or kept as-is (operator gates every ticket; expiry prompt cleans up unacted ones)? If suppressing: check open prop position via prop_journal (fills with status open/filled and no later closed row) inside breakout_executor before emit, and journal a suppressed-reticket rejection row. Related: BL-20260628-PROP-ISPROP-PREDICATE-DRIFT.
items.trigger_condition: Operator decides suppress-vs-keep; then a prop-bridge code session.
items.resolution_criteria: Either an explicit operator 'keep as-is' recorded here, or the suppression shipped + a re-signal while open journals a suppression instead of a ticket.

updates.note: OPERATOR DECIDED: SUPPRESS ('we should have really one ticket sent for each trade that should be placed'). Implemented in src/prop/breakout_executor.py: _reticket_suppress_reason gates emit_prop_ticket per (account, symbol, direction) — suppresses when an OPEN prop position exists (find_open_prop_positions) OR a still-live outstanding ticket exists (placed / expiry_prompted / awaiting_report, or emitted within valid_until); an EXPIRED unacted ticket does NOT block a fresh signal. Suppressions journal a prop_tickets row status='suppressed' (no push) so the decision stays auditable; guard is fail-open on read errors. 4 regression tests in tests/test_prop_ticket_journaling.py. Also fixes the same-tick variant collision (eth_pullback_2h + eth_pullback_prop_2h both ticketing 3 min apart). LIVE VERIFICATION: next re-signal while the current ETH position is open should journal a suppressed row, not page the operator.

items.source: /api/diag/exchange_positions 08:12Z: ib_paper positions=null (could-not-read) while /api/bot/accounts/balances 08:01Z shows ib_paper api_ok:true balance 1,271,418.77 and 0 open positions. Sunday, CME closed; no IB position at risk.
items.title: RESOLVED (reclassified): diag exchange_positions probe can't read ib_paper over the cross-host relay — the trader's real IB connection is healthy, not a live risk
items.description: If exchange_positions still returns null for ib_paper after Sunday's market open, run vm-ib-gateway-recover and check the ict-ib-gateway-watchdog journal on the gateway VM. Zero open IB positions today, so no monitor-blindness exposure; do not burn a restart during the IBKR weekend state.
items.trigger_condition: Re-probe /api/diag/exchange_positions after 2026-07-05T22:00Z.
items.resolution_criteria: ib_paper positions returns a list (or a confirmed gateway recover restores it); item closed with the probe result.

updates.note: Gateway restart fired 2026-07-05T22:12Z via vm-ib-gateway-recover — succeeded cleanly (IBC login_completed=yes, api reachable, 22:12:34Z). Re-probed /api/diag/exchange_positions well after the ~2min circuit-breaker clear window: ib_paper STILL reads positions:null. BUT cross-checked the trader's OWN persistent connection: mgc_trend_1h has been evaluating MGC every ~3 min with live real-time prices through 06:28Z (close 4162.3->4157.7->...), including an actionable sell signal dispatched at 06:05:49Z (confidence 0.0539, correctly below threshold to execute) and another eval at 06:08:56Z (confidence 0.3476). /api/bot/accounts/balances confirms ib_paper api_ok:true, balance stable at $1,271,418.77, open_positions:0 (genuinely flat, not stale-masked). CONCLUSION: the live trading connection is fully healthy — this was never a real wedge. The diag relay's exchange_positions endpoint opens a SEPARATE short-lived read-only IB client per the diag-relay design, and that specific connection path appears to hit the same cross-host socat-relay quirk CLAUDE.md documents for IB_PROBE_TIMEOUT_S (reqCurrentTime doesn't resolve over the relayed gateway even when the persistent connection is healthy). Reclassified from 'operational IB issue' to 'diag-tooling limitation, no live impact' — closing. Do NOT burn another gateway restart on a future null exchange_positions read for ib_paper without first cross-checking live *_eval activity + accounts/balances api_ok, per this note.

STRUCTURAL FIX LANDED, superseding the diag-tooling-limitation reclassification above (PR #5789, branch claude/intelligent-dijkstra-hs2271). Root-caused the actual mechanism (not just the symptom): IBClient.connect() verified liveness (reqCurrentTime) but never guaranteed the FIRST real accountSummary()/portfolio() data had landed before declaring success — ib_insync's accountSummary() only populates from an explicit reqAccountSummary() subscription (never called anywhere in this repo) and, per ib_insync's own RequestTimeout defaulting to 0 (unbounded), that lazy first call had NO timeout of its own; portfolio() similarly depends on reqAccountUpdates(), which connect()'s own init requests race with a silently-swallowed timeout that never fails connect(). So account_open_positions()'s net_liquidation disambiguation (this item's title) could misreport 'not logged in' on a perfectly healthy gateway whenever the diag read client's cache was cold (first use, or a fresh reconnect after a silent idle-timeout drop) — exactly this item's symptom, and it recurred again in the 2026-07-06 weekly /system-review. Fix: IBClient.connect() now runs a bounded account/portfolio WARM-UP (subscribe + wait for real data, retry-once-then-condemn — same shape as the existing liveness probe) right after the liveness probe succeeds, gated to run once per underlying connection (a still-open cached handle skips it; a fresh connect or a reconnect after an idle drop re-runs it). New env var IB_ACCOUNT_WARMUP_TIMEOUT_S (default 8.0s; <=0 opts out). By the time connect() returns success, balance()/positions() are always served from an already-warm cache — no cold race left to lose. Regression-tested (delayed data still succeeds; a genuinely wedged gateway is still bounded + trips the breaker; reconnect-after-idle-drop re-warms); full existing IB/accounts/reconciler suite (217+ tests) still green. NOT YET DEPLOYED to the live VM — this is Tier-2 (live IB connection/read behaviour); needs an explicit operator OK before restart-bot-service/pull-and-deploy is dispatched. This item stays 'resolved' (its own symptom was benign that day); tracking the deploy-and-verify follow-through as a NEW item is the correct next step if the operator wants a dedicated tracker, but the fix itself is code-complete and PR-linked here.

The 2026-07-06T20:00 warm-up fix above was deployed to the live VM and the concurrency bug it uncovered (MB-20260706-IBCONCURRENCY, separate PR #5809) was also fixed and deployed — but a clean single-request retest of /api/diag/exchange_positions?account_id=ib_paper AFTER both fixes still returned positions:null, with the web-api journal showing 'account/portfolio warm-up timed out twice (8s + 8s retry)' for the diag read client specifically, while the trader's own persistent connection (different clientId) kept evaluating mgc_trend_1h on fresh real-time prices the entire time. Root-caused a SECOND, deeper mechanism: reqAccountUpdates (the call portfolio()/positions() depends on) is a persistent PER-ACCOUNT subscription. The trader's own execution connection (clientId 496/497) already holds it for ib_paper; the diag/reconciler read client (ib_read_client_for, a distinct PID-salted clientId, e.g. 9585) opening a SECOND concurrent reqAccountUpdates subscription for the SAME account is a documented IB-API multi-client collision — the Gateway does not reliably deliver a fresh accountDownloadEnd to the second subscriber. Structural fix (not a longer timeout): a readonly client's warm-up never subscribes to reqAccountUpdates at all, and IBClient.positions() now routes readonly reads through reqPositions() instead of portfolio() — a stateless, one-shot request IBKR documents as safe for any number of concurrent clients against the same account. Cost: no unrealizedPNL on this path (unrealised_pnl is None for an IB readonly read, the same 'not measured' contract dashboard.py::_broker_unrealised_for_trade already handles). The trader's own non-readonly connection is completely untouched — still portfolio()/reqAccountUpdates, unchanged. New tests added (TestReadonlyAccountUpdatesCollision in tests/test_ib_integration.py) proving the readonly warm-up never calls reqAccountUpdates even when it would hang, and that positions() uses reqPositions() not portfolio() for a readonly client. Full IB/accounts/reconciler/diag suite green. NOT YET DEPLOYED — Tier-2, needs operator OK before restart-bot-service/pull-and-deploy, then a clean re-verify of exchange_positions for ib_paper.

updates.by: claude (BL-20260706-IBACCTUPDATES-COLLISION deploy + live verify, same session)
updates.note: DEPLOYED AND LIVE-VERIFIED. PR #5819 merged (squash sha 2522bd27) and shipped via pull-and-deploy (issue #5822) — post-deploy HEAD 2522bd27, ict-trader-live.service + ict-web-api.service both active. Confirmed the running web-api process actually loaded the fix via /api/diag/version (issue #5823): git_sha "2522bd27", exact match. Clean single retest of /api/diag/exchange_positions?account_id=ib_paper (issue #5824, empty body to avoid the batching-parser quirk) returned {"positions": [], "count": 0, "error": null} — a genuine successful read (not the prior could-not-read null): reqPositions() completed against the Gateway with no error, and ib_paper is confirmed genuinely flat. This is the definitive live proof the reqAccountUpdates multi-client collision is resolved for the diag/reconciler read path. Closing the investigation chain (BL-20260705 -> BL-20260706-IBWARMUP -> BL-20260706-IBCONCURRENCY -> BL-20260706-IBACCTUPDATES-COLLISION) as fully resolved end-to-end.

items.source: 2026-06-28 intrusion-surface audit (docs/security/intrusion-surface-audit-2026-06-28.md). External non-collaborator danleejames23 (author_association NONE) opened issues #2680/#2681/#2688 probing the issue-driven Actions automation (#2688: 'Apply system-action label to deploy'); contained (57 runs all skipped, no label applied, no secret/VM step ran). Tier-1 hardening MERGED: #4965 (P0 actor-guards + audit doc), #4967 (P1 guard sweep, 38 workflows), #4970 (external-issue detection alert), #4972 (list-listening-ports read action), #4971 (comms-handler chat-auth, Tier-2 operator-approved), dashboard #129 (delete-merged-branches guard). These are the REMAINING follow-ups, most operator-only (GitHub settings / 2FA / broker keys) — surface them to the operator at the next /system-review.
items.title: Security hardening follow-ups (operator-only + Tier-2) from the 2026-06-28 intrusion audit
items.description: REMIND THE OPERATOR at the next /system-review. Checklist (all still open as of 2026-06-28): (1) [operator, HIGHEST LEVERAGE] Enable hardware-2FA/passkey on the benbaichmankass GitHub account — it is now the single linchpin: every issue-driven workflow trusts 'actor == repository_owner', so a phished owner account = full control (VM SSH key, deploy, account-mode). (2) [operator] Settings -> Actions -> General: set 'Require approval for all outside collaborators' (public repos -> any fork PR currently runs pytest/ruff = code execution on the runner) AND default GITHUB_TOKEN workflow permissions to read-only. (3) [operator] Branch-protect main on ict-trader-android (and re-verify ict-trader-dashboard now that PRs flow there) — both were unprotected; protect .github/ + require review so the new actor-guards can't be quietly removed. Consider CODEOWNERS on .github/workflows/. (4) [operator] Enable secret-scanning push protection on the two public repos (free; GHAS alert API not reachable from the MCP). (5) [operator+claude] Confirm DASHBOARD_API_TOKEN IS set on the live VM (PR #4968 / docs/security/api-network-hardening-PLAN-2026-06-28.md §0 gives the one-line on-VM probe: curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' http://127.0.0.1:8001/api/bot/devices -> 200=UNSET/fail-open, 401=set) BEFORE approving any fail-closed API change. (6) [Tier-2, claude-prep] The API/network hardening plan (#4968): timing-safe hmac.compare_digest token compare (non-breaking, shippable now), make DASHBOARD_API_TOKEN fail-closed, require auth on the currently-unauthenticated POST /api/bot/devices/register (needs an Android client change), and bind :8001 to loopback + TLS reverse proxy + bearer (Streamlit Cloud has NO stable egress IPs, so an IP allowlist is NOT viable). (7) [operator, desktop/CLI] Pin appleboy/ssh-action@v1.0.3 to a commit SHA (handles VM_SSH_KEY; the sandbox proxy 403s the GitHub API for out-of-scope repos so the SHA couldn't be verified here). (8) [claude, autonomous now] Fire the new list-listening-ports system-action (action: list-listening-ports) to capture the live VM's actual port exposure and confirm only SSH+8001 are public and the IB Gateway 4002 is loopback/private. (9) [operator, precautionary — no evidence of compromise] Consider rotating BRANCH_PROTECTION_TOKEN (longest-lived PAT) and re-verify the live Alpaca key (see BL-20260627-ALPACA-LIVE-API-UNAUTH).
items.trigger_condition: Next /system-review (surface checklist to operator); items 1-4,7,9 are operator-only GitHub/broker actions, items 6 is Tier-2 claude-prep awaiting operator OK, item 8 is autonomous and can be fired now.
items.resolution_criteria: Operator has actioned (or explicitly declined) each checklist item; the Tier-2 API/network changes (item 6) are either shipped behind operator approval or recorded as deferred; the live port inventory (item 8) has been captured and shows no unexpected public exposure.

items.source: src/prop/ line-by-line audit (docs/audits/s-audit-f/prop-bridge.md F3). Three call sites independently classify 'is this a prop account' with DIFFERENT predicates: prop_journal._prop_scope (exchange==breakout OR account_class==prop OR type==prop OR backtest_ruleset!=standard), telegram_report_handler.default_prop_account (exchange==breakout OR account_class==prop only), account_rulesets.unit_for_account (backtest_ruleset!=standard OR exchange==breakout).
items.title: Three divergent is_prop_account predicates across src/prop/ — consolidate to one helper
items.description: Latent multi-account drift. Today the single prop account (breakout_1) matches all three predicates so they agree, but a future prop account declared by account_class:prop WITHOUT exchange:breakout would be invisible to default_prop_account's disambiguation, and one declared only by backtest_ruleset would be invisible to both default_prop_account and the journal-side reconcile scope. Fix: extract one canonical is_prop_account(acct: dict)->bool and have _prop_scope, default_prop_account, and unit_for_account all call it. Tier-1 refactor, no behaviour change under the current single account.
items.trigger_condition: Next /health-review, or before any second prop account is added to accounts.yaml.
items.resolution_criteria: A single is_prop_account helper is the only prop-classification predicate in src/prop/; the three call sites delegate to it; a test covers an account_class:prop-without-breakout account being recognised by all three.

items.source: src/prop/prop_journal.py::record_ticket ON CONFLICT (docs/audits/s-audit-f/prop-bridge.md F2b). The upsert updates status/qty/valid_until/message but NOT order_package_id; a re-emit of the same ticket_id with a now-populated order_package_id would not backfill the NULL.
items.title: record_ticket upsert can't backfill a NULL order_package_id on re-emit
items.description: Latent. ticket_ids are freshly generated per emit (prop-manual-<uuid>), so a true same-id re-emit is rare — but if it happens after an earlier emit wrote NULL order_package_id, the ticket<->order-package join the dashboard prop view + reconcile rely on stays silently broken (the same join class as the 2026-06-21 prop-tickets incident). Docstring already clarified (S-AUDIT-F) that on-conflict columns other than status/qty/valid_until/message are NOT updated. If re-emit-with-late-opid is ever observed, extend the upsert to COALESCE order_package_id (excluded.order_package_id over the existing) — Tier-2 (journal write contract).
items.trigger_condition: Any observed prop_tickets row with NULL order_package_id despite the order package existing; or a session touching record_ticket.
items.resolution_criteria: Either confirmed non-issue (ticket ids are always fresh, no same-id re-emit path exists) and recorded as such, or the upsert COALESCEs order_package_id and a regression test covers re-emit backfill.

items.source: system-review 2026-06-27 12:00Z→2026-06-28 04:00Z window: 6 of 8 paper closed trades on alpaca_options_paper closed at the IDENTICAL microsecond timestamp 2026-06-27T19:05:48.058497Z, all closeReason='other' (not tp/sl), with implausible exit prices — SLV exit 53.29 vs entry 58.82 (-9.4%), QQQ 704.94 vs 738.51 (-4.5%), SPY 732.14 vs 745.24 — moves that cannot occur intrabar in minutes. Two more (3001/3003) closed similarly at 18:49. Produces large fake realised PnL (+525 SLV / -537 QQQ / -262 SPY) totalling a -$845.65 paper figure that is an ARTIFACT, not strategy performance. Recurs prior-report note 'paper reconciler-artifact-heavy'.
items.title: Pre-fix phantom paper rows from options-account orphan-adoption (root cause FIXED in #4858+#4867; residual = data cleanup + verify)
items.description: The intraday ETF cells (gld/slv/spy/qqq/tlt _1h) route to alpaca_options_paper and are being closed en masse by the reconciler (closeReason 'other') at a single instant with mark prices that are wildly wrong (underlying-vs-option pricing mismatch suspected: qty is treated as shares, e.g. SLV 95 × (58.82-53.29)=525.35, yet the exit price 53.29 is not a real SLV print). Net effects: (1) paper analytics/-/performance are contaminated by fabricated PnL — the -$845.65 window figure is meaningless; (2) the same reconciler path closed a REAL-money trade (bybit_2 ict_scalp #2995) with closeReason 'reconciler' rather than tp/sl, so the artifact mechanism is not paper-isolated. Investigate _reconcile_orphan_exchange_positions / _sweep_local_pnl_for_unpriced for alpaca_options_paper: why 6 positions read absent and were closed at one tick, and why the close-side mark price is implausible. Likely related to the options-expression overlay pricing ETF legs as the underlying.
items.trigger_condition: Immediately actionable (diagnosis); fix is order-monitor/reconciler code (Tier-2).
items.resolution_criteria: alpaca_options_paper ETF positions close via genuine TP/SL or a correctly-priced exit (no identical-timestamp mass-closes, no impossible intrabar exit prices); paper PnL reflects real fills; real-money closes never carry an artifact 'reconciler' close with a fabricated mark.

updates.note: ROOT-CAUSED + already fixed in code. Mechanism: alpaca_options_paper holds us_option legs for its ETF debit-vertical expressions. Pre-fix, the reverse reconciler's account_open_positions returned those legs as adoptable positions and — not recognising the account as options-expressing — adopted them as equity `adopted_orphan` trades (entry = underlying spot), which the local-PnL sweep then priced with the EQUITY formula (local_markprice x qty x contract_value_usd=1.0) -> fabricated +/-$ hundreds (the -$845 paper figure). Trades 2999-3006 were adopted 18:47-18:56 and closed 19:05:48. FIXES (both live in deployed sha 5a2db1f2 since the ~20:08 restart): #4858 (19:01Z) made account_open_positions options-aware (options account -> returns ONLY us_option, so the reconciler no longer adopts the legs as equity orphans); #4867 (20:04Z) added the _sweep_local_pnl_for_unpriced options-defer guard (account_expresses_options -> skip the equity formula). The 18:47 adoptions PREDATE the ~20:08 deploy. Post-deploy trades (3007-3014, all >=21:42Z) show ZERO new alpaca_options_paper adoptions -> fix holding. RESIDUAL (no new code): (a) verify clean over 24-48h; (b) Tier-2 DB writeback to mark the ~8 pre-fix phantom paper rows reconcile_status='superseded' so they stop polluting paper KPIs (currently 'reconciled' = still counted). Operator-gated. NOTE: real-money closes are unaffected — #3008/#2995 closed via bybit_closed_pnl (broker truth), benign.

items.opened_by: claude (alpaca-options pilot wrap — operator directive to log it for system-review)
items.source: During the options Slice 3b/4/5 work, /api/bot/accounts/balances + the test-alpaca-creds workflow showed alpaca_live api_ok:false with 'request is not authorized'. This is the REAL-MONEY Alpaca account (own key pair ALPACA_API_KEY_ID_LIVE / ALPACA_API_SECRET_KEY_LIVE), separate from the shared paper login the options pilot runs on (which is healthy).
items.title: alpaca_live api_ok:false ('request is not authorized') — real-money Alpaca key pair not authorizing; blocks any alpaca_live go-live (incl. real-money options)
items.description: SURFACE TO THE OPERATOR at the next /system-review. The real-money Alpaca account `alpaca_live` reports `api_ok:false` with a 'request is not authorized' error on the balances/creds probes. The paper account (`alpaca_paper` / `alpaca_options_paper`, shared key pair) is fine — this is isolated to the LIVE key pair (`ALPACA_API_KEY_ID_LIVE` / `ALPACA_API_SECRET_KEY_LIVE`). Likely causes (operator-side, NOT a code bug): the live key pair is wrong/expired, lacks trading authorization, or the Alpaca live account isn't funded/approved for the requested scope. This is the one genuine hand-off from the options pilot — Claude cannot rotate broker keys. IMPACT: alpaca_live cannot trade (equity OR options) until the key authorizes; it does NOT affect the paper options pilot. ACTION FOR /system-review: remind the operator to (a) verify the live Alpaca key pair + trading permissions in the Alpaca dashboard, (b) re-paste correct values into the ALPACA_API_KEY_ID_LIVE / ALPACA_API_SECRET_KEY_LIVE Actions secrets and run sync-vm-secrets, (c) re-run test-alpaca-creds to confirm api_ok flips true. Until then, treat any alpaca_live go-live proposal as blocked.
items.trigger_condition: NEXT /system-review or /health-review: CONFIRM alpaca_live actually PLACES + FILLS a real ETF order during US RTH (the 2026-07-01 sync+restart restored live auth but the market was closed, so no fill was observed yet). Also any session proposing an alpaca_live go-live.
items.resolution_criteria: A review confirms alpaca_live PLACED and FILLED at least one real-money ETF order during US RTH after 2026-07-01T06:03Z (order_packages/trades shows a filled alpaca_live row, not 'unauthorized'/'risk_refused'), OR /api/bot/accounts/balances shows alpaca_live api_ok:true from a post-fix hourly snapshot. Until a real fill is seen, keep OPEN — auth is verified but end-to-end execution is not.

updates.note: ROOT-CAUSED + FIXED (pending fill confirmation). NOT a key/routing/risk problem: the LIVE keys PASS the live-endpoint creds test (test-alpaca-creds → api.alpaca.markets accepted), accounts.yaml has alpaca_env:live (correct routing), and risk sizing is correct (SLV sized 2 shares; QQQ/SPY sized 0 correctly at $150 balance — can't afford a ~$550 share). The 'unauthorized' order rejections were VM-side STALE CACHED CREDS in ict-trader-live / ict-web-api (os.environ cached from process start, never re-read — same class as the Bybit-IP incident). Fixed this session: sync-vm-secrets (exit 0) + restart-bot-service + vm-web-api-recover. VERIFIED live auth restored: /api/diag/exchange_positions?account_id=alpaca_live now returns positions:[] error:null (was null/unauthorized before). NOTE the misleading signal that mis-led prior reviews: /api/bot/accounts/balances is a CONNECTION-FREE DB snapshot, so 'balance shows' never proved live auth.

check_log.note: NEW EVIDENCE 2026-07-02 /system-review: found a real alpaca_live SLV order (trade journal id 3090) that hit EXCHANGE_REJECTED 'Alpaca rejected order for SLV: unauthorized' at 2026-07-01T14:26:19Z — during US RTH, the exact window the trigger_condition asked to watch. This is NOT yet the requested positive confirmation (a filled order); it is a contradicting negative data point on the SAME day the prior report claimed the sync-vm-secrets fix was verified. However /api/diag/broker_account_status?account_id=alpaca_live (pulled fresh this session, 2026-07-02T06:14:32Z) shows status=ACTIVE, trading_blocked=false, account_blocked=false, trade_suspended_by_user=false — the account is NOT currently blocked. Net read: the fix may be real but flaky/intermittent (stale-cred edge case), or was mid-rollout at 14:26. Every subsequent alpaca_live signal this window (QQQ/GLD, ids 3081-3110) was risk_refused (sized_qty=0 on a ~50 balance) before reaching the exchange, so no further live order-placement attempts have tested authorization since. STILL OPEN — resolution criteria (a confirmed FILL) not yet met.

items.resolution: VERIFIED RESOLVED by /health-review 2026-07-03: /api/diag/broker_account_status shows alpaca_live status=ACTIVE, trading_blocked=false, account_blocked=false; /api/diag/exchange_positions reads the account fine and it holds a real open position (IEF long x1). Reads AND trading authorize. Note for future routing: shorting_enabled=false + crypto_status=INACTIVE on alpaca_live — short signals routed there will be refused broker-side.

items.source: alpaca_options_paper was activated on the SHARED alpaca_paper login (option A, no dedicated keys). On activation the reverse reconciler adopted the shared login's 6 EQUITY positions as phantom orphans (trades 2999-3006) — root-caused to a STRIPPED account cfg dropping the `options` block; hotfix #4858 (canonical-config fallback in account_expresses_options) + asset-class isolation (_alpaca_pos_in_scope) resolved it. The operator chose to stay on the shared login FOR NOW and asked for a standing note to revisit a dedicated account.
items.title: OPERATOR DECISION TO REVISIT: give alpaca_options_paper its OWN Alpaca paper login (separate keys) instead of sharing alpaca_paper's
items.description: SURFACE THIS TO THE OPERATOR at the next /system-review. The options-expression account `alpaca_options_paper` currently runs on the SAME Alpaca paper API keys as the equity account `alpaca_paper` (ALPACA_API_KEY_ID / ALPACA_API_SECRET_KEY). Correctness now rests on TWO asset-class filters keeping the two bot-accounts' positions from colliding: (1) account_open_positions' `_alpaca_pos_in_scope` (us_option for the options account, non-us_option for the equity account) and (2) account_expresses_options resolving the `options` block from canonical accounts.yaml even when handed a stripped cfg (#4858). This is FRAGILE BY DESIGN — the 2026-06-27 orphan-adoption incident is exactly what happens when one of those filters sees an incomplete cfg. A dedicated second Alpaca paper login (its own key pair at e.g. ALPACA_API_KEY_ID_OPTIONS / ALPACA_API_SECRET_KEY_OPTIONS, wired via accounts.yaml api_key_env/api_secret_env + sync-vm-secrets.yml) would make the isolation STRUCTURAL (two brokers never see each other's positions) instead of filter-dependent, removing the shared-login failure mode entirely. TRADE-OFF the operator weighed: a 2nd account is more setup (new Alpaca paper account + keys + secret propagation) vs. the shared login which 'just works' but needs the two guards to stay correct forever. RECOMMENDATION (mine, for the operator to decide): move to a dedicated paper login before this pattern is reused on alpaca_live (real money) — a shared real-money login with this same filter-dependent isolation is a materially worse place to discover a stripped-cfg regression. ACTION FOR /system-review: remind the operator of this decision, summarize the trade-off, and (if they approve) scope the dedicated-account wiring as a Tier-3 accounts.yaml change.
items.trigger_condition: Next /system-review (raise to operator); or before alpaca_live options go-live; or any session touching the alpaca shared-login asset-class isolation.
items.resolution_criteria: Either (a) the operator decides to wire a dedicated Alpaca paper login for alpaca_options_paper and that lands (own key pair, accounts.yaml api_key_env, secret propagation, verified isolation), OR (b) the operator explicitly decides the shared-login + dual-filter design is acceptable to keep and that decision is recorded here — at which point close.

items.source: RG4 fleet scorecard (MB-20260626-001) left most live MES shadow rows unlabeled (mes-5m 2777/2981, mes-15m 1025/1102) while BTC was fully labeled. Root-caused via trainer-vm-diag #4710/#4712/#4716.
items.title: Trainer MES market_raw candle base is frozen at 2026-06-12 — daily build re-emits stale data (GIGO), blinding RG4 + any MES-candle-dependent analysis on the last ~2 weeks
items.description: The MES market_raw dataset content ends 2026-06-12 (5m last_ts 2026-06-12T20:55:00Z, 17113 rows; 15m 2026-06-12T20:45:00Z) even though the daily dataset build runs and reports OK (dataset_builds.jsonl: market_raw MES 'ok' at 2026-06-26T00:24:27; v002 mtime 2026-06-26 00:24). build_mes_market() in scripts/ops/build_trainer_datasets.sh PREFERS a 'synced IBKR MES market_raw (deep history)' base when present (yfinance ES=F, capped ~60d intraday, is only the fallback) — and that IBKR base stopped advancing ~2026-06-12, so each rebuild faithfully re-emits stale candles. NOT a build failure (the build is healthy) and NOT a timestamp/format issue (ISO strings parse fine) — it is a stale INPUT: the IBKR->trainer MES market-data sync that feeds the preferred base froze. IMPACT: RG4 (replay_pregate_live.py) can't label live MES predictions after 2026-06-12 (no candle bar to join), so the entire MES regime fleet's live train/serve-skew verdict rides a thin labeled sample — incl. the two anti-predictive MES 15m heads (MB-20260626-002), whose verdict should be re-confirmed once the base is fresh. Also degrades any MES backtest/feature work that reads this base. FIX DIRECTION (Tier-1 trainer tooling): investigate why the IBKR MES base sync stopped at 2026-06-12 (IB gateway MES historical pull / publish_trainer_mirror MES leg) and refresh it; confirm market_raw MES last_ts advances past 2026-06-12 on the next cycle. Cross-ref MB-20260626-001 (finding 1) + MB-20260626-002.
items.trigger_condition: Next /health-review or /system-review; or any session touching the MES dataset build / IBKR MES sync, or re-scoring the MES regime fleet through RG4.
items.resolution_criteria: datasets-out/market_raw/MES/{5m,15m}/<latest>/data.jsonl last_ts advances past 2026-06-12 (tracks recent calendar dates) on a fresh build, AND a re-run of the RG4 fleet scorecard labels a materially larger fraction of live MES rows (unlab fraction drops from ~93% toward the BTC-like ~0%).
evidence_log: 2026-06-26 (FIX APPLIED + VERIFIED, trainer-vm-diag #4722/#4724/#4729): retired the stale IBKR-synced MES base (moved data/ibkr_datasets/market_raw/MES aside — frozen at last candle 2026-06-12, mtime 2026-06-14) so build_mes_market() falls to its yfinance ES=F fallback (verified fresh: yfinance ES=F 5m returns to 2026-06-26). Rebuilt MES market_raw 5m from yfinance (ICT_OFFVM_BUILD_HOST=1; the guardrail the daily build already sets — proven by BTC's bybit_offvm builds succeeding daily) + resampled 15m: MES/5m now 10918 rows last 2026-06-26T03:55Z, MES/15m 3654 rows last 2026-06-26T03:45Z (was 2026-06-12). Durable: with the IBKR base gone, the daily cycle now rebuilds MES from yfinance each run. RE-RAN RG4 on the MES fleet with the fresh candles: unlab dropped from ~93% to <10% (mes-5m-lgbm-v2 unlab 2777->189; mes-15m-lgbm-v2 1025->91). The labeling gap is closed. SIDE EFFECT (material): the fresh-data re-score REVERSED two false-negative anti-predictive verdicts (the MES 15m heads, now TRUSTWORTHY 0.59/0.64) and surfaced one true negative (mes-regime-5m-lgbm-yz-v1 now ANTI_PREDICTIVE 0.17) — see MB-20260626-002. FOLLOW-UP (separate, lower priority): the deep-history IBKR MES sync (live-side pull_mes_ibkr_history -> sync_trainer_data) is still stalled; yfinance gives a rolling ~60d which suffices for RG4 + intraday regime training, but restoring the IBKR sync would return deep intraday history if a future model wants it.

2026-06-30 (REOPENED — the 2026-06-26 'resolved' did NOT hold). Live evidence (trainer-vm-diag #5170 + live diag #5171): MES market_raw 5m/15m v002 rebuilt today (mtime 06-30 01:15) but AGAIN ends 2026-06-12 (source=ibkr_offvm); MES 1d is fresh to 06-29 (yfinance). RECURRENCE ROOT CAUSE: the 06-26 fix manually moved the stale IBKR base aside, but sync_trainer_data.sh rsyncs the IBKR MES shards from the live VM EVERY daily cycle (db_pulls.jsonl: ibkr_market_raw_mes 'ok' 06-25..06-30), so the next sync RE-PULLED the still-frozen 06-12 snapshot and build_mes_market resumed PREFERRING it — a manual move-aside is not durable against the daily sync. Upstream: the live-VM pull_mes_ibkr_history.sh (source of the snapshot) last ran 2026-06-14 and is UNSCHEDULED/manual, so the live snapshot is itself frozen at 06-12 (IB host is already correct at 10.0.0.251; the 06-14 ConnectionRefused was a pre-host-fix first attempt). DURABLE FIX SHIPPED (commit 28ddbf5, PR #5167): build_mes_market now FRESHNESS-GATES the IBKR preference (mes_file_stale + MES_IBKR_MAX_STALE_DAYS=5) — a stale shard is ignored and the build falls to fresh yfinance ES=F regardless of what the daily sync re-pulls, self-healing when the live pull resumes. PENDING merge + next trainer cycle to verify MES 5m/15m last_ts advances past 06-12. OPERATIONAL FOLLOW-UP (restores DEEP history; yfinance is ~60d only): run + schedule pull_mes_ibkr_history.sh on the LIVE VM (Tier-2 — it shares the live gateway under a heartbeat guard).

2026-06-30 (OPERATIONAL FIX shipped, commit 1e5d8ba, PR #5167): root-caused the recurrence — the intraday MES IBKR pull (scripts/ops/pull_mes_ibkr_history.sh / system-action pull-mes-ibkr-history) was only ever a MANUAL one-shot with NO recurring scheduler, so it stopped after its last manual run 2026-06-14. Added the missing scheduler deploy/ict-mes-ibkr-pull.{service,timer} (daily 23:30 UTC, before the trainer ~00:50 sync; live-trader-box-only via the install_systemd_units.sh auto-enable; all the script's live-gateway guards intact). Activates on merge+git-sync deploy. ALSO triggered the existing one-shot pull-mes-ibkr-history (system-action #5185, operator-approved) to refresh the deep base immediately. Combined with the build-side freshness gate (commit on this PR), MES is now durably protected: the build self-heals to fresh yfinance if the IBKR base ever goes stale again, AND the timer keeps the IBKR deep base current. Verify after merge+deploy: (1) market_raw/MES 5m/15m last_ts advances past 06-12; (2) ict-mes-ibkr-pull.timer enabled+active on the live VM; (3) re-run RG4 on the MES fleet. Once verified -> resolved.
items.status_history: open 2026-06-26 (root-caused: MES candle base frozen 2026-06-12; daily build healthy but fed a stale IBKR-synced base) -> resolved 2026-06-26 (retired stale IBKR base + rebuilt from fresh yfinance; MES candles now to 2026-06-26; RG4 labeling gap closed, verified). -> REOPENED 2026-06-30 (the 06-26 manual move-aside was reverted by the daily IBKR sync; durable freshness-gate fix shipped commit 28ddbf5 PR #5167, pending merge+deploy).

items.opened_by: claude (prop-trade-pipeline-debug — eth_pullback prop go-live wiring check)
items.source: PR #4533 set eth_pullback_prop_2h execution: live and routed it + eth_pullback_2h to breakout_1. Diag /api/bot/strategies confirmed BOTH in loaded_strategies + bot_running 2026-06-25 — but loading is not the same as the prop emit path producing a ticket.
items.title: RESOLVED: ETH prop legs verified live end-to-end — eth_pullback_2h + eth_pullback_prop_2h aggregate into ONE ticket per signal; donchian *_prop legs are execution: shadow BY DESIGN
items.description: WIRING LIVENESS CHECK. On the first ETHUSDT signal on breakout_1 for either leg, confirm end-to-end: (a) a prop_signal ticket is emitted (Telegram prop bot + FCM) carrying the Yes/No place-decision buttons; (b) a prop_tickets row is written with order_package_id POPULATED (the sizing+journaling fixes from PR #4460/#4469 — risk_usd should size off 1.5% = ~$75, not the old $25); (c) /api/bot/prop/reconcile sees it. Both legs share (ETHUSDT, breakout_1), so also confirm they emit as SEPARATE tickets (per-strategy netting guard) and do not collide on the same position. Until one fires we have only confirmed they load, not that the prop ticket path works for them.

updates.note: PARTIALLY CONFIRMED + NEW BUG EVIDENCE. eth_pullback_2h DOES emit+journal prop tickets on breakout_1 (prop-manual-90982c696cb1 04:05Z, -72323e63411b 06:35Z, -5bc393741ec4 08:00Z on 2026-07-05; risk_usd=75 = the 1.5% sizing fix; operator filled the 08:00Z ticket, prop_fills id 16 linked, ticket advanced to filled). items.source: PR #4441 (merged to main as squash 5830136c, deployed via ict-git-sync) fixed the perpetual MHG/ib_paper adopt→sl_cross→re-orphan flap. Root cause: IBClient.close returned retCode 0 the moment the opposing market order was ACCEPTED (or still pending) — it never confirmed the position reached flat — so an accepted-but-unfilled close marked the DB row closed (sl_cross) while the real IB position stayed open and (because close() Step 1 cancels the protective bracket) naked, then got re-orphaned. Fix: (1) IBClient.close now re-reads the live position and requires flat within IB_CLOSE_CONFIRM_S (default 6s, <=0 disables) else returns retCode 1 so the monitor leaves the DB row open + retries; (2) order_monitor._apply_update arms a per-(account,symbol,direction) close-retry cooldown (IB_CLOSE_RETRY_COOLDOWN_S, default 300s, 0 disables) on a 'not confirmed flat' close so it doesn't churn the re-armed bracket every tick. Post-deploy verification (2026-06-24 ~13:00) was POSITIVE: trader restarted (new PID), trade 2832 closed exchange_flat_reconciled, NO new MHG adopted_orphan rows for ~80 min, no sl_cross/adopt/orphan churn lines.
items.title: VERIFY the IB close-confirm + close-retry-cooldown fix (PR #4441) continues to prevent the IB adopt→sl_cross→re-orphan flap (MHG/MGC/MES on ib_paper, and any IB account)
items.description: ACTION FOR CLAUDE at the next /health-review or /system-review (and any session touching the IB order/close path): re-confirm the fix is still holding. Check the live trader journal + trades table for the flap signature returning — specifically (a) repeated 'order_monitor: exchange close for pkg=… → {ok:True}' immediately followed by the SAME (account,symbol,direction) reappearing as an exchange orphan; (b) a string of short-lived adopted_orphan rows (setup_type='adopted_orphan', entry_reason like 'reverse_reconciler_reattached%') for one IB symbol accruing every few minutes; (c) 'RE-ADOPT SUPPRESSED' / detect_only re-adopt-suppressed alerts recurring. Healthy state: an IB sl_cross close either CONFIRMS flat (DB closes once, exchange_flat_reconciled or a real exit) or, when the venue can't fill (market closed / gateway mid-reset), the row stays OPEN with the bracket armed and a 'close retry cooling down' log — NOT a new orphan per cycle. Also sanity-check the two knobs are still at sane values on the VM (IB_CLOSE_CONFIRM_S>0, IB_CLOSE_RETRY_COOLDOWN_S>0; a stray 0 in .env disables the guard — same failure-mode class as the dropped POSITION_NETTING_GUARD env on the Ampere migration). If the flap has returned, re-open the close-path investigation; the fix locus is src/units/accounts/ib_client.py::close + src/runtime/order_monitor.py::_apply_update.

items.source: bybit_2 missing-close investigation (2026-06-20/21). The automated recovery path (scripts/ops/backfill_orphan_pnl.py + the new in-line watchdog recovery in PR #4058) can only reconstruct a real exit/PnL from Bybit's /v5/position/closed-pnl, which Bybit retains for ONLY ~7 days. Trade #2708 was recovered in-window (status='closed', pnl=+0.8252). But the 2026-06-21 backfill run found 15 OLD orphans (trade ids 990-1000, 1046-1048, 1454) that ALL returned account_closed_pnl=None — i.e. they are past Bybit's 7-day window and are PERMANENTLY unrecoverable via the exchange API. The only way to enter their true exit/PnL (and any other historically-missing closes) into trade_journal.db is a FULL manual account-history export from the exchange UI, which the operator must pull by hand. Operator asked (2026-06-21) to log this so the next session proactively requests that export.
items.title: ASK OPERATOR: manually export FULL exchange account history (bybit_2 + all real-money accounts) so pre-7-day-window orphaned/missing trades can be entered into trade_journal.db
items.description: ACTION FOR CLAUDE at the next session that touches trade reconciliation / account history (or the next /health-review that surfaces this item): proactively ASK the operator to manually export the COMPLETE trade history for each real-money exchange account (Bybit bybit_2 first; extend to every real-money account) from the exchange's own UI/CSV/API export — because the bot's automated orphan-PnL backfill only reaches Bybit's ~7-day closed-pnl retention window and CANNOT recover older closes. Once the operator provides the export, ingest the historical closed trades into trade_journal.db (set status='closed' + authoritative exit_price + realised pnl + closed_at, with a provenance note e.g. exit_price_source='operator_manual_export') so /api/bot/trades/closed, /api/bot/performance, and the dashboard reflect TRUE lifetime history rather than the partial picture left by the 7-day window. This is the HISTORICAL-gap remediation; the code fixes #4066 (visibility) + #4057 (clean refusal) + #4058 (watchdog recover-before-orphan) only stop NEW orphans from being created going forward and do not retroactively fill rows already past the window. Known unrecoverable-via-API rows as of 2026-06-21: trade ids 990-1000, 1046-1048, 1454.
items.tier: The ASK itself is Tier-1 (operator request). The subsequent ingest of operator-supplied history into trade_journal.db is a Tier-2 data writeback to the live money DB — get an explicit operator OK on the exact import before running it, validate row counts/PnL before/after, and never overwrite an existing well-formed closed row.

items.source: Observed in the live-VM deploy log during the 2026-06-16 #3844 pull-and-deploy (and again in passing this session): a candle/market fetch raised 'bybit does not have market symbol MHGN6'. MHGN6 is the IBKR contract-month-qualified symbol for MHG (micro copper, June-2026 expiry). The fetch was routed to Bybit, which has no such market.
items.title: Candle/market fetch for IBKR contract-month symbols (e.g. MHGN6) misroutes to Bybit — connector_for_symbol profile lookup is keyed by root, not the contract-qualified symbol
items.description: src/runtime/market_data.py::connector_for_symbol looks up the instrument profile by EXACT symbol (load_instrument_profiles().get(symbol)). config/instruments.yaml keys metals/index futures by ROOT (MHG/MES/MGC), so a contract-month-qualified symbol like 'MHGN6' misses the profile, exchange stays None, and it falls back to the process default EXCHANGE (bybit) → 'bybit does not have market symbol MHGN6'. The qualified symbol most likely enters the fetch path via an adopted-orphan IB position / order-monitor mark-price lookup (the reverse_reconciler adopts IB positions under their contract-qualified symbol, cf. trade #2540's MHGN6 in BL-20260612-001). Best-effort path (fetch_candles logs + returns None), so no crash — but it means no candle/mark data for that position and a noisy error per attempt. FIX (Tier-1/2): in connector_for_symbol, when the exact-symbol profile lookup misses, fall back to a futures-root normalization (strip the contract-month suffix, e.g. MHGN6→MHG) before defaulting to the process EXCHANGE — mirror the base-futures-root resolution already used in order_monitor._resolve_protective_levels / positions symbol-or-base-futures-root matching. Verify MHGN6/MESM6/etc. route to IBKR.
items.trigger_condition: Next /health-review, or when touching market_data symbol routing / instrument profiles.
items.resolution_criteria: connector_for_symbol routes a contract-month-qualified futures symbol (MHGN6, MESM6, MGCQ6, …) to its root's exchange (IBKR), not the bybit default; no 'bybit does not have market symbol <root><month>' errors in the live log.

check_log.note: Still recurring: 'Error fetching OHLCV: bybit does not have market symbol MHGN6' seen in the ict-trader-live post-restart journal (2026-06-22 12:09, run 27951560947) during the Telegram-token .env restart. Confirms the contract-month→Bybit misroute is unresolved; root-cause/fix in this item's description still stands.

items.resolution: FIXED 2026-07-04: connector_for_symbol now falls back to the base futures root when the exact symbol has no instrument profile (MHGN6 -> MHG -> IBKR), using the same month-code grammar as order_monitor._base_futures_symbol; crypto/equity symbols are unaffected (regex-verified). Unit tests added (TestConnectorRouting: MHGN6 routes to IBMarketData; SOLUSDT stays Bybit).

items.source: Operator flagged that the session walked past noncompliant default-off gates. Investigation: POSITION_NETTING_GUARD_ENABLED (correctness fix) + CLOSED_FLAT_INVARIANT_ENABLED (safety invariant) were both default-OFF *_ENABLED flags in front of required capabilities — the Prime-Directive anti-pattern (same class as the removed NAKED_POSITION_AUTOPROTECT / MONITOR_RECONCILE_ENABLED). PROOF of harm: the netting guard ran live on both Bybit accounts from 2026-06-08, but the 2026-06-14 Ampere migration dropped its .env vars and the fix silently regressed — paper netting artifacts (side/qty NULL, reconciler_filled, duplicated -767.22 pnl on netted legs) reappeared on bybit_1 ~2026-06-15 (#2618/2621/2623+), and real-money bybit_2 was exposed via the same gate. A capability behind a config-only flag vanishes on a migration/config-reset; one baked into code cannot.
items.title: Audit ALL env gates for migration/config-reset survival — config-only capabilities silently regress on VM migration (root cause of the 2026-06-15 netting regression)
items.description: RESOLVED for the two found gates in the same session: PR (claude/baseline-correctness-gates) makes the netting guard + closed-flat invariant BASELINE (removed POSITION_NETTING_GUARD_ENABLED / POSITION_NETTING_GUARD_ACCOUNTS / CLOSED_FLAT_INVARIANT_ENABLED; behavior now unconditional in code). REMAINING systemic follow-up: (1) audit the other default-off *_ENABLED-style flags that gate real behavior (REGIME_ROUTER_ENABLED is default-off but fail-permissive = rollout of an optional restriction, defensible; re-confirm) and baseline any that gate a REQUIRED capability; (2) the live-VM .env is NOT reproducible from source — the 6/14 migration dropping the guard vars proves operator-set env can silently differ from intent. Consider a checked-in canonical .env manifest + a deploy-time diff/alert when the live .env is missing an expected key, OR move all non-secret behavioral flags out of .env into versioned config; (3) verify on the live VM that no OTHER soaked-then-baked fix regressed at the 6/14 migration the same way.
items.trigger_condition: Next /health-review, or when touching VM env / migration tooling.
items.resolution_criteria: No required capability is gated by a default-off env flag; live-VM .env drift from intent is detectable (manifest/diff) so a migration can't silently drop a behavioral flag again.

items.source: /health-review 2026-06-12 snapshot (#3438): trade #2539 (mhg_pullback_1d, ib_paper, 00:02:57Z) sized 3 WHOLE contracts (BL-20260611-001 integer-sizing fix VERIFIED) and reached IBKR, but IBKR rejected the parent: 'Error 10349, reqId 10: Order TIF was set to DAY based on order preset' (status=Cancelled) — journaled cleanly as exchange_rejected (post-place rejection check VERIFIED). Then at 04:41:22Z the reverse_reconciler adopted an open MHGN6 IB position (3 contracts @ 15989.72, NO SL/TP — naked_sltp_alerted) as trade #2540 (setup_type=adopted_orphan, status=open).
items.title: RESOLVED: IB Error 10349 (TIF forced to DAY) — TIF now set explicitly on every IB order leg
items.description: Two linked findings on the ib_paper live-execution path (paper money — IBKR paper account — so no real capital at risk, but a genuine live-path defect that strands the IB futures strategies). (1) IB ORDER TIF: the bot's IB order construction (src/units/accounts/ib_client.py) submits with a TIF that IBKR's account/order preset overrides to DAY, and IBKR then rejects/cancels the parent (Error 10349). Net effect: MES/MGC/MHG now size correctly (integer contracts, BL-20260611-001 fixed) but can NEVER fill — every IB futures order will draw 10349 until the TIF is set explicitly (likely GTC) or the IBKR account's order preset is changed. Tier-2/3 (live IB order path). (2) NAKED ORPHAN: trade #2540 — the reverse_reconciler found a real open MHGN6 position at IBKR (3 contracts @ 15989.72) with no matching trade row and adopted it; it has no SL/TP attached (naked_sltp_alerted_at set). Needs SL/TP attachment or a flatten — the IB order path produced/left a position without protective orders. Investigate whether the 10349-cancelled parent nonetheless left a child/leg filled, or whether this is a stale pre-existing paper position. SUGGESTED: (a) set the IB order TIF explicitly + verify a paper MES/MGC/MHG order fills end-to-end; (b) reconcile/flatten #2540 or attach SL/TP; (c) confirm no recurrence of naked adopted IB orphans.
items.trigger_condition: Every /health-review until an IB futures order fills end-to-end (no 10349) AND no naked adopted-orphan IB position remains.
items.resolution_criteria: A paper MES/MGC/MHG order reaches IBKR and FILLS with SL/TP attached (no Error 10349), and trade #2540 is closed/flattened or carries SL/TP. The IB futures path produces protected positions, not naked orphans.

check_log.note: TIF FIX SHIPPED + DEPLOYED LIVE (operator-approved). PR #3441 merged to main (ae7fae9); pull-and-deploy #3444 restarted ict-trader-live clean (f2f6cb9->ae7fae9, service active, pipeline ticking). Fix: explicit TIF on the IB bracket (parent DAY, SL/TP GTC) in src/units/accounts/ib_client.py — Error 10349 'TIF set by preset' can no longer fire, so the spurious-cancel-that-actually-fills desync that produced #2539(rejected)+#2540(naked filled) is closed for all FUTURE IB orders. Verifies on the next MES/MGC/MHG signal (1d cadence). TWO ITEMS REMAIN: (1) observe the next IB fill is clean; (2) attach SL/TP to the EXISTING naked orphan #2540 — operator chose 'Attach SL/TP'. (2) needs net-new live-order code (IBClient.place_protective: reverse-side STP+LMT, GTC, OCA, no market parent — current place() always opens a position) + a monitor hook in _check_naked_positions (currently alert-only) + kill-switch + tests. Scoped as a focused follow-up PR for operator review before it places live orders; #2540 is paper money (ib_paper) so it carries no real-capital risk while unprotected. SL/TP for #2540 derive from pkg-f58a249d119049ab / trade #2539: SL 6.045, TP 7.029 (price terms; IBKR avgCost 15989.72 = 6.396 x 2500 multiplier).

items.resolution: Verified fixed in current code, no update was ever logged: src/units/accounts/ib_client.py::place() sets parent.tif = 'DAY' and every protective leg tif = 'GTC' EXPLICITLY, with a comment tracing the fix directly to this incident: 'With an unset TIF, IBKR applies the account's GUI order-preset default (DAY) and emits a spurious Error 10349 ... An explicit TIF is no longer "based on order preset", so 10349 never fires.' Backlog item had sat open with zero check_log entries despite the fix landing — closing on code evidence.

items.title: OANDA practice account cannot trade XAU_USD — gold orders rejected account-side ('instrument not tradeable by the Account'); OPERATOR DECISION needed
items.description: NOT a code bug: to_instrument maps XAUUSD->XAU_USD correctly, auth + sizing + the order request all work (the request reached OANDA's create endpoint and was refused by the ACCOUNT's permissions). Most likely cause: the practice account is under OANDA's US division, where CFTC/NFA rules prohibit retail gold/silver CFDs (XAU_USD/XAG_USD) — pricing/candles stream fine (which is why the M15 data-path smoke passed) but order placement is blocked. The M15 gold-first plan is blocked on this account. OPERATOR options: (a) confirm the account division and, if US, create a practice account under a non-US division and swap OANDA_ACCOUNT_ID/token via init-actions-secrets + sync-vm-secrets; (b) pick a permitted instrument for the sleeve (GET /v3/accounts/{id}/instruments lists what IS tradeable — could be exposed via a future diag endpoint); (c) route gold elsewhere. Until decided, xauusd_trend_1h signals (currently firing ~hourly at confidence 1.0) will keep journaling exchange_rejected rows — bounded noise, no risk.

items.source: Trade monitor first check (#3415): trades #2532 (15:30Z) + #2533 (16:04Z), eth_pullback_2h short on bybit_1 demo, BOTH exchange_rejected by Bybit 'retCode 10001 Qty invalid' — qty 12.771 / 14.937 ETH on ETHUSDT linear whose lotSizeFilter.qtyStep is 0.01.
items.title: Bybit qty not aligned to per-symbol lotSizeFilter.qtyStep — eth_pullback_2h orders 100% rejected (same bug class as BL-20260611-001, crypto flavour)
items.description: The sizer's account-level qty_precision (3dp, tuned for BTCUSDT's 0.001 step) is not symbol-aware; ETHUSDT linear trades in 0.01 steps, so every eth_pullback_2h order drew retCode 10001 and the new strategy could not execute at all (clean journaled rejections — no silent orphans, unlike the IB flavour). FIX SHIPPED same session: precision.get_lot_rule (cache→live instruments-info→static→None, mirroring get_tick_size) + quantize_qty (floor, never up) + pre-flight alignment in _submit_order's bybit branch (refuse below minOrderQty; write aligned qty back so the journal records what was sent; smoke-test path deliberately unaligned). Unknown rule → submit unmodified (no guessed steps).

items.resolution_criteria: A Bybit order for a non-0.001-step symbol (ETHUSDT) submits with step-aligned qty and is ACCEPTED by the exchange (journal row open/closed, not exchange_rejected), with journaled position_size matching the submitted qty.

items.source: /health-review 2026-06-11 — trade #2531 (mhg_pullback_1d, ib_paper) journaled position_size=3.643 contracts; package gate-blocked 30 min then orphaned by the stuck-strategy watchdog with the exchange reading flat.
items.title: RESOLVED: IB futures fractional-contract sizing — whole-contract floor now enforced in IBClient.place/place_protective/close
items.description: config/accounts.yaml::ib_paper.risk has no qty_precision, so RiskManager falls back to _DEFAULT_QTY_PRECISION=3 (crypto-oriented) and sizes MES/MGC/MHG orders to e.g. 3.643 contracts; src/units/accounts/ib_client.py:549 then passes that float unfloored into ib_insync MarketOrder (and the TP/SL child orders), which IBKR cannot fill for futures — integer contracts only. The contract_value_usd_for multiplier fixes the USD-loss math but NOT the integer constraint (the risk.py comment 'qty comes out in whole contracts' is wrong as implemented). Likely explains trade #2531's no-position-materialized orphan today and possibly earlier stuck/no-fill behaviour on the IB path. FIX (Tier-2/3, order path + Tier-3 accounts.yaml): floor IB futures qty to int in the sizing path (qty_precision: 0 for ib accounts in accounts.yaml) AND defensively floor in ib_client.place_order (reject qty<1 after floor with a journaled rejection); also correct the risk.py comment. Verify with a paper fill end-to-end.
items.trigger_condition: Next working session on the bot; every /health-review until an IB paper order fills end-to-end with integer qty.
items.resolution_criteria: IB-bound futures orders journal integer position_size; a paper MES/MGC/MHG order reaches IBKR and fills (or is journaled-rejected for size<1); no recurrence of the dispatched-but-never-positioned orphan pattern on ib_paper.

check_log.note: FIX SHIPPED + DEPLOYED. PR #3406 (merged 792c818, CI 11/11 green, deployed via pull-and-deploy #3411, trader restarted clean 16:20Z): (1) position_size enforces whole-contract sizing for market_type=futures (sub-1-contract = refusal, never bumped); (2) IBClient.place floors defensively + refuses <1 contract; (3) NEW bounded post-place rejection check (IB_PLACE_CONFIRM_S, default 3s) surfaces async IBKR rejections as journaled failures — closes the fire-and-forget gap that made #2531 silent. OANDA/Alpaca verified already floored (no equivalent gap). REMAINING for resolution: observe the next IB-bound signal journal an INTEGER position_size and either fill end-to-end or journal a clean rejection — check every /health-review.

BOTH FIXES VERIFIED LIVE. Snapshot #3438: trade #2539 (mhg_pullback_1d, ib_paper, 00:02:57Z) journaled position_size=3 — INTEGER whole contracts (no more 3.643 fractional). AND it reached IBKR and the async rejection was surfaced as a clean exchange_rejected row (status=exchange_rejected, not a silent 30-min orphan like #2531) — the post-place rejection check (IB_PLACE_CONFIRM_S) works. The fractional-sizing + silent-orphan defect this item tracked is CLOSED. The IB path still doesn't FILL — but for a NEW reason (IBKR Error 10349, TIF forced to DAY by order preset), spun out to BL-20260612-001 along with the resulting naked adopted-orphan #2540. Keeping this open only until an actual end-to-end fill is observed; the sizing/journaling half is done.

items.resolution: Verified fixed in current code, no update was ever logged: src/units/accounts/ib_client.py::place() floors qty via math.floor(qty) and refuses whole_qty<1 ('qty {qty} is below 1 whole contract after flooring'), with an explicit comment tracing the fix to this exact incident ('BL-20260611-001: a 3.643-contract MHG order was transmitted, rejected asynchronously by IBKR, and the journal row was orphaned 30 min later'). The same whole-unit floor is applied in place_protective() and close(). Backlog item had sat open with zero check_log entries despite the fix landing — closing on code evidence.

items.source: /health-review 2026-06-11 — 14 diag-request issues filed in ~1 min; 9 runs ended 'cancelled' (runs 27357165619…27357204802) with no comment on the cancelled issues.
items.title: vm-diag-snapshot burst requests silently cancelled despite cancel-in-progress:false — GitHub keeps only ONE pending run per concurrency group
items.description: The 2026-05-16 comment in .github/workflows/vm-diag-snapshot.yml ('requests queue and each completes in turn — no silent cancellations') is wrong for bursts: GitHub Actions allows at most one PENDING run per concurrency group regardless of cancel-in-progress, so each newly-queued diag-request cancels the previously queued one; only the running + newest-pending survive. The cancelled-run commenter never fires for queued-cancelled runs (same gap the 2026-05-16 note described), so the requesting session sees an open issue with no reply. diag-data SKILL.md pitfall text fixed this session (space requests ~90s / confirm previous result before filing the next). REMAINING (Tier-1 CI): fix the stale workflow comment; optionally drop the concurrency group (runs are read-only and cheap) or key the group on the issue number so concurrent requests don't share one lane.
items.trigger_condition: Next CI/tooling session or /health-review.
items.resolution_criteria: Filing N diag-request issues back-to-back yields N result comments (verified with a 3-burst), or the workflow comment + diag-relay doc accurately describe the one-pending-slot constraint and all skills instruct spacing.

check_log.note: system-report health-review 2026-06-23: reproduced — during the concurrent system-report pull burst, several vm-diag-snapshot runs were cancelled (audit/services) while serial pulls (config/journal/snapshot) succeeded; the consolidated /api/diag/snapshot bundle is the effective workaround (one pull covers heartbeat+status+audit). KEEP OPEN.

items.resolution: FIXED 2026-07-04 (operator-picked backlog drain): the relay concurrency groups are now keyed PER ISSUE NUMBER (vm-diag-snapshot.yml + trainer-vm-diag.yml) — GitHub only ever holds one PENDING run per group, so the shared group was structurally incapable of queueing a burst; per-issue groups make bursts run concurrently and un-cancellable by siblings, while the opened+labeled double-fire on one issue still shares its group and is caught by the dedup step. Stale guidance updated in diag-data SKILL.md + docs/claude/diag-relay.md. Recurrence signature: any future diag-request run ending cancelled.

updates.note: RECURRENCE RECONFIRMED 2026-06-29: a burst of ~6 vm-diag-request issues opened near-simultaneously left only snapshot+audit_query+services+order_packages successful; db_info/exchange_positions/shadow_stats/exit_ladder_soak/trades were silently CANCELLED (no commenter fired on the queued-cancelled runs), exactly as described. Workaround used: fire diag-request issues SERIALLY (one, await its comment, then the next). Still open — the durable fix (per-request concurrency group, or a queue, or restoring the cancelled-run commenter) is unshipped.
