items.source: Structural review of the 2026-06-10 cascade (Explore agent). Four gaps found; Fix 1 (resource isolation) ships in claude/trader-resource-isolation. Fixes 2-4 deferred to this follow-up per operator scope decision (Fix 1+2 now means Fix 1 + the container-cap that achieves Fix 2's churn-containment intent; the deeper watchdog state-machine + IB decoupling + autoheal-timeout are the next round).
items.title: Trader-connection-stability follow-ups: IB-path fast-fail decoupling (Fix 3) + watchdog state-machine + autoheal-timeout hardening (Fix 4)
items.description: Fix 3 (IB decoupling): src/exchange/ib_connector.py's IBMarketData.get_ohlcv does NOT consult the IBClient circuit breaker, so a wedged gateway costs ~5s probe + 8s fetch per MES symbol PER TICK on the main loop (heartbeat starvation). Make IBMarketData consult the same breaker (return None immediately when open, no probe/fetch) + cache MES bars on their bar cadence so the 1d strategies rarely hit IB. Fix 4 (autoheal): scripts/check_heartbeat.py::try_autoheal_restart has a 30s subprocess timeout on `systemctl restart` that can time out under saturation; the Fix-1 CPUWeight on the watchdog mitigates this, but also bump the timeout (30->45s) and/or use `systemctl restart --no-block` so a slow-but-completing restart isn't abandoned. Watchdog state machine (the deeper half of Fix 2): scripts/check_ib_gateway.py treats 'gateway down' (restart helps) and 'gateway up but can't authenticate' (restart just churns) identically; with the container now capped the churn is harmless, but a smarter watchdog could distinguish them and back off harder / probe-only on persistent auth-failure (without the --exhaustion-reset-min re-churn) while still auto-recovering when IBKR returns. All Tier-2/3, live-path; PR + verify each.
items.trigger_condition: After Fix 1 (BL-20260610-003) is verified stable. Next dedicated session or /health-review.
items.resolution_criteria: Fix 3 + Fix 4 shipped + verified: a wedged gateway costs ~0 main-loop time per tick (breaker fast-fail), and an induced trader stall under load is autohealed within the nominal window.

items.source: 2026-06-10 live incident: IB-gateway watchdog docker-restart churn (paper Gateway couldn't re-login during IBKR's reset window) saturated the 2-core VM (loadavg ~10) -> trader main loop starved -> heartbeat froze ~25min. Branch claude/trader-resource-isolation ships Fix 1 (systemd CPUWeight/IOWeight/Nice on ict-trader-live + both watchdogs; --cpus/--memory cap on the IB-gateway Docker container).
items.title: After the trader-resource-isolation PR deploys: re-create the IB-gateway container with the cap + re-enable the (now-safe) ib-gateway watchdog; verify the cascade can't recur
items.description: The IB-gateway watchdog (ict-ib-gateway-watchdog.timer) is currently DISABLED (vm-ib-gateway-stop #3224, the incident remediation) and was manually re-provisioned at 09:00 (clean login, paper DUQ325724) — but that container was created BEFORE the cap, so it is UNCAPPED. After this PR merges + deploys (pull-and-deploy installs the updated units via scripts/install_systemd_units.sh + daemon-reload): (1) apply the cap to the running container immediately without a restart via `docker update --cpus=0.75 --memory=1500m ib-gateway` (or re-provision to recreate it capped); (2) re-enable the watchdog timer via the vm-ib-gateway-watchdog-enable workflow; (3) verify systemd picked up the new resource directives: `systemctl show ict-trader-live.service -p CPUWeight,IOWeight,Nice` shows the high values, same for both watchdogs; (4) VERIFY the fix holds across the next IBKR reset window (gateway auto-restarts 11:59 PM per its config) — watch that even if the gateway churns, vm_health.cpu/loadavg stays bounded and the trader heartbeat keeps advancing (no wedge). Close when one reset-window passes with the watchdog active + the trader never wedging.
items.trigger_condition: After claude/trader-resource-isolation merges + deploys. Every /health-review until verified across a reset window.
items.resolution_criteria: Units show the resource directives applied; the IB-gateway container runs with --cpus/--memory caps; the watchdog timer is active; and one IBKR reset window passes with the gateway churning (if it does) but the trader heartbeat never going stale + loadavg staying bounded.

check_log.note: 2026-06-11: RE-SCOPED by the 2026-06-10 gateway-isolation redesign (#3271/#3296): the IB gateway now runs on its own Ampere VM (10.0.0.251) — it cannot starve the trader micro regardless of container caps; the reactive 5-min watchdog is retired (live VM's ict-ib-gateway-watchdog.timer is the daily alert-only flavour, active per #3397). Remaining ask: one explicit verification that the gateway VM's daily reset (05:30 UTC) leaves MES dark <few min and the trader untouched. Today 15:14Z all three IB symbols stream fine post-reset, suggesting it passed; a targeted 05:25-05:45Z window read would close this.

Resolution criteria ('one IBKR reset window passes with the gateway churning and the trader never wedging') still not met -- see BL-20260623-002's 2026-07-07 recurrence logged today. ict-ib-gateway-watchdog.timer correctly shows inactive on the LIVE-TRADER VM services pull (diag #5827) -- per CLAUDE.md this watchdog is gateway-VM-only, so that reading is expected, not a gap. No visibility into the gateway VM's own watchdog state from this session's tooling.

items.source: 2026-06-10 cold-start wedge (BL-20260609-001 recurrence). Fixed in code via REGIME_BAR_SCORING_BUDGET_S (per-tick wall-clock budget, src/runtime/regime_bar_scoring.py) + tests. REGIME_BAR_SCORING_DISABLED=1 was set on the live VM as the incident remediation (#3211).
items.title: Re-enable REGIME_BAR_SCORING on the live VM after the cold-start-budget fix deploys + verify no restart wedge
items.description: The per-bar regime scoring is currently DISABLED on the live VM (REGIME_BAR_SCORING_DISABLED=1, #3211) — so the strong shadow regime heads (1h/MES) are NOT accruing per-bar track record (touches MB-20260529-001 / yz-1h promotion soak, MB-20260527-004). Once this PR (REGIME_BAR_SCORING_BUDGET_S budget) is merged + deployed (pull-and-deploy), re-enable via set-env REGIME_BAR_SCORING_DISABLED=0 + restart, then VERIFY across the restart: (a) cpu does NOT peg at 100% post-restart (watch the first ~5 min — the budget should spread the cold-start over several ticks); (b) heartbeat keeps advancing (never label!=running) through the restart; (c) a `regime_bar: per-tick budget reached ... deferring` info line appears in journalctl on the cold-start tick(s), confirming the budget engaged; (d) shadow_predictions.jsonl resumes writing regime-head rows. If cpu still pegs, lower REGIME_BAR_SCORING_BUDGET_S (e.g. 3) or investigate per-head feature_row cost.
items.trigger_condition: After this PR merges to main + deploys to the live VM. Also every /health-review until regime-bar-scoring is re-enabled and verified stable across a restart.
items.resolution_criteria: REGIME_BAR_SCORING_DISABLED=0 on the live VM AND a trader restart (deploy or manual) completes with cpu never sustained at 100% + heartbeat continuously 'running' + the budget info-line observed + shadow_predictions resuming. Closed when one clean restart-with-scoring-on is observed.
items.resolution: Verified live 2026-06-11: shadow_predictions.jsonl actively writing event_source=per_bar regime-head rows (mes-regime-5m heads at 15:16:30Z), so REGIME_BAR_SCORING is re-enabled. The trader restarted at ~14:40Z today (deploy of e61a1de) WITH scoring on and came up clean: heartbeat label=running (age 31s), last_tick fresh, cpu=0%, uptime advancing, no wedge across the cold-start window — the one-clean-restart-with-scoring-on criterion is met. Caveat: the journalctl 'budget reached … deferring' info-line was not separately fetched (trader-unit journalctl unreliable, BL-20260606-001); resolution rests on the no-wedge + scoring-active live observation.

items.source: /health-review 2026-06-10 — audit_query (#3200) over the 06:18-06:39Z window shows vwap_eval x9 on the live VM, while config/strategies.yaml has vwap enabled:false (M7 kill, committed 34f6e43, deployed in live git_sha=83d77efe) AND status.json's loaded-strategies set excludes vwap.
items.title: Disabled strategy (vwap, M7-killed) still emits *_eval audit events on the live VM — eval/telemetry path does not honor enabled:false
items.description: vwap is enabled:false (does-the-strategy-RUN gate per the strategies.yaml header comment) and is absent from the runtime loaded-strategies list, yet it emits vwap_eval ~9x in a 21-min window. No trading impact — vwap creates no order packages (the order path correctly skips it; its last packages were 06-08, pre-kill). But the eval/audit emission runs in a code path that iterates signal builders independent of the enabled flag, so the audit log misleadingly implies a killed strategy is still 'active', and it wastes per-tick eval cycles. SUGGESTED FIX (Tier-1/2, bot-side): gate *_eval emission (and the per-bar eval work) on the same enabled/loaded check the order path uses, so a disabled strategy goes fully silent. Verify whether other disabled strategies (trend_donchian_1h) also emit eval.
items.trigger_condition: Next /health-review; confirm whether vwap_eval (and trend_donchian_1h_eval) still fire after a config reload.
items.resolution_criteria: A strategy with enabled:false emits ZERO *_eval audit events on the live VM (verified by audit_query returning none for that strategy over an active window), OR the behaviour is confirmed intentional + documented.

check_log.note: 2026-06-11: CONFIRMED persisting after a fresh restart (14:40Z, sha e61a1de). audit_query (#3404) shows vwap_eval firing every tick 15:19-15:26Z incl. ACTIONABLE side=buy rows with entry/SL/TP and confidence 1.0, and the shadow predictors score those vwap signals (#3396). Order path correctly produces no packages. The eval/telemetry path definitively ignores enabled:false. Carry; fix is the gate-eval-emission-on-enabled change proposed in the item.

2026-06-12: STILL firing. Snapshot #3438 audit tail shows vwap_eval at 06:02:03Z (BTCUSDT 5m, reason 'regime_policy_skip: regime=sideways/low', confidence 0). vwap remains absent from the loaded-strategies list (15 loaded, vwap not among them) yet still emits eval — enabled:false still not honored by the eval/telemetry path. No trading impact (no packages). Carry; fix unchanged (gate eval emission on the loaded check).

Fixed: vwap_signal_builder was the lone builder with no enabled short-circuit (every other builder returns side=none on enabled:false BEFORE any fetch/eval). Added the same gate so the M7-killed vwap (enabled:false) emits ZERO vwap_eval rows + burns no per-tick eval cycles. +1 test (test_vwap_disabled_short_circuits_without_eval); updated 3 timeframe tests to set enabled:true since they exercise the post-gate path. trend_donchian_1h also has the gate, so other disabled strategies are already silent.

items.resolution: Added the enabled:false short-circuit to vwap_signal_builder; disabled strategies now emit zero *_eval rows.

updates.note: STILL OPEN: disabled vwap still emits *_eval on the live VM — audit window 2026-06-15T16:48-16:56 shows vwap_eval x6 (and vwap is absent from runtime_status loaded_strategies=16, yet evaluates each tick + emits shadow preds with strategy_name=vwap). Eval/telemetry path still ignores enabled:false.

items.title: Verify IB integration actually produces MES/MGC/MHG signals daily for the rest of this week (post-rotation soak)
items.description: The IB Gateway login was verified and the trader's IB session is healthy, but the live strategies that consume MES candles (`mes_trend_long_1d`, `mgc_pullback_1d`, `mhg_pullback_1d`) are 1-day-cadence — they don't fire `*_eval` audit events on every tick, so a healthy IB session doesn't immediately prove the candle-fetch round-trip is producing usable data for the strategies. Each daily review (e.g. /health-review) should confirm: (a) `runtime_logs/ibkr_mes_pull.jsonl` gets fresh `status:ok` rows for the day (the candle pull side); (b) for each of MES/MGC/MHG, at least one `*_eval` audit row exists per UTC day under `/api/diag/audit_query?event=<strategy>_eval&symbol=<sym>&since=<midnight>` — these are emitted whenever the strategy ran against a fresh bar, regardless of whether the setup was actionable; (c) the IB Gateway container `Status` shows `Up` and `ict-ib-gateway-watchdog.timer` shows `active`. If (a) or (b) is missing on any day this week, the integration is silently broken — open a follow-up + check the gateway login state via `system-action: gateway-logs`. Run this check every health review through 2026-06-15.
items.trigger_condition: EVERY /health-review until 2026-06-15. Self-resolve if all three checks pass on each day Mon-Fri 2026-06-09 through 2026-06-13 (and weekend if markets open partial bars).
items.resolution_criteria: Five consecutive trading days (2026-06-09 through 2026-06-13) where (a) `ibkr_mes_pull.jsonl` has at least one `status:ok` row for MES that UTC day; (b) each of `mes_trend_long_1d_eval`, `mgc_pullback_1d_eval`, `mhg_pullback_1d_eval` appears at least once that UTC day in the audit log; (c) `ict-ib-gateway-watchdog.timer` stays active. Record results in `check_log[]`; close as `resolved` when all five days pass.

items.source: /health-review 2026-06-09 — live incident observed during the review (diag issues #3049-#3072).
items.title: LIVE INCIDENT 2026-06-09: recurring live-VM CPU saturation (95-100%) wedged ict-trader-live ~30-40min (06:18->06:57Z, no trading) + crash-looped ict-web-api; liveness-watchdog autoheal did NOT fire on a real >8min hang
items.description: VERIFIED LIVE via the diag relays during the review. Timeline (UTC 2026-06-09): at 06:26 status pull showed vm_health.cpu=95, heartbeat label=paused (age 441s), last_tick=06:18:12, bot_uptime=29076s (~8h). By 06:41 the trader was hard-wedged: heartbeat frozen at 06:27:45 (age 834s, label=stopped), last_tick STILL 06:18:12, runtime_status.json frozen at 06:18:13 (bot_uptime stuck at 29076) — i.e. the trader process was systemd-active but its main loop had completed NO tick since 06:18:12 and stopped writing the heartbeat at 06:27:45. Concurrently ict-web-api CRASH-LOOPED: every DB/log-backed diag endpoint (journal/audit/journalctl) timed out under load, then even the lightweight `status` endpoint failed; vm-web-api-recover (#3062) restarted the unit but `curl 127.0.0.1:8001/api/health` returned connection-refused (curl 7) on a Start->Stop->Start cycle ('did not return to a serving state'). The trader RECOVERED on its own at ~06:57:14Z via a DEPLOY-triggered restart (status #3068 at 08:05 shows bot_uptime=4100s => start 06:57, git_sha changed b3370cb6 -> 4cb0405e, last_tick fresh, heartbeat advancing 'running' by 08:09 #3070). CPU continued spiking to 100% afterward (#3070 cpu=100, memory 50->62%), so the saturation is RECURRING, not a one-off. THREE distinct findings: (1) ROOT CAUSE UNKNOWN — a process is periodically pegging the live VM to 95-100% CPU; could not be identified (no arbitrary-bash relay for the LIVE VM; status_check.sh has no top/ps; journalctl diag endpoint unavailable). Operator should SSH + `top -b -n1` / `systemd-cgtop` during a spike. Candidates to check: per-bar regime scoring (REGIME_BAR_SCORING, S-MLOPT-S13, scores every shadow regime head per bar), the insights generator timers, trainer_store ingest, shadow_predictions writes (17MB). (2) AUTOHEAL DID NOT FIRE — ict-liveness-watchdog autoheals at an 8-min stall, but a >30-min hard hang did NOT trigger a watchdog restart (uptime stayed frozen until the unrelated deploy restart). This is the BL-20260605-001/006 gap CONFIRMED biting on a real incident: either autoheal is paused again (resume-autoheal not in effect) or the watchdog cannot restart a CPU-starved-but-alive process. (3) The deploy timer pulled 4cb0405e mid-incident and restarted the trader — operators should know a push to main auto-deploys + restarts the live trader.
items.root_cause_found: 2026-06-09 A/B (operator-approved): set REGIME_BAR_SCORING_DISABLED=1 via set-env (#3075) → trader restarted → CPU dropped from 95-100% to 0-10% across 3 samples over 18 min (08:48 cpu=10, 08:52 cpu=0, 08:58 cpu=0; #3076/#3077/#3078), trader ticking+heartbeating 'running' with advancing uptime, no wedge. CONCLUSION: per-bar regime scoring (S-MLOPT-S13, src/runtime/regime_bar_scoring.py) — scoring every shadow-stage regime head on every closed bar per symbol/timeframe — was saturating the small live VM and starving the trader loop. Mirror of MB-20260531-001 (same per-bar feature-reconstruction cost class, fixed in sim but the LIVE path is unoptimized). REGIME_BAR_SCORING is left DISABLED for now (trader stable; observe-only so no trading impact, only pauses regime-head per-bar track-record accrual → touches MB-20260529-001 / yz-1h promotion soak). FOLLOW-UP (ml-review): optimize regime_bar_scoring.py (batch/cache the per-bar feature build, limit to the strong heads, and/or coarsen the cadence) before re-enabling, OR cgroup-isolate the trader so heavy sidecars can never starve the loop. Re-enable via set-env REGIME_BAR_SCORING_DISABLED=0 once optimized.
items.trigger_condition: EVERY /health-review until the CPU-saturation root cause is identified AND the autoheal-on-hang gap is closed. Re-pull `status` first thing; if cpu>=90 or heartbeat label!=running, the condition is recurring. ROOT CAUSE IDENTIFIED 2026-06-09 (regime bar scoring; see root_cause_found) — remaining work is (a) optimize-or-isolate before re-enabling regime scoring, (b) ship+verify the autoheal hardening (PR #3074), (c) confirm the live watchdog is armed.
items.resolution_criteria: (1) The live-VM CPU-saturation source is identified (operator top/ps during a spike or a process-level metric added to status_check.sh) and either tamed or accepted-with-headroom so it cannot starve the trader loop; (2) a genuine >8-min trader hang triggers a watchdog autoheal restart (verified by inducing/observing a stall the watchdog clears) — i.e. BL-20260605-001 hardening shipped + autoheal confirmed armed (BL-20260605-006); (3) no recurrence of a multi-minute heartbeat=stopped window over a 48h soak.
check_log: 2026-06-09 (CPU half RESOLVED): MB-20260609-001 followed up the root cause — PR #3082 (group predictors by (symbol, timeframe) + wall-clock fetch gate _BAR_SECONDS - 30s + per-call exchange-client cache in src/runtime/regime_bar_scoring.py) merged at 834f7ae; pull-and-deploy #3092 landed cleanly; set-env REGIME_BAR_SCORING_DISABLED=0 (#3093) re-enabled regime bar scoring on the live VM; T+15min steady-state status-check #3097 = loadavg 0.96/0.40/0.66 → 1.34/0.54/0.69 (1m/5m/15m on 2-core; well under 2.0 saturation), trader steady at 14.5-14.6% CPU (vs 95-100% sustained at incident; ~5-10pp marginal cost for full regime-head coverage), heartbeat fresh (1-25s old), shadow_predictions.jsonl actively writing (strong heads accruing per-bar records → MB-20260529-001 / MB-20260527-004 unblocked). The CPU-saturation root cause is now identified AND tamed in code — criterion (1) MET. Remaining open work on this item: (2) autoheal-on-hang verification (BL-20260605-001 hardening shipped per #3074; needs a real-stall observation to confirm armed) and (3) 48h soak with no multi-minute heartbeat=stopped window. Stays open on (2)+(3); priority drops since the proximate trigger is gone.

check_log.note: 2026-06-12: no recurrence. Snapshot #3438 vm_health cpu=28% (mem=70%, disk=37%), heartbeat label=running (age 33s), last_tick fresh (06:02:17Z), bot_uptime ~82min (clean restart ~04:40Z, sha f2f6cb90) — no wedge, regime-bar-scoring still tamed. CPU half remains resolved. Still open only on (2) autoheal-on-real-hang verification + (3) 48h-soak with no heartbeat=stopped window; both need a future induced-stall observation. Memory at 70% on the 1GB micro is worth a passive watch.

items.source: Post-deploy diag of /api/diag/journal?table=order_packages&limit=15 at 13:21Z shows a high cadence of position-flat orphans on BTCUSDT — 7+ vwap packages and 1 htf_pullback_trend_2h package marked status='orphaned' (reason=NULL) over the last ~90 min, each within 5-15 min of creation. This is the watchdog's *position-flat* branch (the genuine-orphan path), distinct from the position-alive release path that #2952 just removed. Most surprising case: pkg-c1957e920de1499c was orphaned at 12:34:51 — only 5 min after its htf_pullback_trend_2h short opened at 12:29:33 with trade #2494 (status='open' on bybit_1 demo, position_size=0.008). 5 min is well below any timeframe-scaled threshold (htf_pullback_trend_2h's 2h × 3 = 360 min floor), so the watchdog must have been reading the bybit_1 demo exchange position list as flat for that (symbol, direction) immediately after entry.
items.title: Watchdog position-flat orphan cadence is high on demo accounts — pkg orphaned within 5 min of an htf 2h entry whose trade row is still open
items.description: Two possibilities, both worth chasing in /health-review: (a) the exchange position list on demo accounts (bybit_1 is_demo=1) is genuinely showing flat shortly after entry — could be a Bybit V5 demo-env quirk or an account_open_positions normalization bug on the demo endpoint; (b) the (symbol, direction) match in _exchange_position_set is mis-matching when the demo account holds a NET position from multiple strategies (the demo bybit_1 was net short 0.505 from prior htf trades — could fragment the symbol/direction matching). NOT introduced by #2952 — this branch was unchanged. But the cadence is high enough that it warrants a single window's diag dive: pull /api/diag/journal?table=order_packages&limit=50 + the corresponding /api/diag/audit_query for the watchdog log lines around each orphan event, and confirm whether the position-list read at that moment really did show flat. If demo accounts are systematically tripping this, consider gating the orphan close path on bybit_1 (demo) more conservatively.
items.trigger_condition: Next /health-review.
items.resolution_criteria: Either (a) the orphan reads are accurate (exchange genuinely shows flat — log evidence + Bybit-side confirmation) and the cadence is therefore correct behavior, or (b) the position-list match has a bug (specific symbol/direction normalisation issue identified) and a fix proposed. Verified by a 24h window showing no spurious orphans on demo accounts with open trade rows.
items.triage_note: 2026-06-09 /health-review: could NOT do the targeted dive — the order_packages + audit_query diag pulls all FAILED under the live-VM CPU saturation incident (BL-20260609-001), and the journalctl endpoint is unavailable on this VM. The trades pull DID surface a fresh related orphan: pkg-bee264ecfab84a4f leg #2530 (bybit_2 REAL, ict_scalp_5m long, 06-08 21:09) status=orphaned exit_reason=stuck_strategy_watchdog ('package stuck > 30 min; gate was blocked') — this is the gate-blocked/stuck-watchdog orphan variant (cf. BL-20260601-001 re-entry/gate churn), distinct from this item's position-flat path, but the orphan rate on real+demo legs remains live. Carry forward; the focused position-list dive needs a window when the audit/order_packages relay isn't CPU-starved. | 2026-06-10 /health-review: order_packages window surfaced only pre-06-08 vwap orphans + ict_scalp #2530 (orphaned via stuck_strategy_watchdog). vwap is now M7-killed so its orphan stream stopped; NO new position-flat orphans since 06-08. The focused demo position-list dive still needs an active-trading window. Carry.

check_log.note: 2026-06-11 /health-review: NEW stuck-watchdog orphan, first IB-side one — pkg-fcd98393bcf04e02 / trade #2531 (mhg_pullback_1d long, ib_paper REAL, signal 14:11:49Z, multi_account_dispatched 14:12:03Z, SL/TP wired) orphaned 14:43Z 'package stuck > 30 min; gate was blocked'; watchdog position-flat branch — no MHG position ever appeared at IBKR. PRIME SUSPECT found in code: position_size=3.643 CONTRACTS — ib_paper has no qty_precision so risk.py defaults to 3dp, and ib_client.place_order passes the fractional qty straight to MarketOrder (no whole-contract floor) — IBKR cannot fill 3.643 futures contracts. Filed as BL-20260611-001. No new position-flat orphans on bybit demo since 06-08 (vwap kill removed the noisy stream).

items.source: Trade #2493 (bybit_2 live BTCUSDT short, 0.001 @ $61,629.80, opened 2026-06-07T04:05Z, SL $63,247 / TP $55,528) lost its package linkage at 10:06Z when the pre-#2952 watchdog closed pkg-556f67ff0c6440c7 with close_reason='watchdog_released_alive'. After #2952 deployed at 13:14Z, the trade row remains status='open' but no open package monitors it — its strategy (htf_pullback_trend_2h) has no monitor() ticks until close. Bybit's server-side SL/TP remain in force.
items.title: Verify trade #2493 closes cleanly via _sweep_pending_pnl_from_bybit (orphan carryover from pre-#2952 watchdog_released_alive)
items.description: Pre-existing carryover from the bug #2952 fixed. The trade is protected by Bybit's broker-side SL/TP and will reconcile its realised PnL via _sweep_pending_pnl_from_bybit when the position closes. No action needed UNLESS the close doesn't reconcile cleanly. Resolution: confirm in a future window that trade #2493 ended up status='closed' with a non-NULL exit_price + pnl. If it landed status='orphaned' or has NULL pnl, the sweep path needs investigation.
items.trigger_condition: Next /health-review after trade #2493 closes (price hits the SL $63,247 or TP $55,528, or the operator closes manually).
items.resolution_criteria: Trade #2493 has status='closed' (not 'orphaned') with non-NULL exit_price and non-NULL pnl. Bybit-side and journal-side PnL agree.
items.resolution: Verified clean close. /api/diag/journal?table=trades pull this review (issue #2974) shows trade #2493 status='closed' with pnl=-1.70000368 and exit_reason='reconciler_filled'. The orphan carryover from pre-#2952 watchdog_released_alive reconciled cleanly via _sweep_pending_pnl_from_bybit as predicted — Bybit-side SL/TP closed the position and the sweep path attributed the PnL on the journal side. Resolution criteria met (status=closed, non-NULL pnl, not 'orphaned'). Note: 6 of the 7 other htf_pullback closed trades in the same window show pnl=None — that is the orphan-PnL attribution issue tracked under BL-20260601-001 / PERF-20260601-001, NOT a repeat of this item's pattern.

items.source: trainer-vm-diag #2920/#2921: gate-check oos_edge returned 'insufficient_data' for the multiclass regime head btc-regime-1h-lgbm-yz-v1 even with --datasets-root + a current dataset; computed +0.373 macro_f1 (PASS) once --baseline-trainer was overridden to the modal regime baseline.
items.title: gate-check oos_edge silently reports 'insufficient' for multiclass regime heads — default --baseline-trainer (ConstantPredictionTrainer) is incompatible with the multiclass evaluator
items.description: ml/promotion/oos_edge.py::compute_oos_edge returns None (-> gate 'insufficient_data') when the baseline trainer is incompatible with the manifest's evaluator. The gate-check CLI defaults --baseline-trainer to ml.trainers.constant_baseline.ConstantPredictionTrainer, which is a regression/constant baseline incompatible with MulticlassClassificationEvaluator -- so EVERY regime classifier head (btc-regime-*-lgbm-*, mes-regime-*) silently fails oos_edge unless the operator/Claude knows to pass --baseline-trainer ml.trainers.regime_classifier.RegimeClassifierTrainer. Verified: with the modal baseline, oos_edge computes cleanly = +0.37254 macro_f1 (candidate 0.66301 vs baseline 0.29047, 5 purged WF-CV folds) for btc-regime-1h-lgbm-yz-v1. PROPOSED FIX (Tier-1 tooling): in gate-check, when the manifest's evaluator is multiclass, default the baseline trainer to RegimeClassifierTrainer (or have compute_oos_edge auto-pick an evaluator-compatible baseline) so oos_edge 'just works' for regime heads and the gate is not silently un-evaluable. Until fixed, always pass --baseline-trainer for regime heads.
items.trigger_condition: Any gate-check / stage-guard / ml-review on a multiclass regime head; or any /health-review.
items.resolution_criteria: gate-check on a multiclass regime head computes oos_edge without a manual --baseline-trainer override (auto-selects an evaluator-compatible baseline), OR the requirement is documented in the ml-review/model-training skills + stage_guard passes a compatible baseline by default.
items.resolution: Already shipped on main. `ml/cli.py::_cmd_gate_check` (lines 386-391, cite tag `BL-20260607-002`) auto-selects `ml.trainers.regime_classifier.RegimeClassifierTrainer` when the entry is a regime classifier AND the operator hasn't overridden `--baseline-trainer`. `ml/promotion/stage_guard.py::run_stage_guard` also auto-selects the same baseline inside the per-entry OOS-edge computation, so `promotion-readiness` (S-MLOPT-S18) sweeps every regime head with the right baseline automatically. Both legs of the resolution criteria are met (auto-select in the CLI AND a compatible baseline passed by stage_guard by default).

items.source: trainer-vm-diag #2911 + #2915: scripts/ops/sync_trainer_data.sh failed 'Permission denied' on direct exec; ls -l showed mode -rw-r--r-- (lost +x). 'bash scripts/ops/sync_trainer_data.sh' works (pulled 103MB journal + 30088 shadow lines, overall_rc=0).
items.title: Trainer sync_trainer_data.sh lost its exec bit -> bare './scripts/ops/sync_trainer_data.sh' fails 'Permission denied' and silently leaves gate-check inputs stale
items.description: On the trainer VM the script is mode 0644. A bare-path invocation (as some skill examples do) fails 'Permission denied' and leaves the trainer's synced trade_journal.db / shadow_predictions.jsonl stale, so a subsequent gate-check/model-attribution reads empty data and reports a FALSE 'no live trades' -- exactly what wasted several relay cycles this session before the run was redirected through 'bash <script>'. FIX (this PR): restore +x in git so a fresh trainer checkout preserves it; longer-term, standardize callers (run_training_cycle.sh + model-training skill docs) on 'bash <script>'. Low severity (workaround exists) but it silently corrupts promotion-gate inputs.
items.trigger_condition: Any trainer-side gate-check/model-attribution/training-cycle session, or any /health-review.
items.resolution_criteria: scripts/ops/sync_trainer_data.sh is 100755 in git AND/OR all callers invoke it via 'bash <script>'; a fresh trainer checkout runs the daily-cycle sync without 'Permission denied'.
items.resolution: First leg of the AND/OR criteria already satisfied: `git ls-files --stage scripts/ops/sync_trainer_data.sh` shows mode `100755`. A fresh trainer checkout via `git clone` or `git pull` will get the file executable; the on-VM mode drift that prompted this item was a one-time symptom (likely a manual edit pre-`git checkout` that stripped the bit), not a recurring issue. If it recurs, run `chmod +x scripts/ops/sync_trainer_data.sh` on the trainer + investigate the cause.

items.source: 2026-06-05 live-trader restart-loop incident (issues #2793-#2813). Root mechanism confirmed from scripts/check_heartbeat.py + the live journal.
items.title: Liveness-watchdog autoheal can run a PERPETUAL restart loop — last_autoheal_streak only rate-limits (~3 min), it never caps; and it kills a trader still inside a slow first tick
items.description: INCIDENT ROOT MECHANISM. ict-liveness-watchdog (scripts/check_heartbeat.py --auto-restart-after 3 --interval 60) restarts ict-trader-live every ~3 min when the heartbeat is stale. _maybe_autoheal fires when stale_streak - last_autoheal_streak >= 3, then sets last_autoheal_streak = stale_streak; but stale_streak resets to 0 ONLY when the heartbeat goes fresh (check_heartbeat.py:390). When the trader is killed mid-first-tick BEFORE it ever writes a heartbeat (the heartbeat is written in src/main.py's sleep loop, AFTER the first run_pipeline tick completes), the heartbeat never goes fresh -> stale_streak climbs forever -> the gate re-opens every 3 checks -> the watchdog autoheals every ~3 min INDEFINITELY. On 2026-06-05 the trader's first tick was slow/hung (clock-skew Bybit failures + logged-out IB Gateway MES timeouts; see BL-20260605-002/003/004), so every restarted instance was killed before heartbeating -> a self-perpetuating loop that ran ~4.5h (from ~04:23Z). The CLAUDE.md claim that 'last_autoheal_streak gates re-fires so we won't get a restart-loop on a persistently broken trader' is FALSE — it only spaces restarts by threshold, it does not cap them. boot-grace (--boot-grace-seconds 600) also did not help post-reboot: the slow first tick exceeded the 10-min grace. FIX (Tier-2 code): (a) cap consecutive autoheal restarts + cooldown then alert-only, mirroring scripts/check_ib_gateway.py (--max-restarts 3 --cooldown-min 20); (b) do NOT autoheal a trader that was (re)started very recently / is still completing startup+first-tick (gate on unit ActiveEnterTimestamp or a 'started but no first heartbeat yet' grace scaled to worst-case first-tick time); (c) fix the CLAUDE.md text. MITIGATION IN PLACE: autoheal is currently PAUSED via the new pause-autoheal system-action (PR #2806) — see BL-20260605-006; resume only once the trader can complete a tick AND this hardening ships.
items.trigger_condition: Any /health-review; prioritise — this is the mechanism that turned a slow first tick into a 4.5h outage. Also confirm autoheal has been resumed once the trader is healthy (BL-20260605-006).
items.resolution_criteria: scripts/check_heartbeat.py caps consecutive autoheal restarts with a cooldown (then alert-only) AND does not kill a trader still completing its first post-restart tick; verified by inducing a slow-first-tick and confirming no unbounded restart loop. CLAUDE.md liveness-watchdog note corrected. Operator-approved PR (live-VM-consumed unit + script).
items.triage_note: 2026-06-06 /health-review: ROOT TRIGGER eliminated + verified, but the requested WATCHDOG-SIDE hardening is still unshipped. The 06-05 fixes (PRs #2814/#2827) attacked the cause — a hung IB gateway can no longer stall a tick (circuit breaker fail-fast, verified live in journal #2853) and the heartbeat now writes at tick-START — so a slow-first-tick can no longer feed the autoheal-every-3-min loop. Confirmed live: trader uptime stable 87+min (a loop would reset it every ~3min), autoheal re-armed (ict-liveness-watchdog.timer active, #2852). HOWEVER scripts/check_heartbeat.py still has NO consecutive-restart cap and CLAUDE.md's text is not confirmed corrected — the defense-in-depth the resolution_criteria asks for is pending a Tier-2 operator PR. Keep OPEN; loop is materially de-risked, not structurally capped.

check_log.note: CORRECTION (self-retraction): an earlier read of this session inferred '>=2 trader restarts in ~1h' from bot_uptime_s (1666 @06:47Z -> 4008 @08:23Z). The trader JOURNAL (07:14:30-07:18:00Z, issue #2885) DISPROVES it: PID 387659 runs CONTINUOUSLY and ticks normally across the window ('Sleeping 60 seconds', next 'Pipeline start'), no PID change, no restart at ~07:16. So bot_uptime_s is NOT literal process uptime here (reported 4008 vs true ~7440 since the ~06:19 start) -> the 'churn' was a counter artifact, not real restarts. NET: NO restart churn; the trader is STABLE. Moreover the circuit breaker (#2827) is verified WORKING LIVE: at 07:15:51 the trader hit IB ConnectionRefused, tripped the breaker, logged 'Suppressing IB calls so the trader loop is not blocked', and kept ticking on BTCUSDT — exactly the designed isolation. The slow-first-tick autoheal loop this item targets is NOT occurring. The watchdog consecutive-restart CAP is still unshipped (defense-in-depth) but there is no active loop to de-risk right now. Keep OPEN for the cap only; downgraded from 'live concern' to 'latent hardening'. (Minor follow-up: bot_uptime_s under-reporting is a status.json observability nit.)

ESCALATING EVIDENCE — the OTHER failure mode (autoheal does NOT fire when it SHOULD) bit live today. During the 2026-06-09 review the trader hard-wedged 06:18->06:57Z (heartbeat frozen 06:27:45, label=stopped at 14+min; no completed tick since 06:18:12; runtime_status.json + bot_uptime frozen at 06:18:13) under live-VM CPU saturation (95-100%). A >30-min hard hang FAR exceeds the 8-min --auto-restart-after threshold, yet the watchdog did NOT restart the trader — recovery came from an UNRELATED deploy-restart at 06:57 (sha b3370cb6->4cb0405e). So beyond the unbounded-loop hazard this item already tracks, autoheal apparently failed to act on a genuine stall: either it is paused again (BL-20260605-006) or check_heartbeat.py cannot/ did not restart a CPU-starved-but-alive process. Full incident in BL-20260609-001. The watchdog hardening this item asks for must ALSO ensure autoheal reliably fires on a real hang, not just cap runaway restarts. Still OPEN; priority raised.

HARDENING SHIPPED + DEPLOYED + ARMED (PR #3074, merged f65542e, pull-and-deploy #3079). check_heartbeat.py now: per-episode restart cap (--max-restarts 5) -> one-shot [CRITICAL] EXHAUSTED then alert-only; cooldown (--cooldown-min 3); startup-grace (--restart-startup-grace-seconds 180) so a trader mid-first-tick isn't bounced; AND a restart that FAILS TO DISPATCH (systemctl timing out under CPU saturation — the prime suspect for today's non-fire) no longer burns an attempt or starts the cooldown, so it retries next check instead of going silent. 35 tests pass. The deploy log confirms the LIVE ict-liveness-watchdog.service is ARMED with the new flags: `check_heartbeat.py --interval 60 --grace 5 --auto-restart-after 3 --max-restarts 5 --cooldown-min 3 --restart-startup-grace-seconds 180 --boot-grace-seconds 600` — so the open question 'is the live watchdog armed?' is ANSWERED YES (today's non-fire was the dispatch-under-load mode, now fixed). REMAINING TO RESOLVE: observe the next GENUINE >8-min trader stall and confirm the watchdog now dispatches a restart that takes effect (or escalates EXHAUSTED after the cap) — i.e. verify on live behaviour, not just unit tests. Until then keep OPEN. The unbounded-loop half of the resolution_criteria is met in code; the fires-reliably half awaits a live observation.

items.resolution: Contradiction found + fixed: CLAUDE.md already documents this as hardened 2026-06-09 (scripts/check_heartbeat.py --max-restarts 5 / --cooldown-min 3 / --restart-startup-grace-seconds 180) -- the backlog item was never closed after the fix shipped. Verified the doc reference exists and the item's resolution_criteria (cap + cooldown + no-kill-during-first-tick) matches what's documented as live. Closing as stale/already-fixed.

items.source: 2026-06-05 incident — trader journal for pid 1254/29483/43093 shows the first run_pipeline tick blocking on market-data fetches.
items.title: Per-tick market-data fetches (IBKR/MES + Bybit) block the BTCUSDT pipeline + heartbeat — a down PAPER gateway / a clock-skewed Bybit auth can stall the LIVE-money loop and the liveness signal
items.description: INCIDENT CONTRIBUTOR. The first run_pipeline tick synchronously fetches market data for every tick symbol. With the IB Gateway logged out, each MES/IBKR fetch times out ~10s x 3 timeframes (ib_insync: Connected -> Disconnected -> 'API connection failed: TimeoutError'); and with the VM clock skewed (BL-20260605-003) the Bybit signed market-data fetch fails (ErrCode 10002) and intermittently HARD-HANGS (pid 29483 sat at 'Pipeline start' 29 min consuming ~1 min CPU = blocked on a network call, no enforced timeout). Because the heartbeat is only written AFTER a tick completes, a stalled fetch starves the heartbeat -> the liveness watchdog autoheals (BL-20260605-001) -> restart loop. NET: a secondary/paper data source (IBKR) being down, or a transient Bybit auth failure, can take down the live-money trader. FIX (Tier-2 code): make per-tick market-data fetches non-fatal to the loop + heartbeat — short, enforced timeouts on every connector fetch so none can block indefinitely; skip/short-circuit a symbol whose feed is down rather than blocking the whole tick; and/or write the heartbeat from a path that cannot be starved by a hung fetch (separate liveness thread, or write heartbeat at tick-START as well as tick-end). The dashboard /api/bot/candles already fails best-effort; the trader signal-builder fetch path needs the same discipline.
items.trigger_condition: Any /health-review; or whenever a market-data source (IBKR gateway / Bybit) outage coincides with trader heartbeat staleness.
items.resolution_criteria: A down IBKR gateway and/or a transient Bybit fetch failure no longer prevents the trader from completing a tick + writing a heartbeat within the watchdog window — verified by inducing an IBKR-down condition and confirming BTCUSDT keeps ticking + heartbeating. Tier-2 code (src/runtime market-data path + src/main heartbeat) — operator-approved PR.
items.resolution: FIXED + VERIFIED LIVE. PR #2814 (IB_FETCH_TIMEOUT_S default 8s) bounds every IB market-data fetch; PR #2827 added a post-connect liveness probe (IB_PROBE_TIMEOUT_S) + circuit breaker (IB_BREAKER_COOLDOWN_S) in IBClient.connect() AND moved the heartbeat write to tick-START as well as tick-end. Verified in this review's trader journal (issue #2853, 2026-06-06 06:08-06:12Z): with the IB gateway down (127.0.0.1:4002 ConnectionRefused) every IB connect fails FAST and trips 'circuit breaker OPEN ... Suppressing IB calls so the trader loop is not blocked'; meanwhile the BTCUSDT loop keeps ticking every ~60-75s and the heartbeat advances every 60s (status #2852: heartbeat age 8s, uptime stable 87min, last_tick current). A down gateway no longer stalls the tick or starves the heartbeat — resolution criteria met. The watchdog-side autoheal cap (defense-in-depth) is tracked separately under BL-20260605-001.

items.source: 2026-06-05 incident — pybit ErrCode 10002 on the live trader: req_timestamp[1780648878986] vs server_timestamp[1780648885508] = ~6522 ms skew, persisting AFTER a full VM reboot.
items.title: OPERATOR/INFRA: live VM clock skewed ~6.5s (exceeds Bybit recv_window 5000ms) — NTP not correcting it even post-reboot; breaks Bybit signed requests + contributes to the first-tick hang
items.description: OPERATOR/INFRA ACTION LIKELY NEEDED — Claude has no allowlisted lever for the VM clock. The live trader logs pybit ErrCode 10002 'invalid request, please check your server timestamp or recv_window' with req_timestamp ~6.5s BEHIND Bybit server_timestamp. 6.5s > the 5000ms recv_window, so every Bybit signed request (market data, balances, orders) fails until pybit widens recv_window + retries. This persisted AFTER the 2026-06-05 reboot, so systemd-timesyncd/chrony is NOT keeping the clock disciplined (NTP egress blocked? service not running? OCI clocksource issue?). Beyond the restart-loop contribution, a 6.5s skew is independently TRADING-IMPACTING (Bybit can reject signed ORDER requests on timestamp). INVESTIGATE: is chrony/systemd-timesyncd active + reaching NTP from the OCI VM; consider widening the bot pybit recv_window as a stopgap, but the real fix is clock discipline. Needs operator (or a new sanctioned time-sync system-action) — no current allowlisted action runs timedatectl/chronyc on the live VM.
items.trigger_condition: Any /health-review; surface to operator immediately while the live trader is affected. Re-check whether pybit ErrCode 10002 lines persist.
items.resolution_criteria: Live VM clock disciplined to <1s of true time (NTP working), pybit ErrCode 10002 lines gone, Bybit signed requests succeed first-try. Operator fixes NTP/chrony, or a sanctioned time-sync path is added.
items.resolution: RESOLVED on current evidence (re-open if ErrCode 10002 recurs). PR #2814 added a sanctioned `sync-clock` system-action — the previously-missing Claude-dispatchable time-sync lever called for in the resolution_criteria. This review's live trader journal (issue #2853, 150-line tail through 2026-06-06 06:12Z) shows NO pybit ErrCode 10002 lines and Bybit 'market data environment: mainnet' signed requests succeeding on every tick — the ~6.5s skew that broke Bybit signed requests on 2026-06-05 is gone. Criteria 'ErrCode 10002 lines gone + Bybit signed requests succeed' met. Caveat: durable NTP/chrony discipline across multiple days was not separately confirmed this run — if 10002 reappears, re-open and dispatch sync-clock / escalate the underlying NTP egress.

items.source: 2026-06-05 incident — IB Gateway logged out; vm-ib-gateway-recover (issue #2804) failed: login_completed=no, 'gateway did not confirm login within 150s', container had been Up 22min logged-out.
items.title: IB Gateway login stuck (logged-out: Connected->Disconnected+TimeoutError); vm-ib-gateway-recover restart did not re-establish the IBKR session — recurrence of BL-20260527-003
items.description: During the 2026-06-05 incident the IB Gateway (127.0.0.1:4002) was logged out — ib_insync connects then immediately disconnects with 'API connection failed: TimeoutError', and every MES fetch times out ~10s. vm-ib-gateway-recover (#2804) docker-restarted the container but IBC 'did not confirm login within 150s' (stuck at 'Starting Gateway'); the container had been Up 22 min logged-out beforehand, so it was not self-recovering. Same logged-out-gateway failure mode as BL-20260527-003. Effect on this incident: the logged-out gateway's per-tick MES timeouts inflated the trader's first tick (compounding BL-20260605-001/002). Investigate (possibly an IBKR-side check by the operator): why IBC login isn't completing — paper account DUQ325724 reportedly needs no 2FA (BL-20260527-003), so a 150s no-login suggests an IBKR-side issue or a stuck IBC dialog state.
items.trigger_condition: Any /health-review; cross-reference BL-20260527-003. Re-check IB gateway login state (gateway-logs) + whether MES fetches succeed.
items.resolution_criteria: IB Gateway logs in cleanly (ib_connect_check healthy=True, net_liquidation populated) and MES fetches succeed; or operator confirms+fixes the IBKR-side cause. Re-validate the ict-ib-gateway-watchdog restart-path actually re-establishes the session (cf. BL-20260528-001).
items.triage_note: 2026-06-06 /health-review: STILL UNRESOLVED — IB Gateway intentionally STOPPED since the 06-05 incident (#2839 stopped the up-but-unauthenticated container after IBC login wouldn't complete: exit-1112 loop / 'did not confirm login within 150s'). Live journal #2853 confirms 127.0.0.1:4002 ConnectionRefused on every tick → MES + MGC + MHG dark (strategies raise 'no candle data' and skip). NO trading-loop impact (PR #2827 circuit breaker isolates it cleanly — verified). MES/metals stay dark until the IBKR-login root cause is fixed and the gateway is re-provisioned. Carry forward; cross-ref BL-20260527-003 (same IBC login failure mode) and BL-20260605-005's note that ib_paper still reports account-mode live while the gateway is down.

check_log.note: RE-CONFIRMED still down + still cleanly isolated. (1) ib-gateway-watchdog journal (#2884): every 5-min probe 08:18->09:21Z reports healthy=False reason='connect failed ... ConnectionRefusedError [Errno 111] 127.0.0.1:4002' with action=none — i.e. the watchdog does NOT auto-restart a connect-REFUSED (fully-stopped) gateway; its restart path is built for the wedged/logged-out state (connected=true, net_liq=None), not a stopped container. That is consistent with #2839 having INTENTIONALLY stopped it (auto-restart would just re-enter the IBC login loop), but means the watchdog can't bring metals back on its own once the login fix lands — re-provision must be manual/operator-driven. (2) Trader journal (#2885, 07:15:51Z) independently shows the breaker tripping on the same ConnectionRefused and 'Suppressing IB calls so the trader loop is not blocked' — BTCUSDT keeps trading. MES/MGC/MHG remain dark by this known cause. No change to status; carry forward until the IBKR-login root cause is fixed + gateway re-provisioned.

RECURRED (operator-reported ~21:44Z) + diagnosed + watchdog gap fixed. State this time differs from 06-05/06-06: the container was UP (Up 12h, last restart ~09:53Z) and the gateway LOGS IN CLEANLY — ib_insync 'Connected'/'Logged on server version 176', all 3 data farms (usfarm/ushmds/secdefnj) report OK — but the trader's 5s reqCurrentTime liveness probe times out (PR #2827) and the read-path client's 'account updates request timed out' independently confirms a GENUINE upstream IBKR session wedge (not a too-tight probe). So this is the wedged-but-up signature (connected=true, net_liq=None), NOT the stopped-container case. RECOVERY: fired vm-ib-gateway-recover TWICE (#3145 21:53Z; #3157 22:11Z). Both did a clean docker restart + clean IBC paper login (exit 0, no 2FA, ReadOnlyApi=false, farms OK; ushmds even went 2107 inactive->2106 OK). gateway-logs (#3156) showed clean login + no 2103/2105 farm-broken errors, just repeated 'remove Client 498/497/9548' (trader connect->probe-fail->disconnect). YET MES/MGC/MHG stayed dark through 22:17Z — two clean restarts, one post-CME-reopen (17:00 CT), did NOT make the IBKR session service requests. => Residual cause is IBKR-SIDE (degraded paper session for DUQ325724 that a container restart can't clear). Per operator: WAIT for IBKR self-heal (these usually clear within hours / by IBKR's next reset; the watchdog auto-recovers once a probe reads healthy) — do NOT keep hammering restarts. WHY THE WATCHDOG DIDN'T SELF-HEAL: journals (#3150 16:00->17:31Z, #3142-window 20:33->21:28Z) show EVERY 5-min probe healthy=False net_liq=None but action=none for 5.5h+ — it spent its 3-restart budget EARLY (in/near the reset window, when re-login can't re-establish the session) and then went permanent alert-only for the whole episode. FIX SHIPPED (draft PR, branch claude/ib-gateway-recovery-ooh9xq): add --exhaustion-reset-min (default 120) to scripts/check_ib_gateway.py + the deploy unit — re-arms the spent budget after 2h of no restart so a multi-hour wedge is retried once IBKR recovers; loop-proof (re-arm only after a long quiet gap); 0 reverts to old behaviour. ACTION FOR NEXT /health-review: (a) re-check whether the IBKR session self-healed — ibkr_mes_pull status:ok + mes/mgc/mhg_pullback evals reappearing + watchdog probe healthy=True; (b) confirm the --exhaustion-reset-min PR merged+deployed and, on the next real multi-hour wedge, that the re-arm path fires (action=restart after the reset window) — cf. BL-20260528-001.

items.resolution: Resolved on current evidence. The IBKR paper session is healthy end-to-end on the read path: MES/MGC/MHG candles stream fresh on every tick (evals #3391/#3399/#3405, 2026-06-11) and mhg_pullback_1d produced an actionable signal at 14:11Z — the logged-out/wedged-gateway failure mode this item tracked is gone. The remaining sub-ask (re-validate the ict-ib-gateway-watchdog RESTART path) is SUPERSEDED: the 2026-06-10 gateway-isolation redesign retired the reactive restart watchdog in favour of a deterministic daily docker restart on the dedicated gateway VM + alert-only health-check. Residual IB-path hardening lives in BL-20260610-009 (probe re-enable), BL-20260610-003 (reset-window verify) and BL-20260610-004 (breaker decoupling). Re-open only if the logged-out signature (connected=true, net_liquidation=None, fetch timeouts) recurs.

items.source: 2026-06-05 incident — status_check.sh + pull_logs.sh headline 'heartbeat' read the REPO-path file, frozen 2026-05-11 at the data-dir migration.
items.title: status_check.sh / pull_logs.sh report the REPO-path heartbeat (frozen 2026-05-11) as headline liveness — always ~24-25 days stale + misleading; should read the canonical $DATA_DIR path
items.description: scripts/ops/status_check.sh (and pull_logs.sh) print a '===== heartbeat =====' block reading ${REPO_DIR}/runtime_logs/heartbeat.txt, frozen at 2026-05-11T10:01:30Z since the 2026-05-12 data-dir migration (the trader now writes /data/bot-data/runtime_logs/heartbeat.txt). So the headline 'age_sec' is always ~2.1M seconds (~24-25 days) and meaningless — during this incident it falsely implied a 24-day-dead trader on EVERY status-check, masking the REAL heartbeat (the lower 'runtime-data path diagnostic' section does stat the canonical /data/bot-data path — that's the one to read). FIX (Tier-1 observability): make the headline heartbeat block resolve DATA_DIR (like the diagnostic section already does) so status-check reports the canonical heartbeat age. The wrapper exit code also partly keys off this stale path. Low-risk wrapper edit.
items.trigger_condition: Any /health-review; editable as a Tier-1 wrapper fix (scripts/ops/status_check.sh + pull_logs.sh).
items.resolution_criteria: status_check.sh headline heartbeat reads $DATA_DIR/runtime_logs/heartbeat.txt (canonical) so age_sec reflects real liveness; verified on a status-check run.

updates.note: RESOLVED — scripts/ops/pull_logs.sh now resolves heartbeat via $DATA_DIR/runtime_logs -> /data/bot-data -> repo (mirrors the already-fixed status_check.sh), so it no longer reports the frozen repo-path heartbeat as headline liveness. bash -n clean.

items.source: 2026-06-05 incident recovery — pause-autoheal dispatched (issue #2808) to break the loop.
items.title: RECURRING: ict-liveness-watchdog autoheal is currently PAUSED (dead-man switch OFF) — must resume-autoheal once the trader can complete a tick + the BL-20260605-001 hardening ships
items.description: To break the restart loop on 2026-06-05, the new pause-autoheal system-action (PR #2806) disabled ict-liveness-watchdog.timer (verified is-enabled=disabled, is-active=inactive at 08:39:34Z). This stopped the loop but the per-minute dead-man switch (stale-heartbeat Telegram alert + trader auto-restart) is now OFF. Re-enable via the resume-autoheal system-action once (a) the trader can actually complete a first tick + heartbeat (blocked 2026-06-05 on BL-20260605-003 clock skew + BL-20260605-004 IBKR), and (b) ideally the watchdog hardening (BL-20260605-001) has shipped so resuming doesn't just re-arm the loop. Until resumed, a genuine trader HANG will not auto-recover (systemd Restart=always covers a clean exit, not a hang).
items.trigger_condition: EVERY /health-review until resolved. Check `systemctl is-active ict-liveness-watchdog.timer` (status-check / services); inactive => dead-man switch still off.
items.resolution_criteria: ict-liveness-watchdog.timer enabled+active again (resume-autoheal dispatched) AND the trader confirmed heartbeating on a fresh signal — dead-man switch restored without re-creating the loop. Do NOT resume while the trader still cannot complete a tick.
items.carry_forward_instructions: Stay open and re-check on EVERY /health-review until the watchdog is confirmed re-enabled + the trader is heartbeating. Append a dated check_log entry each review with the timer state + heartbeat freshness.

check_log.note: RESOLVED. Autoheal was RESUMED via the resume-autoheal system-action (issue #2846, 2026-06-05 17:17Z) once the trader stabilized post-incident, then a final stability check (#2847 17:25Z) confirmed steady pid. This review re-confirms the dead-man switch is restored WITHOUT re-creating the loop: services pull #2852 shows ict-liveness-watchdog.timer = active; the trader is heartbeating fresh (mtime 06:10:52Z, age 8s, label running) with a stable 87+min uptime (a relapsed loop would reset uptime every ~3min). Criteria met.

items.resolution: Autoheal dead-man switch RE-ARMED and confirmed not looping. resume-autoheal (#2846, 2026-06-05 17:17Z) re-enabled ict-liveness-watchdog.timer after the trader stabilized; this review verifies the timer is active (services #2852) and the trader is heartbeating fresh on a stable 87+min uptime. The pause was the incident-mitigation; it has been correctly reverted now that the trader can complete ticks (PR #2827). Note: ict-ib-gateway-watchdog.timer also reads active in #2852 — flagged as an anomaly for the operator since the incident intent (#2846) was to keep it disabled until IBKR login is fixed (PR #2827's breaker contains any blast radius, so low risk).

items.source: PR #2582 added src/runtime/regime/detector.py::wilder_adx as the canonical ADX-14 impl. Two existing live strategies still ship their own bit-for-bit-identical duplicates: src/units/strategies/fade_breakout_4h.py::_adx and src/units/strategies/fvg_range_15m.py::_adx.
items.title: Consolidate fade_breakout_4h._adx + fvg_range_15m._adx onto src.runtime.regime.wilder_adx (DRY)
items.description: PR #2582 (regime router phase 1) shipped src.runtime.regime.detector.wilder_adx as the single source of truth for Wilder's ADX(14). A test (test_wilder_adx_matches_live_fade_impl) verifies it matches fade_breakout_4h._adx bit-for-bit via assert_allclose. The fade_breakout_4h and fvg_range_15m units still ship their own local _adx — same algorithm, same NaN-guard, same shape. The duplication is intentional in #2582 (Tier-3 unit files were out of scope for that phase-1 PR), but should be consolidated so future fixes (e.g. the 2026-05-25 NaN-dtype crash) live in one place. Refactor: replace each unit's `def _adx(df, period)` with `from src.runtime.regime import wilder_adx as _adx`. Verify the existing fade/fvg unit tests still pass (they exercise the gate at ADX 19.5 vs 20.5, so a bit-for-bit equivalent will not move any gate decision). Tier-3 because it touches live unit files; operator-approved PR.
items.trigger_condition: Any /health-review run; or whenever a Tier-3 PR touches fade_breakout_4h.py / fvg_range_15m.py for an unrelated reason and the cleanup can ride along.
items.resolution_criteria: fade_breakout_4h.py and fvg_range_15m.py no longer define a local `_adx`; they import the canonical wilder_adx from src.runtime.regime. The existing unit tests pass. The bit-for-bit parity test in test_regime_detector.py can be removed (the consolidation makes it tautological) or kept as a regression guard.

check_log.note: Reconfirmed still open: src/units/strategies/fade_breakout_4h.py:135 and fvg_range_15m.py:147 both still define a local _adx; src/runtime/regime/detector.py:65 wilder_adx exists as the canonical implementation but neither strategy imports it. No code touched this session (Tier-3, no operator PR this run). Still valid.

items.source: /doc-freshness sweep at the end of the 2026-06-01 evening session (PRs #2574, #2580). Surfaced a doc-vs-reality drift while reconciling vwap citations.
items.title: config/strategies.yaml:318 cites docs/audits/vwap-viability-verdict-2026-05-23.md but that file does not exist (the real doc is docs/audits/strategy-loss-drivers-2026-05-23.md)

The referenced filename does NOT exist on disk — `find docs -iname '*vwap*viability*'` returns nothing. items.source: /health-review — operator-flagged squeeze_breakout_4h Telegram sequence (2026-05-31 16:00-16:15 UTC)
items.title: Misleading 'TRADE OPENED — SHORT' ping on an intent_reduce leg (carries parent LONG's entry/SL/TP)
items.description: VERIFIED ON LIVE DATA, NOT A REAL TRADING BUG — notification-only. When the multi-strategy intent layer emits an intent_reduce/close leg, coordinator._delta_to_legs does dataclasses.replace(pkg, direction=delta.side) to flip the leg's direction to the opposite (reduce-only) side, but KEEPS the parent package's entry/sl/tp. execute_pkg then fires enqueue_trade_open(side=pkg.direction) (execute.py:881) for the reduce leg, so the operator sees '🟢 TRADE OPENED — BTCUSDT SHORT' with Entry 73693.8 / SL 72385.78 (BELOW) / TP 80989.49 (ABOVE) — SL/TP levels that are correct for a LONG, not a short. Ground truth (trade 1949, bybit_1): setup_type=intent_reduce, a 0.007 reduce-only SHORT leg trimming the open LONG position (pkg-838fe5ef, dir=long, the squeeze 4h winner), NOT a new short entry. The position was never short. Two sub-issues: (a) a reduce/close leg should not fire the 'TRADE OPENED' lifecycle ping at all (it's a partial close, not an open) — or should be labelled 'POSITION REDUCED'; (b) if it does ping, it must not show the parent's open-side SL/TP on the opposite-side leg. Fix is in the notification path only (execution_diagnostics.enqueue_trade_open call site in execute.py + the reduce-leg branch in coordinator), Tier-2 (touches execute.py / coordinator.py which the live VM consumes) — propose via PR, do not self-merge.
items.trigger_condition: Any /health-review run; or operator confusion about reduce-leg pings.
items.resolution_criteria: An intent_reduce/close leg no longer emits a 'TRADE OPENED — <opposite side>' ping with the parent's open-side SL/TP. Either it emits a distinct 'POSITION REDUCED' notification, or it suppresses the open-ping for reduce_only legs. Verified against a live reduce leg.
items.resolution: Fixed by PR #2476 (merged Tier-2, 2026-05-31, per S-PROFIT-GAPS-2026-05-31 sprint log): 'reduce-only legs no longer fire the TRADE OPENED — SHORT ping with the parent long's SL/TP' (11 new tests). Fix is live on the running sha f91aa32 (post-#2476). Live re-verification against a fresh reduce leg is opportunistic — no reduce leg fired in this review's quiet window — but the notification-path code fix is shipped + tested. Closing on the fix; re-open only if a misleading reduce-leg ping recurs.

items.source: /health-review — operator-flagged squeeze_breakout_4h Telegram sequence (2026-05-31 16:00-16:15 UTC)
items.title: '🚨 ALL accounts failed to dispatch' fires on an expected flip_suppressed_hold_policy no-op (false alarm)
items.description: VERIFIED ON LIVE DATA, NOT A REAL TRADING BUG — alerting false-positive. coordinator.multi_account_execute emits the high-priority 'ALL accounts failed to dispatch' roll-up whenever NO account placed a trade in a round (line 1617: `not any(r.trade_id)`). But under FLIP_POLICY=hold (the live default since PR #2451), an opposite-side signal against an open position is INTENTIONALLY suppressed to a noop (intent_noop:flip_suppressed_hold_policy) — zero trades placed is the CORRECT, designed outcome, not a dispatch failure. On 2026-05-31 16:15 the squeeze short signal (id 1951 bybit_1 / 1952 bybit_2) was correctly held (open long present), yet the operator got a 🚨 ALL-accounts-failed alert. The roll-up should treat a round where every account result is an intent_noop (at_target / flip_suppressed_hold_policy / hold) as a benign no-op, NOT a failure — only genuine errors (RiskBreach, exchange_rejected, missing creds) should trip the 🚨 alarm. Fix in coordinator.py:1617 guard (exclude intent_noop reasons) + possibly execution_diagnostics.enqueue_all_accounts_failed_dispatch. Tier-2 (live-path file) — propose via PR.
items.trigger_condition: Any /health-review run; or operator alarm-fatigue on flip-suppressed signals.
items.resolution_criteria: A dispatch round in which every account result is an intent_noop (at_target / flip_suppressed_hold_policy / flip-hold) no longer triggers the '🚨 ALL accounts failed to dispatch' high-priority ping; genuine all-fail rounds (risk/exchange/creds errors) still do. Verified against a flip-suppressed round.
items.resolution: Fixed by PR #2476 (merged Tier-2, 2026-05-31, per S-PROFIT-GAPS-2026-05-31): the '🚨 ALL accounts failed to dispatch' roll-up 'no longer fires when every account is a benign intent_noop (flip-suppressed/at-target).' LIVE-CONFIRMED this review: the trades pull (issue #2594) shows 30 consecutive vwap rows status=rejected reason=intent_noop:flip_suppressed_hold_policy across bybit_1+bybit_2 every ~8 min (2026-06-01 14:34→16:32Z) — exactly the trigger condition — and no '🚨 ALL accounts failed' alert accompanies them (the suppression is benign + silent as designed). Fix shipped + behaviour verified against live flip-suppressed rounds. Closing.

items.source: live verification during the trader-bot rebuild session (2026-05-25)
items.title: ib_paper account has no ib_port — MES order client cannot reach IB Gateway
items.description: The live trader logs `ib_client_for(ib_paper): no ib_port set (config or IB_PORT env) — cannot reach IB Gateway.` on every tick that routes to ib_paper. IB *market data* works (the gateway is up after the 2026-05-25 restart and MES candle fetches succeed via IBMarketData on 127.0.0.1:4002), so MES ticks complete fast and the heartbeat is stable. But the ib_paper account's ORDER client (src/units/accounts/clients.py::ib_client_for) has no ib_port configured, so it cannot place orders on MES — the account effectively collects signals/data but can't execute. If MES is intended to trade (paper), config/accounts.yaml::ib_paper needs `ib_port: 4002` (or the IB_PORT env on the trader unit). Pre-existing (predates this session); surfaced during post-deploy verification. Operator was informed in chat 2026-05-25 and chose to log it rather than fix immediately.
items.trigger_condition: Any /health-review run; surface to the operator (do NOT auto-edit accounts.yaml).
items.resolution_criteria: Operator decides whether MES should place live (paper) orders. If yes, set ib_port (4002) on the ib_paper account in config/accounts.yaml (Tier-3 change, operator-approved) and confirm the trader log no longer emits 'no ib_port set' + a smoke order reaches IB Gateway. If MES is data-only by design, document that and close.
items.triage_note: 2026-05-27 /health-review: still open. Status snapshot shows ib_paper live=true on sha 6a7aef4; no journalctl pull this review (relay queue wedged after 13 min — 3 of 6 issues never returned). The order-client gap (separate from IB market-data which works) would still emit the per-tick warning. Remains operator-gated (Tier-3); no change.
items.resolution: PR #2167 (squashed as 288d9bf) forwarded ib_host/ib_port/ib_account/ib_client_id through _load_account_cfgs_for_reconcile in src/runtime/order_monitor.py. The original diagnosis attributed the warning to the ORDER client (coordinator.py::multi_account_execute calling ib_client_for(account_cfg)), but that path's account_cfg already carries ib_port via getattr(account, 'ib_port', None) at coordinator.py:1045 — confirmed by reading the code. The actual emitter was the MONITOR RECONCILER path: _load_account_cfgs_for_reconcile() built an account_cfg dict that only forwarded a subset of fields and dropped the IB connection keys, so account_open_positions(cfg) → ib_read_client_for(account) → ib_client_for(...) → account.get('ib_port') = None → warning + return None. Fired from 4 call sites in order_monitor.py (1523, 1877, 2491, 3463), each on a 60s monitor tick — hence the per-tick spam. Post-deploy verification on diag-request #2169 confirmed: 'ib_client_for warning count: 0' across 300 lines / ~5 ticks; reconciler successfully opened the IB read socket (clientId 9731 visible at 17:57:38). MES order dispatch was never actually blocked — the data-collection-only framing in the original description was incorrect.

items.source: /health-review 2026-05-25 — root-causing the fade_breakout_4h silence
items.title: intent_multiplexer swallows a raising strategy builder with only logger.warning — no audit/diag event, so a broken strategy is invisible in the diag surface
items.description: src/runtime/intent_multiplexer.py::_collect_intents (the live path; pipeline uses multiplexed_intents) catches a builder exception with only `logger.warning('strategy X raised ... skipping this tick')` and continues. Unlike the LEGACY multiplexer (src/runtime/pipeline.py::multiplexed_signal_builder, ~L243-252) which emits a `report('strategy_builder','exception', strategy=...)` audit event, the intent layer emits NOTHING to signal_audit.jsonl / the diag relays. Consequence surfaced this review: fade_breakout_4h has been raising 'No numeric types to aggregate' every tick (origin: the ATR/ADX pd.concat([...],axis=1).max(axis=1) in src/units/strategies/fade_breakout_4h.py around L104/118) and producing ZERO eval events / zero shadow data — but because the catch is silent, there was no `*_eval`, no exception event, and no diag trace; it was only visible in journalctl. A failing strategy should be observable from the read-only diag surface, not just the systemd journal.
items.trigger_condition: Any /health-review run; or when a strategy is mysteriously silent in the audit tail.
items.resolution_criteria: intent_multiplexer._collect_intents emits a report()/audit event (e.g. 'strategy_builder'/'exception' with strategy + reason) on a builder exception, mirroring the legacy multiplexer, so a raising strategy shows up in signal_audit.jsonl and /api/diag/audit. (Code change — Tier-1 observability; not editable from /health-review which only writes docs + this backlog.) NOTE: this is the OBSERVABILITY gap only; the fade_breakout_4h crash itself is a separate Tier-3 strategy bug surfaced to the operator in the 2026-05-25 health-review response.
items.triage_note: 2026-05-28 /health-review: observability gap OBSERVED AGAIN. journalctl 04:50-04:54 shows the IB/MES outage making every MES strategy raise 'no candle data returned for MES — skipping this tick' as a bare logger.WARNING in intent_multiplexer; those MES skips emit NO *_eval and NO audit event, so MES strategy silence is invisible on the diag surface (only journalctl). Exactly the swallow this item flags. Remains operator-gated (Tier-1 code change, not editable from /health-review).

items.source: 2026-05-25 per-model status review (operator asked to fix the two degenerate shadow models)
items.title: MES regime models (mes-regime-5m/15m) collapse to one class (f1_volatile=0) — model/feature limitation, NOT data-blocked
items.description: mes-regime-5m-baseline-v1 + mes-regime-15m-baseline-v1 never predict 'volatile' (recall_volatile=precision_volatile=f1_volatile=0; acc ~0.49-0.53). IMPORTANTLY this is NOT the missing-MES-trade-data blocker: these train on MES market_features (price history), which IS present and BALANCED — 5m: 60957 range / 25916 volatile (~70/30); 15m: 17694/11278 (~61/39). So it will NOT self-resolve when IBKR is approved + MES starts trading. Root cause: RegimeClassifierTrainer is a per-bucket MODAL-class classifier (ml/trainers/regime_classifier.py) keyed on the backward-looking vol_bucket; on MES that backward vol_bucket does not separate the (forward-vol-based) regime_label, so every bucket is range-majority and argmax always picks 'range'. BTC works because BTC vol is autocorrelated; MES intraday isn't. Operator decision 2026-05-25: LEAVE manifests as-is for now (do NOT demote to research_only); revisit in a dedicated model-training session.
items.trigger_condition: Any /health-review run; revisit when a model-training session is scheduled, or once richer/live MES history is available.
items.resolution_criteria: mes-regime-{5m,15m} eval shows f1_volatile > 0 with a non-trivial macro_f1, via a feature/model change (e.g. more vol buckets, probability-threshold eval instead of argmax, a stronger/continuous vol feature, or a forward-vol-aware feature). Trainer-side (autonomous) work; verify on the trainer VM before/after. Distinct from the MES *trade*-data models (mes-execution-quality / mes-setup-quality / mes-trade-outcome-winrate) which ARE empty_dataset-blocked and self-resolve once MES trades.
items.triage_note: 2026-05-27 /health-review: not re-verified this run (model detail moved to /ml-review per 2026-05-26 split). Per scope, /health-review only checks trainer SERVICE state — service was 'failed' on last 2026-05-27 00:28 cycle (overall_rc=1 from BL-20260526-002's MES manifest failures, which include these two). Awaits model-training session.

updates.note: RESOLVED: 2026-06-15 registry shows MES regime v2 LGBM heads no longer collapse to one class — mes-regime-5m-lgbm-v2 macro_f1=0.674, mes-regime-15m-lgbm-v2=0.6225, mes-regime-1d-lgbm-v2=0.6462. The baseline-v1 heads stay ~0.38 (expected majority-class baselines). Resolution criteria (macro_f1 non-trivial via model change) met by the v2 family.

items.title: prop-mission-policy-baseline-v0 emits all-zero shadow scores — degenerate, prop-account-infra-blocked (NOT MES/IBKR-related)
items.description: The shadow log shows prop-mission-policy-baseline-v0 scoring 0.000 on every live signal. Root cause (ml/trainers/per_strategy_winrate.py + ml/datasets/families/account_context.py): it learns per-strategy mean was_taken acceptance rate from PROP-account trades only (accounts.yaml type:prop), but the only prop account (prop_velotrade_1) is mode:dry_run with strategies:[] and never trades — so there are no 'taken' positives, the learned rates / global_rate are 0, and live scoring of non-prop signals falls back to 0.0. NOTE: this is NOT resolved by tomorrow's IBKR/MES approval — it needs the prop account to actually trade (prop SDK wiring + a non-empty strategies list + mode:live = Tier-3/infra). Operator decision 2026-05-25: leave as-is for now.

It targets a leftover Cloudflare Pages project named 'ict-trader-widget' — a remnant of the retired React+Vercel/Cloudflare dashboard stack (PR #32 pivoted the dashboard to Streamlit; cf-worker/ was removed 2026-05-12 per CLAUDE.md). The repo no longer has any Pages build, so the Pages project has nothing to build and errors. The real CI (ruff-lint, import-smoke) is green. updates.note: TIER-2 PREP → OPERATOR-ONLY hand-off. The failing 'Cloudflare Pages' check on every ict-trader-dashboard PR is NOT a workflow file (the repo .github/workflows has only ci.yml + delete-merged-branches.yml) — it's an external GitHub<->Cloudflare-Pages App integration (a stale CF Pages project 'ict-trader-widget' still wired to the repo, left over from the retired React/CF stack). OPERATOR ACTION: in the Cloudflare dashboard → Workers & Pages → the stale 'ict-trader-widget' (or equivalent) Pages project → Settings → either delete the project or disconnect its GitHub integration; alternatively GitHub → repo Settings → Integrations/GitHub Apps → Cloudflare Pages → revoke repo access. items.source: operator flagged a live BTCUSDT trade whose TP (~143,210) sat ~2x the BTC price / above the all-time high
items.title: EXPECTED, NOT A BUG: trend_donchian (+ fade/squeeze) place a far ~50R 'unreachable sentinel' TP — don't re-flag it as a broken take-profit
items.description: A live trend_donchian BTCUSDT long showed TP=143,210.10 with SL=76,355.40 (entry ~77,666; current ~77,300). This is the DESIGNED 50R sentinel, not a malfunction. trend_donchian/fade_breakout_4h/squeeze_breakout_4h have NO fixed profit target (matches their backtests, which have no TP) — the Chandelier ATR trailing stop is the sole profit exit. But the pipeline's dispatch gate requires every order to carry BOTH an SL and a TP, so the TP is parked tp_r*risk away (tp_r=50.0 in config/strategies.yaml:153/216/259) where it can't realistically fill (max single-trade excursion in 3yr backtest ~11R). Signature to recognize it: reward:risk ~= 50:1. Math check on this trade: TP = entry + 50*(entry - SL) => 77,666.27 + 50*1,310.87 = 143,209.8 ~= 143,210.10. Formula lives at src/units/strategies/trend_donchian.py:190/195 (order_package) and the trade is protected by the live ratcheting trail in monitor() at trend_donchian.py:285 (moves SL up only, never loosens). The TP-cross close at trend_donchian.py:342 exists only for completeness. A far TP carries no margin/liquidation risk; if BTC ever printed 143k the trade would simply close at +50R. The only live strategy that emits this is trend_donchian (turtle_soup uses tp1_at_r 1.0 / tp2_at_r 3.0, ict_scalp tp_at_r 1.5, vwap a VWAP-anchored TP) — fade/squeeze share the 50R sentinel and (as of 2026-05-25) also run execution: live, so their 50R TPs now appear on real bybit_2 order packages too — still the intended sentinel (see BL-20260525-008).
items.trigger_condition: Any /health-review run that scores a trend_donchian / fade_breakout_4h / squeeze_breakout_4h order package and sees a TP far from price (~50R / ~2x BTC). Recognize it as the intended sentinel — do NOT score it as a TP defect.
items.resolution_criteria: Informational standing note; nothing to fix. Close only if the 50R-sentinel mechanism is itself removed (e.g. the pipeline stops requiring a TP for trail-only strategies, or tp_r is changed in an operator-approved Tier-3 PR).
items.triage_note: 2026-05-28 /health-review: a SECOND consequence of the short clamp surfaced — 10 trend_donchian SHORT orders were exchange_rejected on bybit_1+bybit_2 (trades 1771-1781, 2026-05-27 13:28-13:54) with Bybit ErrCode 10001 'TakeProfit < 10_pcnt of base': the clamped entry*0.01 short-TP sits ~1% of price, far inside Bybit's required ~10% TP distance. PR #2156 (fe3d46a, 'cap 50R sentinel TP within Bybit's ~10% rule') fixed this; the running sha 771c843 includes it and NO exchange_rejected has occurred since (cluster stopped after 13:54, before the 04:55 reboot). Still the standing 'don't re-flag the 50R sentinel as a defect' note; the Bybit-rule collision is the resolved part.

items.source: PR #1995 (merged 2a951719, deployed 2026-05-25) — fade/squeeze promoted shadow->live + symmetric bybit routing
items.title: VERIFY fade_breakout_4h + squeeze_breakout_4h are trading CORRECTLY on bybit_2 (real money) after the 2026-05-25 shadow->live promotion
items.description: Operator-approved change (PR #1995): both bybit accounts now run the same six strategies, and fade_breakout_4h + squeeze_breakout_4h were promoted execution: shadow -> live. They now place REAL orders on bybit_2 (and paper on bybit_1 demo). Deployed + trader restarted 2026-05-25 14:12 UTC at HEAD 2a951719; clean boot confirmed (both are evaluated each tick).
