items.source: Investigating the trend_donchian stuck-watchdog alert (trade 1722 / pkg-ac6bb4b3b33f4339); fixed the alert symptom in PR #2002
items.title: Zero-sized same-direction reinforcement signals persist as open order_packages (clutter + watchdog churn)
items.description: trend_donchian (and any breakout strategy) re-fires a same-direction signal on each new bar while the breakout persists. The intent multiplexer correctly refuses to pyramid onto an already-open position — it sizes the reinforcement to target_qty=0.0 (aggregation_reason 'same_direction_reinforcement: ... max target_qty=0.0', sized_qty_by_account={}) — but the zero-qty package is STILL persisted as status='open' with linked_trade_id set, even though no exchange order was placed. These ghost packages never close on their own; their updated_at goes stale and they accumulate in order_packages, and they are what trips the stuck-strategy watchdog (the live snapshot for trade 1722 showed pkg-ac6bb4b3b33f4339 and earlier ones already watchdog_released_alive). PR #2002 made the watchdog timeframe-aware so it no longer false-alerts on these, but the underlying clutter remains. Root fix: a reinforcement that sizes to 0 (no order to monitor) should NOT create a lingering open order_packages row — either skip persisting it, persist it with a terminal status (e.g. status='skipped'/'no_order' + close_reason), or auto-close it so it's never gated/monitored/watchdog'd. Verify the strategy_monocle gate (pipeline.py::_has_open_package_for_strategy) and the per-strategy gates still behave correctly afterward.
items.trigger_condition: Any /health-review run; or whenever order_packages shows multiple open same-direction zero-qty packages for one live position. Investigate the intent_multiplexer / order-package persistence path.
items.resolution_criteria: A same-direction reinforcement that aggregates to target_qty=0 no longer leaves a lingering status='open' order_packages row (it is skipped or persisted terminal), so ghost packages stop accumulating and the stuck-watchdog has nothing benign to flag. Order-path change (Tier-2): open a draft PR + operator review.
items.triage_note: 2026-05-28 /health-review: STILL HAPPENING — now 19/19 in-window packages (2026-05-28 04:04-06:04) carry aggregated_target_qty=0.0 + empty sized_qty_by_account, linked_trade_id=null, and ALL ended status=orphaned by the 5min reconciler (100%). Both edge cases present: same_direction_reinforcement AND conflict_resolved_by_priority (vwap priority=40 long beats trend_donchian short, then aggregates to 0). journalctl 06:00 confirms the downstream effect: strategy_monocle skips dispatch ('strategy=vwap already has open package pkg-5d6aa3051b364cdc') because the prior ghost is still open — self-perpetuating churn until the 5min orphan. Code change still pending — Tier-2.
items.resolution: Resolved by the dry/shadow orphan-labeling fix (PR #4339, #4342). A zero-qty same-direction-reinforcement dispatch now writes a journaled rejection row in execute_pkg's is_dry branch; _sweep_unlinked_packages path (1) relabels the package 'rejected' instead of orphaning it. VERIFIED LIVE 2026-06-23: pkg-7d5d8b394bc04502 (tlt_pullback_1h, aggregation_reason 'same_direction_reinforcement ... max target_qty=0.0', sized_qty_by_account={}) now status='rejected', rejected_by=monitor_reconciler — not orphaned.

items.source: session wrap-up — PR #2062 explicitly scoped MES manifests out; they still fail in every training cycle.
items.title: MES regime + setup-quality manifests fail every trainer cycle — need yfinance/ES=F intraday market_raw on the trainer + non-empty MES setup_labels shard at v002
items.description: PR #2062's build_btcusdt_pair helper fixed the BTCUSDT 5m/15m v002 shards but explicitly deferred the MES failures. Three manifests fail every cycle with FileNotFoundError at: datasets-out/market_features/MES/5m/v002/data.jsonl (mes-regime-5m.yaml), datasets-out/market_features/MES/15m/v002/data.jsonl (mes-regime-15m.yaml), and datasets-out/setup_labels/MES/all/v002/data.jsonl (mes-setup-quality.yaml). Root causes: (a) scripts/ops/build_trainer_datasets.sh only builds market_raw/features for BTCUSDT (Bybit V5 offvm adapter); MES needs the yfinance/ES=F intraday adapter — that wiring exists in the separate scripts/ops/run_mes_training.sh one-shot but isn't part of the daily cycle. (b) The cycle's setup_labels build is symbol-scope=all (not MES); a separate `build_family setup_labels --symbol-scope MES` invocation at v002 is needed (will produce an empty shard until MES trades, but an empty shard at least lets the manifest skip cleanly via empty_dataset instead of failing). Net effect: trainer cycle exits with overall_rc=1 every run even though all BTC manifests succeed.
items.trigger_condition: Any /health-review run; or any session attempting to train MES regime / setup-quality models.
items.resolution_criteria: Either (a) build_trainer_datasets.sh extended to invoke a build_market_pair-like helper for MES using the yfinance/ES=F adapter for 5m + 15m plus a MES-scoped setup_labels build, so the three failing manifests either train (where data is present) or empty_dataset-skip (where it isn't); OR (b) the three failing manifests are explicitly scoped out of the daily cycle (e.g. via a TRAINING_MANIFESTS-exclude wrapper) so overall_rc returns 0. Cycle log on next run shows zero manifest_failed events. Pure trainer-VM tooling change; autonomous (Tier-1).

Timer enabled+active, fired on schedule, next fire Fri 2026-05-29 00:17. Service-health graded WATCH this review; manifest detail belongs to /ml-review.

items.source: /health-review 2026-05-27 — three of six diag-relay issues opened back-to-back never returned a comment
items.title: vm-diag-snapshot relay: subset of issues opened in quick succession never comment+close (stuck OPEN; 38 stale opens accumulated since 2026-05-17)
items.description: This review opened six diag issues at 2026-05-27T07:19:28..54Z. Three returned comments within 30s and auto-closed normally (#2125 status, #2128 order_packages, #2130 trainer-vm). Three never returned a comment in 16+ min and stayed OPEN (#2126 services, #2127 audit, #2129 trades). Cross-check: there are now 38 open vm-diag-request issues going back to #1327 (2026-05-17) — a chronic accumulation. Hypothesis: when issues open within seconds of each other, the concurrency-group serializer (`group: vm-diag-snapshot, cancel-in-progress: false`) starts them but a subset of runs fail/timeout SSH or curl in a way that the post-step `if: cancelled()` / `if: failure()` comment-back step doesn't fire — so the issue is left open with no comment. The 2026-05-16 commentary in vm-diag-snapshot.yml already flagged this exact failure mode (#1278/#1281/#1284). Effect: /health-review can't reliably pull a full state set in one fire; chronic open-issue clutter on the repo. Code-level fix needed in .github/workflows/vm-diag-snapshot.yml: add an always() post-step that drops a 'workflow ran but produced no output / failed at step X' comment so the issue stops looking 'in flight'.
items.trigger_condition: Any /health-review run; or any session that opens >2 diag issues in quick succession and notices subsequent ones never return.
items.resolution_criteria: vm-diag-snapshot.yml carries an `if: always()` (or `if: cancelled()` + `if: failure()`) closing-comment step that ALWAYS posts a result-or-error comment + closes the issue, so no diag issue can be left silently OPEN. Then sweep the 38 stale open issues — close as 'workflow run silently failed; re-open if you need the data'. Code change (Tier-1 observability); not editable from /health-review.
items.resolution: ROOT-CAUSE refined 2026-05-27: vm-diag-snapshot.yml ALREADY carries `if: cancelled()` + `if: failure()` + an implicit-success handler that together cover every terminal job state. The remaining gap is when GitHub's webhook layer batches rapidly-filed `issues.opened` events and silently drops some — the workflow never starts, so none of the handlers fire. There's no in-workflow fix for that. Instead, a SEPARATE sweeper workflow `.github/workflows/diag-relay-sweep.yml` was shipped (PR #2144, merged 2026-05-27 ~09:50Z): daily cron at 06:00 UTC + on-demand workflow_dispatch; finds open `vm-diag-request` / `trainer-vm-diag-request` issues >30 min old with no `github-actions[bot]` reply, comments + closes them. VERIFIED 2026-05-28: the first 06:00 UTC cron ran and cleared the entire stale backlog — `list_issues(label=vm-diag-request, state=OPEN)` returned 0 (the ~38 issues going back to #1327 are all closed). The webhook-drop wedge still recurs (this review's #2173/#2174/#2175 never returned and were closed manually), but that is now an accepted condition: the sweeper cleans up any silently-dropped issue within ≤24h, so no diag-request stays OPEN indefinitely. Mitigation confirmed working end-to-end.
items.triage_note: 2026-05-28 /health-review: sweeper verified — 0 open vm-diag-request issues remain after the 06:00 cron. Re-wedge of 3 rapid-fired pulls observed but auto-cleanup confirmed. Marked resolved. | 2026-06-01 /health-review: webhook batch-drop RECURRED at high rate — opened 6 issues at 16:57:01Z (#2585-2590); only #2585 (status, first of the burst) and #2590 (trainer, separate workflow) returned + closed; #2586/2587/2588/2589 (services/audit/order_packages/trades) were silently dropped (stayed OPEN, no run). Re-fired one-at-a-time spaced ~70s apart (#2591/2592/2593/2594) — all four then returned cleanly. Confirms the accepted-condition framing: prevention isn't fixed, but spaced single fires work and the diag-relay-sweep cron will close the 4 stale opens within ≤24h. Operational note for future reviews: fire diag pulls SEQUENTIALLY, not in a burst. Staying resolved (mitigation holds).

items.source: session wrap-up — `python -m ml compare setup-quality-baseline-v0 setup-quality-lgbm-v2` after PR #2053 + #2067 cycles.
items.title: setup-quality-lgbm-v2 underperforms v0 baseline on n=76 closed trades — too little data for LightGBM
items.description: setup-quality-lgbm-v2 trained cleanly (PR #2053's LightGBM stack works end-to-end) but loses to setup-quality-baseline-v0 on the eval split: MAE 0.0710 vs 0.0478, MSE 0.0114 vs 0.0092 (n_eval=76). Cause is dataset size, not model quality — 76 closed trades is too small for a gradient-boosted tree to beat a simple per-group-mean baseline, even with the conservative regularization PR #2053 added (min_data_in_leaf=10, num_leaves=15, lambda_l2=1.0). Promoting this model would mean shipping noise instead of signal. The model is auto-wired into the shadow factory by the 2026-05-19 default-flip rule, so it's currently logging shadow predictions on every live signal — that's fine and produces useful data, but the model itself shouldn't be considered for promotion past shadow until either it beats baseline at higher n, or the manifest is explicitly demoted/deprecated. Distinct from BL-20260526-001 (BTC regime models which DO beat their baselines on macro_f1 + the imbalance-aware path).
items.trigger_condition: Any /health-review run; revisit specifically when n_trades >= ~1000 closed trades suitable for setup_labels OR in a dedicated model-training session reviewing the v2 setup-quality manifest.
items.resolution_criteria: Either (a) on a fresh training cycle with n_eval >= 1000 the v2 model beats the baseline on MAE+MSE, in which case keep at shadow and let it accumulate live shadow predictions; OR (b) the model is demoted from shadow to research_only via `python -m ml promote-stage setup-quality-lgbm-v2 --new-stage research_only` (operator-approved, semantic change to the registry); OR (c) the manifest is deprecated (removed from ml/configs/, model retired). Decision belongs to a model-training session, not this backlog item.

items.title: config/strategies.yaml comments on tp_r=50.0 still say 'effectively-unreachable sentinel' without mentioning the 2026-05-27 short-branch clamp
items.description: PR #2141 (merged 2026-05-27 09:01Z) clamped the short-TP formula in trend_donchian/fade_breakout_4h/squeeze_breakout_4h to `max(entry*0.01, entry - tp_r*risk)` so the pre-flight tp>0 guard accepts the order at BTC prices where unclamped 50R would go negative. The comments in config/strategies.yaml around lines 149-153 (trend_donchian), 218 (fade_breakout_4h), and 265-267 (squeeze_breakout_4h) still describe the sentinel as the unclamped formula. Not a contradiction (the clamped 1%-of-entry floor IS still effectively unreachable, so the comments' INTENT is correct), but the wording could be refreshed for accuracy. config/strategies.yaml is Tier-3, so this skill cannot edit it — surface to the operator. Minor.

items.source: Today's IB Gateway wedge — Up 21h, upstream IB session dead.
items.title: IBC nightly auto-restart doesn't reliably recover the upstream IB session
items.description: IBC's `Auto restart time` is configured to 11:59 PM on the ib-gateway container (verified in the IBC log on issue #2165 post-recovery: `Auto restart time already set to 11:59 PM`). Yet today the container was 'Up 21 hours' with its upstream IB session entirely dead (Warning 2103 usfarm / 2105 ushmds / 2157 secdefnj all 'broken'; every API call timed out at 10s; 200/200 log lines were `remove Client <id>` with no upstream activity). Either IBC's nightly restart didn't fire, or it fired and didn't successfully re-authenticate to IB's data farms. The autonomous `vm-ib-gateway-recover` workflow is the current mitigation (Claude can dispatch it whenever the symptom recurs), but the root cause needs investigating. Candidates: 2FA timing on the nightly relogin, stale Java/Gateway state on Auto-restart that needs a hard container restart, or IB-side throttling on rapid reconnects. Probable scope: inspect IBC config (jts.ini, ibc.ini) + the docker entrypoint + gateway startup logs around the 11:59 PM window over multiple days to see if the restart fires at all.
items.trigger_condition: Any /health-review run; investigate via gateway-logs + provision_ib_gateway.sh on the next 12-24h MES outage.
items.resolution_criteria: Either IBC's nightly auto-restart reliably re-establishes the upstream IB session (verifiable by 7 consecutive days with no `Up >20h with broken farm connections` state), or a sanctioned scheduled job replaces it (e.g. systemd timer triggering vm-ib-gateway-recover at 11:55 PM UTC, before IB's reset window).
items.resolution: ROOT CAUSE REFINED 2026-05-28 (corrects the original 2FA/nightly-restart hypotheses). gateway-logs (issue #2184) showed the container 'Up 13h' but logged OUT of IBKR since `2026-05-28 04:30:50 IBC: detected dialog entitled: Unrecognized Username or Password` — i.e. during IBKR's overnight server-reset window the Gateway's in-place re-login hit a TRANSIENT bad-credential dialog and IBC parked on it, never retrying. NOT 2FA (the paper account DUQ325724 logs in with no 2FA — confirmed by the #2185 recover log: 'Click button: Paper Log In' → 'Login has completed', no Mobile prompt). NOT a real bad password (a full container restart re-logins cleanly — that's why vm-ib-gateway-recover works). NOT `restart: unless-stopped` (the process hangs on the dialog, it never dies, so Docker never restarts it). The gap: nothing auto-recovers a stuck login-failure dialog (`TWOFA_TIMEOUT_ACTION=restart` only covers 2FA timeouts, which never fire for paper). FIX (operator-approved 2026-05-28, branch claude/health-check-CjAWV, draft PR #2183): a symptom-driven auto-heal watchdog — scripts/check_ib_gateway.py + deploy/ict-ib-gateway-watchdog.{service,timer}, firing every 5 min: probes ib_paper via ib_connect_check (healthy ⇔ connected AND net_liquidation populated — a logged-out gateway reports connected=true but net_liquidation=None), and after 2 consecutive wedged checks runs scripts/ops/restart_ib_gateway.sh, with max-restarts=3 + 20-min cooldown so a genuine bad-cred/lockout can never become a restart loop (then it alert-only escalates). The fixed daily scheduled-restart option was considered and DROPPED as non-structural per operator — the watchdog self-heals any wedge within ~5-10 min, fine for delayed paper data. REMAINING: operator merge + pull-and-deploy, then verify the watchdog auto-heals across a few overnight IBKR resets (target: no manual vm-ib-gateway-recover needed).
items.triage_note: 2026-05-28 working session: RECURRED + diagnosed + fix built. gateway-logs caught the 04:30:50Z 'Unrecognized Username or Password' dialog (logged-out 2h+); restored live via vm-ib-gateway-recover #2185 (clean paper login, no 2FA). Auto-heal watchdog shipped to draft PR #2183. 2026-05-28 (S-MES-AUTOHEAL): MERGED (#2183 -> main 6972a9f) + DEPLOYED (4d4d0c3 via pull-and-deploy #2196); watchdog confirmed LIVE — timer active + healthy probe (probe: healthy=True net_liquidation=1000600.6 action=none) in the #2196 post-restart systemd dump. The HEALTHY path is proven; the RESTART path on a real wedge is not yet observed in prod — tracked for next-health-review verification as BL-20260528-001.

items.source: Post-deploy journal during PR #2167 ship — first signal emitted after deploy triggered FCM push attempts that all 404'd.
items.title: FCM mobile-push notifier hits Firebase with stale / unregistered device tokens (404 NOT_FOUND)
items.description: When the trader emitted a fresh BTCUSDT VWAP signal at 2026-05-27T17:57:30Z, the M12 mobile-push notifier (src/runtime/mobile_push/notifier.py) fired FCM push attempts that came back `status=404 body={"error":{"code":404, "message":"Requested entity was not found.","status":"NOT_FOUND","details":[...FcmEr...]}}` — Google saying the device registration tokens FCM is targeting don't exist. Visible in the post-deploy journalctl on issue #2168. Means notifications aren't reaching the Android app. Either the dashboard/Android app never registered tokens after FCM init, or the tokens stored in the bot's token store are stale (device uninstall / FCM revocation / token rotation). Notifier should be marking these tokens as dead on 404 and stop retrying them; investigate whether notifier.py implements the FCM `UNREGISTERED` cleanup (remove-on-404) or silently retries. Pre-existing (the FCM key leak earlier today is unrelated — that was about the credential shape in .env, not the device tokens).
items.trigger_condition: Any /health-review run; tractable as a code-only investigation (no live-trading risk).
items.resolution_criteria: Either (a) confirm + ship a token-cleanup-on-404 path in notifier.py so dead tokens are pruned automatically, or (b) confirm the cleanup already exists and the token store has been drained, and document the operator path to re-register the Android app's FCM token. The 404 cluster should stop appearing in the journal after a fresh signal emission.
items.triage_note: 2026-05-28 /health-review: CONFIRMED STILL OCCURRING. journalctl shows a flood of 'FCM publish non-2xx: status=404 ... NOT_FOUND' on every signal emission — ~12+ per emit at 04:55:36-38 (post-boot fade signal) and again at 06:00:35-36 (vwap signal_emitted). Dead/unregistered device tokens are not being pruned on 404; the notifier keeps targeting them each emit. No token-cleanup-on-404 path is taking effect. Tier-1 code investigation in src/runtime/mobile_push/notifier.py — not editable from /health-review.

check_log.note: 2026-05-30 (/health-review, operator-confirmed): push DELIVERY IS WORKING - operator confirms notifications are landing on the phone. So the FCM 404 NOT_FOUND lines (still seen in ict-trader-live journalctl, e.g. ~23:50Z 2026-05-30) are LOG-NOISE from a stale/secondary unregistered token, NOT a delivery outage. Severity downgraded: remaining work is just pruning 404'd tokens from the registry so the notifier stops logging WARN+error bodies on every cycle. Not a user-facing problem.

items.source: First VWAP signal after PR #2167 deploy — visible in post-deploy journalctl on issue #2168.
items.title: VWAP signal at 2026-05-27T17:57:35Z sized to target_qty=0.0
items.description: Brand-new BTCUSDT buy signal emitted at 17:57:35Z (entry 74790.9, deviation -1.36σ below VWAP, regime=weak-down/medium, policy_allow=True). Aggregated with fade_breakout_4h in same-direction reinforcement (`aggregation_reason: same_direction_reinforcement: 2 intents agree on long; max target_qty=0.0 from strategy=vwap`). But `target_qty=0.0` from the intent layer means the aggregated package size is zero — coordinator would either skip dispatch or refuse via the zero-qty gate. Could be a transient balance/risk-cap condition (daily_loss_budget exhausted, available USDT below min_balance, sizing math underflow), or a config drift. One observation, not yet a pattern. Next /performance-review window should grade the post-deploy VWAP cohort and check whether target_qty stays at zero (= effective shadow even though execution=live) or recovers.
items.trigger_condition: Next /performance-review window after at least 3 more BTCUSDT VWAP signals fire.
items.resolution_criteria: Either confirm target_qty>0 on the subsequent VWAP signals (transient single-tick artifact, close), or trace the sizing-to-zero root cause (which strategy/account/RiskManager refused, with the structured reason token from _explain_zero_sized_qty).
items.triage_note: 2026-05-28 /health-review: NOT transient — sustained on every in-window vwap signal. Two distinct contributors now visible: (1) vwap is execution:shadow, so the intent layer emits target_qty=0.0 BY DESIGN (journalctl 06:00: "vwap emitted intent side=long target_qty=0.000000 priority=40"); (2) on bybit_1 (demo) the trades table shows status=rejected risk_refused sized_qty=0 'check daily-loss budget...' — bybit_1 closed -108 on 2026-05-28 (trade 1784) which exceeds its daily_usd=100 cap, so RiskManager is correctly refusing further trades for the UTC day (Prime-Directive per-trade refusal, not a bug). Net effect: no live vwap order, as expected. Full per-account confirmation belongs to /performance-review.
items.resolution: Resolved by the same orphan-labeling fix as BL-20260525-009 (PR #4339, #4342) — a target_qty=0.0 reinforcement package now lands a 'rejected' journal row and is relabeled 'rejected', not stranded open/orphaned. Verified live (see BL-20260525-009).

items.source: Today's FCM-credential bleed incident (issue #2157).
items.title: Operator-only follow-ups from the FCM private-key bleed
items.description:
The 2026-05-27 systemd-EnvironmentFile bleed dumped the FCM service-account PEM private key into the journalctl tail on TWO pull-and-deploy issue comments. PR #2158 shipped the scrub-env-noncompliant system-action and it ran successfully (12 lines stripped, journal warning is now gone — verified on issue #2161). What's left is operator-only because the MCP tools I have can neither delete issue comments (no delete_issue_comment) nor vacuum the journal (no journalctl vacuum system-action) nor rotate the FCM key (that's Google Cloud Console). Three steps:
  1. Delete the comments containing the leaked key (one-click `...` → Delete on each):
     - https://github.com/benbaichmankass/ict-trading-bot/issues/2157#issuecomment-4555473441
     - https://github.com/benbaichmankass/ict-trading-bot/issues/2159#issuecomment-4555811974
  2. Rotate the FCM service-account key at Google Cloud Console (only step that actually neutralizes the exposure — the journal copies on the VM, webhook archives, and any third-party GitHub mirror still hold the old key). After rotation, re-dispatch `set-mobile-push-secrets` with the new JSON in the FCM_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_JSON secret to update the on-disk file at ${DATA_DIR}/fcm_service_account.json.
  3. Optional: `sudo journalctl --rotate && sudo journalctl --vacuum-time=1s` on the live VM to wipe historical journal copies containing the key. Reduces forensic surface; the scrub already stops future bleeds at source.
items.trigger_condition: Operator-only — no autonomous action possible. Surface every /health-review run until resolved.
items.resolution_criteria: Both leaked issue comments deleted (404 on the comment URLs); FCM service-account key rotated at Google Cloud Console; ${DATA_DIR}/fcm_service_account.json updated to the new credential and a fresh push test succeeds (kicks the notifier and confirms no 404 NOT_FOUND).

items.source: Operator request at session close — confirm the deployed IB Gateway watchdog actually heals a real wedge, not just that it probes healthy.
items.title: Verify the ict-ib-gateway-watchdog RESTART path fires end-to-end on a real overnight IBKR-reset wedge
items.description: The auto-heal watchdog (the BL-20260527-003 fix, PR #2183) went live on the trader VM on 2026-05-28 (main 6972a9f, deployed as 4d4d0c3 via pull-and-deploy #2196). Its HEALTHY path is confirmed — the #2196 post-restart systemd dump shows `probe: healthy=True reason=net_liquidation=1000600.6 action=none` and the timer active (TriggeredBy). What is NOT yet observed in production is the RESTART path: that on a genuine wedge (a logged-out Gateway reports `connected=true` but `net_liquidation=None`) the watchdog actually runs `scripts/ops/restart_ib_gateway.sh` and brings `ib_paper`/MES back with no human in the loop. The natural test is IBKR's overnight server-reset window (~04:30 UTC), the same window that produced the 2026-05-28 wedge. The watchdog journal is now readable from the diag surface — `ict-ib-gateway-watchdog.{service,timer}` was added to diag.py `_CANONICAL_UNITS` in #2192, so `/api/diag/journalctl?unit=ict-ib-gateway-watchdog.service` works.
items.trigger_condition: Any /health-review run on or after 2026-05-29: pull `/api/diag/journalctl?unit=ict-ib-gateway-watchdog.service` covering the overnight window (use ?since=/?until= for the ~04:00-05:30 UTC reset) and read the per-fire `probe:` lines.
items.resolution_criteria: A /health-review confirms in the watchdog journal at least one real wedge that the watchdog auto-recovered: a probe `healthy=False` (net_liquidation=None) followed within ~2 checks by a logged `action=restart`, then a subsequent probe `healthy=True` — with NO manual vm-ib-gateway-recover dispatch in that window. Confirm across at least one (ideally a couple) overnight resets, then mark BL-20260527-003 fully resolved. If MES instead stayed healthy through the reset (no wedge to heal), record that and keep watching — absence of a wedge is not yet proof the restart path works. If guard rails tripped (`max-restarts 3` exhausted -> alert-only), surface to the operator: that means a genuine lockout the watchdog can't fix.
items.triage_note: 2026-05-29 /health-review: HEALTHY PATH re-confirmed across the post-reset window — journalctl?unit=ict-ib-gateway-watchdog.service (issue #2291) shows the timer firing every ~5 min from 05:41Z through 09:42Z, EVERY probe `healthy=True net_liquidation=1000686.4 action=none`. No wedge occurred at this overnight IBKR reset, so the RESTART path is STILL unexercised in production (no action=restart line). Per resolution criteria, absence of a wedge is not proof — keep open and keep watching. (net_liquidation drifted 1000600.6->1000686.4 vs the 2026-05-28 dump; MES barely trading.)
items.resolution: Closed as SUPERSEDED, not verified: the ict-ib-gateway-watchdog reactive restart path this item wanted exercised end-to-end was retired by the 2026-06-10 gateway-isolation redesign (daily deterministic docker restart on the dedicated gateway VM + alert-only health check replace it). The restart-path-fires-on-real-wedge verification is no longer a meaningful target; the daily-reset behaviour is covered by BL-20260610-003's remaining check.

items.source: S-ANDROID-S9 session (diag pulls while debugging the Android app)
items.title: vm-diag-snapshot relay rejects multi-param diag paths (the & query separator)
items.description: Issue-driven diag requests whose title carries a & failed this session (#2251 `journal?table=order_packages&limit=40`, #2252 `journal?table=trades&limit=40`) while single-param paths (`journal?table=order_packages`, `db_info`) and the broad `snapshot?limit=200` succeeded. Suspected cause: GitHub stores the title's & as the HTML entity &; the workflow decodes it (${raw//&/&}) but the path-validation regex ^[A-Za-z0-9/?&=_.:%-]+$ in .github/workflows/vm-diag-snapshot.yml rejects the ; if any entity slips through un-decoded, so the run exits 1 with 'Rejected diag_path'. Workaround in use: single-param paths or the broad snapshot. Low priority — the relay works for the common cases.
items.trigger_condition: Any /health-review run, or the next time a session needs a multi-param diag pull.
items.resolution_criteria: A multi-param diag request (e.g. journal?table=trades&limit=40) completes and posts JSON back, OR the workflow title decode/validation is fixed (fully decode & before the regex, or URL-decode) and verified with a multi-param pull.
items.resolution: VERIFIED FIXED EMPIRICALLY 2026-05-29: a fresh multi-param pull `journal?table=trades&limit=5` (issue #2315) returned JSON (5 rows) and auto-closed — the resolution criteria's first clause is met. vm-diag-snapshot.yml already carries the `${raw//&/&}` entity-decode (lines ~123) and the path-validation regex already allows `&` (`^[A-Za-z0-9/?&=_.:%-]+$`), so the multi-param path now decodes and validates correctly. The prior-session failures (#2251/#2252) were not reproduced; no code change required. NOTE: the EARLIER multi-param failures observed in THIS session's first half (#2293/#2294 etc.) were the ict-web-api deploy-bounce outage (~09:45-09:52Z), not the & decode — single-param paths failed in that same window too.

items.title: ict-trader-live floods 'FCM publish non-2xx: status=404 NOT_FOUND' from mobile_push.notifier

NOT YET OBSERVED: an actual post-deploy dispatch on an in-scope account with sized_qty>0. REGRESSION (do NOT close — escalate to operator immediately): a NEW post-deploy 'risk_refused: sized_qty=0' rejection appears on an in-scope account while that account's realized daily loss is small relative to 5% of its equity, meaning the percentage cap did not take effect.
items.carry_forward_instructions: This item MUST stay open and be re-checked on EVERY subsequent /health-review until the resolution criteria are met — even if the next several reviews land in a quiet market. If a review runs with no qualifying dispatch, DO NOT close it: append a dated entry to check_log recording (a) heartbeat label + last_tick (liveness), (b) newest trade-row id + timestamp, (c) whether any in-scope dispatch occurred since the deploy, and leave status=open so the NEXT review continues the watch. Only the review that actually observes a qualifying dispatch (or a regression) changes the status.

check_log.note: Liveness OK (git_sha b149b15, last_tick 00:53:27Z). No in-scope dispatch since deploy; newest trade id=1866 @ 2026-05-28 22:25:01. Quiet market.
Liveness OK (heartbeat 'running' age 46s, last_tick 08:32:38Z, uptime ~9h, no unexpected restart). Still no in-scope dispatch; newest trade id=1866. ~10h no-signal lull. Pending live confirmation.

Liveness OK (status #2302: heartbeat 'running' age 3s, last_tick 09:52:07Z, git_sha 22e2cf0 after a git-sync deploy bounce, bybit_1/bybit_2/ib_paper live). Trades table READ (#2304): newest row still id=1866 @ 2026-05-28 22:25:01 — PRE-deploy, no in-scope trade row created after the 2026-05-28T23:29:36Z deploy. All recent rows (1845-1866) are the pre-deploy rejected/sz=0/is_demo=0 BTCUSDT-short cluster. order_packages (#2307) also static since 22:25:01 (vwap BTCUSDT short, all orphaned/closed). audit (#2308): all 6 strategies emit *_eval every tick, all regime-gated non-actionable — genuine no-signal lull (~11.5h), NOT a pipeline break. RESOLVED. Trades pull (#2384) shows a QUALIFYING post-deploy dispatch: trade id=1867 account_id=bybit_1 (in-scope) created 2026-05-29T15:12:15Z — status=open, position_size=2.173 (>0), is_dry=false, ict_scalp_5m, entry 73704.1 / SL 72442.4 / TP 75596.7. The sibling row id=1868 (bybit_2, is_demo=0, same pkg-5dc66cf92b5e407f) is status=rejected sz=0 but the reason is intent_noop:at_target (already holds 0.001 BTC matching target) — a benign no-op, NOT a daily-loss-cap refusal, so NOT a regression. items.resolution: Observed the qualifying post-deploy live dispatch: bybit_1 trade id=1867 (2026-05-29T15:12:15Z) sized position_size=2.173 >0, status=open, is_dry=false — the %-based daily-loss cap (daily_loss_pct=0.05, PR #2265 main b149b15) no longer zeroes bybit_1 sizing on a $274k balance. No regression on the sibling bybit_2 leg (intent_noop:at_target, not a cap refusal). The recurring watch is satisfied.

items.source: /health-review 2026-05-29 — a docs-only commit deploy bounced the live trader + web-api mid-review, and the (unnecessary) recover dispatches were all webhook-dropped.
items.title: git-sync deploy restarts the LIVE TRADER for docs-only commits (brief trading + read-surface interruption); web-api has no VM-side self-heal and the GitHub recover dispatch is webhook-flaky
items.description: OBSERVED this review: between status #2286 (09:44:05Z, uptime 36577s, git_sha b149b15) and status #2302 (09:52:15Z, uptime 405s, git_sha 22e2cf0) the live trader RESTARTED and HEAD advanced b149b15 -> 22e2cf0. The only commit in that range is 22e2cf0 (#2284, a DOCS-ONLY 'docs(health-review): recurring carry-forward item' change). So the ict-git-sync timer pulled the new commit and ran the full deploy_pull_restart path, which restarts ALL ict-* units (trader + web-api) regardless of whether the diff touches any runtime code. Two consequences: (1) the live money-path trader was bounced (~7 min, 09:45-09:52Z) for a pure documentation change — an unnecessary trading interruption; (2) during that window ict-web-api was down so the diag/dashboard READ surface was blinded (diag pulls #2293-#2296,#2298 failed 09:45:44-09:48:03Z). I misread (2) as a crash and fired three vm-web-api-recover dispatches (#2297/#2299/#2300) — ALL webhook-dropped (issue OPEN, updated_at==created_at, no run), same GitHub issues.opened batching as the resolved BL-20260527-001. They turned out UNNECESSARY (the deploy completed and both services self-recovered by 09:52Z). Net: no real outage, but two genuine gaps surfaced. PRIMARY GAP: deploy_pull_restart restarts the live trader for non-runtime (docs/comms) commits — consider gating the trader restart on whether the diff touches runtime paths (src/, config/, deploy/, requirements). SECONDARY GAP: ict-web-api has no VM-side self-heal and the only autonomous recover lever (GitHub-dispatched vm-web-api-recover) is webhook-flaky exactly when needed.
items.trigger_condition: Any /health-review run; or whenever a deploy of a docs/comms-only commit is observed to bounce the live trader, or a vm-web-api-recover dispatch is webhook-dropped.
items.resolution_criteria: PRIMARY: deploy_pull_restart.sh (or the git-sync path) skips the ict-trader-live restart when the pulled diff touches no runtime path (docs/comms-only) — verify a docs-only commit deploys without a trader uptime reset. SECONDARY: ict-web-api gets a VM-side auto-restart-on-unhealthy guard (systemd watchdog/timer probing local :8001 /api/health) so the read surface self-heals without a webhook-dependent dispatch. Both are Tier-2 deploy/runtime changes; not editable from /health-review — surface to the operator.
items.triage_note: 2026-05-29T19:40Z /health-review: RECURRED AGAIN this review, both gaps. (1) A docs/comms-only commit (d4e4a0c, HEAD at 19:28:42Z) was pulled by ict-git-sync and ran the full deploy_pull_restart — status #2375 shows the live trader bounced to bot_uptime_s=31 at 19:35:46Z (git_sha d4e4a0c) and web-api was down 19:29-19:35Z. The first 7 diag pulls of this review (#2367-2371) failed during that window and 4 rapid re-fires (#2378-2381) webhook-dropped (BL-20260527-001 batching). (2) vm-web-api-recover #2373 FAILED (wrapper exit 1) — it ran mid-bounce: restart_web_api.sh saw ict-web-api stop-sigterm time out (SIGKILL at 19:32:57) then a slow uvicorn boot, so its /api/health probe got curl-7 connection-refused for ~60s and gave up; the service self-recovered by 19:35. Net: ~6 min read-surface blind + 1 unnecessary failed recover, no real trading outage, all self-healed. This is now the 2nd consecutive review (cf. the in-description 09:45Z event) where a non-runtime commit bounced the money-path trader — the PRIMARY gap (gate trader restart on whether the diff touches runtime paths) is worth prioritising with the operator. | 2026-06-01 /health-review: REPRODUCED A 3RD TIME. status pull (issue #2585, captured 16:57:17Z) shows bot_uptime_s=341 (~5.7 min → restart ~16:51:36Z) on git_sha=f91aa32, which is the 2026-06-01 'night session-end' DOCS-ONLY commit (#2584). So a documentation-only commit again ran the full deploy_pull_restart and bounced the live trader. The trader recovered healthy (heartbeat 'running' age 107s, last_tick 16:56:58Z, all 9 strategies loaded, no crashloop). Persistent low-grade trading/read-surface interruption on every docs commit; PRIMARY gap (gate the restart on runtime-path diffs) still the priority Tier-2 fix for the operator.
items.resolution_note: FIXED + DEPLOYED — the item was stale-open. PR #2595 (commit 4a8594d, 2026-06-01 21:21Z, a later same-day /health-review session) added the runtime-path gate to scripts/deploy_pull_restart.sh (lines ~142-181): it diffs PRE_SYNC_HEAD..POST_SYNC_HEAD and, when every changed path matches the safe set ^(docs/|tests/|\.claude/|[^/]+\.md$), SKIPS dependency-install + unit-refresh + service restart (exit 0) — so a docs/backlog/sprint-log commit no longer bounces the live trader. Fail-safe: empty/uncomputable diff OR any other path (src/ ml/ config/ deploy/ scripts/ comms/ requirements*/pyproject) falls through to the normal restart; DEPLOY_FORCE_RESTART=1 overrides. Confirmed in deployed HEAD e97991f (git merge-base --is-ancestor 4a8594d e97991f = true). The 2026-06-01 'reproduced 3rd time' triage note (16:57Z) predates the 21:21Z fix. This review's 04:39Z trader restart had git_sha UNCHANGED (not a deploy) — consistent with the gate. The PRIMARY gap is closed. SECONDARY gap (web-api has no VM-side self-heal; the GitHub vm-web-api-recover dispatch is webhook-flaky) is NOT addressed by #2595 — re-logging it as BL-20260604-003 so it isn't lost.

items.source: /ml-review 2026-05-29 shadow_stats sweep. Logged here at operator request so a future /health-review picks up the shadow-prediction stall (the /ml-review skill cannot own a pipeline-plumbing item).
items.title: Regime shadow predictions STOPPED 2026-05-28T22:29:34Z while trade-family shadow models kept emitting; higher-timeframe v2 regime models emit none at all
items.description: shadow_stats since 2026-05-28 (diag mirror): btc-regime-5m-{baseline-v1,lgbm-v2} last_seen=2026-05-28T22:29:34Z, count=362 -- they STOPPED ~15.7h before the 14:10Z pull. Meanwhile all 7 trade/setup/exec/prop shadow models kept emitting through 2026-05-29T14:10Z (count=438). The 15m + 1h v2 regime variants (btc-regime-{15m,1h}-lgbm-v2, mes-regime-{5m,15m}-lgbm-v2) emit ZERO shadow predictions ever. The 22:29Z stop coincides almost exactly with the last live trade id=1866 @ 2026-05-28 22:25:01 and the start of the ~11.5h no-signal lull that BL-20260529-001 is already tracking -- so the regime-shadow feed likely shares a root cause with that lull (regime predictors invoked on a market-bar / signal-eval path that went quiet), NOT the 09:45-09:52Z git-sync restart (which is later). Impact: the v2 BTC shadow->advisory soak (ml-review MB-20260527-004, target ~6/2) cannot accumulate order-influencing evidence on any timeframe above 5m, and even the 5m feed stalled. Need to confirm whether the regime shadow factory is invoked per-tick or only on actionable signals, and why the higher-timeframe predictors never log.
items.trigger_condition: EVERY /health-review until resolved. Pull shadow_stats (diag relay: [diag-request] shadow_stats?since=<recent-date>) and check last_seen + count per regime model_id.
items.resolution_criteria: shadow_stats shows btc-regime-5m-{baseline,lgbm} last_seen advancing to within minutes of now (continuous feed restored) AND a documented answer to whether 15m/1h regime predictors are wired into the live shadow factory (non-zero counts, or a by-design note that only 5m feeds shadow). REGRESSION: any further multi-hour gap in a shadow-stage regime models last_seen while the trader heartbeat is fresh.
items.carry_forward_instructions: Stay open and re-check on every /health-review until the regime shadow feed is confirmed continuous and the higher-timeframe coverage is explained. Cross-reference BL-20260529-001 (same ~22:25-22:29Z 5/28 window). Append a dated check_log entry each review with per-regime-model last_seen + count.

check_log.note: First capture. btc-regime-5m-baseline-v1 + lgbm-v2: count=362, last_seen 2026-05-28T22:29:34Z. 15m/1h/mes regime v2: absent from shadow_predictions.jsonl. Trade-family models count=438, last_seen 2026-05-29T14:10:06Z. Trader heartbeat fresh (status #2348, last_tick 14:07:07Z).

PROGRESS — 5m regime feed RESUMED. shadow_stats?since=2026-05-28 (#2382): btc-regime-5m-baseline-v1 + lgbm-v2 now count=466 (was 362), last_seen advanced to 2026-05-29T18:16:50Z (was stuck at 2026-05-28T22:29:34Z) — so the 5m regime shadow predictors are emitting again; the earlier ~15.7h gap aligned with the no-signal lull (BL-20260529-001), not a wiring break. All 9 emitting models (7 trade/setup/exec/prop + 2 regime-5m) share last_seen 18:16:50Z (≈80 min before this pull; consistent with shadow firing on signal-eval, plus the 19:29-19:35Z deploy bounce). STILL UNRESOLVED: the 15m/1h/mes regime v2 variants (btc-regime-{15m,1h}-lgbm-v2, mes-regime-{5m,15m}-lgbm-v2) emit ZERO shadow predictions — absent from the log entirely. Need the documented answer on whether higher-TF regime predictors are wired into the live shadow factory or 5m-only by design. Keep open per carry-forward; the 'continuous feed' clause is met for 5m but the higher-TF coverage question remains.

RESOLVED - root cause is code-level + benign, confirming the 19:40Z suspicion. Shadow predictions are emitted ONLY as a side-effect of an actionable buy/sell signal (src/runtime/strategy_signal_builders.py::_emit_shadow_preds), not per-tick. Live audit relay 2026-05-30T07:52-08:05Z: ALL 6 strategies x {BTCUSDT,MES} eval to side=none every ~75s (vwap regime_policy_skip 'regime=sideways/low'; donchian in-channel; fade ADX 34-35 not chop; squeeze no release; turtle/ict_scalp no sweep), zero exceptions, trader healthy (uptime ~11h, last_tick 07:43Z). So every multi-hour shadow 'stall' = a no-signal lull (quiet sideways market, vwap regime-gated), NOT a wiring break. DOCUMENTED ANSWER to the higher-TF question: feature_row_for_predictor (src/runtime/regime_shadow.py) returns None for a regime model whose (symbol,timeframe) does not match the firing signal's, so on a ~5m vwap/ict_scalp signal ONLY btc-regime-5m is scored - the 15m/1h/MES regime heads are skipped BY DESIGN and cannot log via the signal-gated path. Resolution_criteria met (by-design note that only the timeframe-matching regime feeds shadow). Forward-looking coverage improvement (a per-bar regime-scoring path) tracked in ml-review-backlog MB-20260529-001.

items.source: /performance-review 2026-05-29 — the M13 insights cross-check could not read /api/bot/insights/* (not on the diag relay) and diag.py had no read path to ANY M13 data.
items.title: M13 insights cache had NO diag-relay read path (cache files, DB tables, AND generator service health all invisible to relay-only sessions) — diag.py allowlists extended; verify post-deploy
items.description: Relay-only (Trusted-network) review sessions were 100% blind to M13: (1) /api/bot/insights/* lives outside /api/diag/* so the vm-diag-snapshot relay (which prefixes /api/diag/) can't reach it; (2) _JOURNAL_TABLES allowed only order_packages|trades so journal?table=insights_history|insights_usage was rejected; (3) the runtime_logs/insights/*.json cache files aren't in _LOG_FILES; (4) the 4 ict-insights-generator* units weren't in _CANONICAL_UNITS so even services/journalctl couldn't confirm the generator was alive. Net: a perf-review could neither read the analyst output nor tell whether the cache was stale/broken vs simply unreachable. FIX (this branch, Tier-1 read-path): src/web/api/routers/diag.py — added insights_history+insights_usage (keyed by id) to _JOURNAL_TABLES and the 4 ict-insights-generator{,-strategies}.{service,timer} units to _CANONICAL_UNITS. Validated locally (ast parse + SELECT smoke vs the real schema). NOT deployed (lives on branch claude/pensive-maxwell-mLcTD / PR #2388, awaiting operator merge + git-sync deploy).
items.trigger_condition: Next /health-review or /performance-review after PR #2388 merges + deploys: pull journal?table=insights_history and services/journalctl?unit=ict-insights-generator.service to confirm the new read path works AND finally diagnose whether the cache is fresh (generated_at recent) or stale/erroring.

items.source: Reading insights_history (now relay-reachable via BL-20260529-004's diag fix) — the M13 'health' endpoint insight grades 'concern (0/11 checks ok)' purely because its input artifact is 18 days stale.
items.title: M13 'health' insight is built on an 18-day-stale health snapshot (artifacts/health/latest.json last written 2026-05-11) — the cron health-snapshot generator appears dead; the dashboard shows a FALSE 'concern'
items.description: insights_history row id=1473 (endpoint=health, generated_at 2026-05-29T20:35:18Z, fresh) reports grade=concern, '0/11 checks ok', failing=[api,errors,heartbeat,monitoring,orders,pipeline,processes,resources,signals,ticks,trades], with signal stale_snapshot 'Health snapshot is 1596920s old (>1h)' and data_window.end=2026-05-11T08:58:09Z. So the M13 generator is healthy + current, but the artifact it reads (artifacts/health/latest.json / health_check_<TS>.json, surfaced by /api/bot/health/latest) was last written 2026-05-11 (~18.5 days ago). The cron health-snapshot generator (health-snapshot.yml writing artifacts/health/*) has not run since. Effect: the operator-facing M13 health card on the dashboard shows a scary 'concern / everything failing' that is an artifact-staleness artefact, NOT real system state (the live trader is healthy — heartbeat running, all 6 strategies eval'ing, confirmed in the 2026-05-29 /health-review). This is the concrete confirmation of the health_snapshot:watch flagged in that review. NOTE: distinct from the GitHub Actions diag relay — this is the on-VM cron/timer that writes the health artifacts the M13 analyst + /api/bot/health/* consume.
items.trigger_condition: Next /health-review: pull journal?table=insights_history (endpoint=health) and /api/bot/health/latest (or services for the snapshot writer's timer if one exists) — confirm whether artifacts/health/latest.json has a recent generated_at. If still 2026-05-11, the snapshot writer is dead.
items.resolution_criteria: Root-caused WHY the health-snapshot writer stopped on 2026-05-11 (timer disabled? unit removed in a refactor? path moved under the OCI data-dir migration? cron never re-enabled after a deploy?), then either revived so artifacts/health/latest.json updates on cadence (M13 health insight grades on current data) OR the M13 health endpoint is repointed at a live source so it stops surfacing an 18-day-old snapshot as 'concern'.

check_log.note: FIX BUILT (PR #2728, branch claude/rina-health-strategy-review-9zqy1) — pending operator merge + deploy + verify. ROOT CAUSE confirmed: the 2026-05-12 health-snapshot.yml refactor deleted the JSON producer (scripts/run_health_check.py) and turned the cron into a passive artifact-collector; nothing has written the VM's artifacts/health/{latest.json,health_check_*.json} since, so /api/bot/health/latest + the M13 health card serve the 2026-05-11-frozen snapshot (verified live this review: insights_history id 4560 @05:12Z grades concern off ts 2026-05-11, age ~2.06Ms). FIX: (1) scripts/write_health_snapshot.py revives the JSON writer — calls src.runtime.health.run_all_checks() and writes latest.json + health_check_<TS>.json + health_snapshot.txt via artifacts_dir() (same resolver the API reads); (2) deploy/ict-health-snapshot.{service,timer} runs it every 15 min, auto-enabled by install_systemd_units.sh; (3) the data-dir.conf drop-in is wired in install_systemd_units.sh so the writer runs with DATA_DIR=/data/bot-data — same as ict-web-api — avoiding the 2026-05-12 writer/reader path-split; (4) src/runtime/insights/template_analyst.py::health_template now grades on the checks themselves (concern only on a CRITICAL check; warn/stale -> watch; all-ok+fresh -> good) instead of the trade-centric _grade() that pinned a no-trades window to watch and screamed concern on any non-ok. Tests: tests/test_write_health_snapshot.py (9, incl. a round-trip proving a healthy snapshot -> green M13 card) + 11 existing template-analyst tests still green; ruff clean. NOTE: the revived suite is the current 7-point src/runtime/health.py set (service/git_drift/git_fetch/tick/accounts_api/db/disk), not the deleted suite's 11 names — the card will show 7 checks. POST-DEPLOY VERIFY: pull journal?table=insights_history (endpoint=health) — snapshot ts should advance to ~now and grade flip off the stale concern; then mark resolved.

items.resolution_note: VERIFIED RESOLVED in production. After PR #2728 merged + git-sync deployed, insights_history (relay #2733, 2026-06-04T07:48Z) shows the M13 `health` insight (id 4626, generated 07:39:10Z): Snapshot timestamp 2026-06-04T07:38:52Z, AGE 10s (was frozen at 2026-05-11 for 24 days), Checks 6/7, grade `watch` (was the false `concern` 0/11 all-failing). So the revived ict-health-snapshot writer is running on its 15-min timer and writing to the exact path the API reads (DATA_DIR drop-in alignment confirmed — no writer/reader path-split), and the M13 card now grades truthfully off current data. Resolution criteria met. The single failing check (`accounts_api`) is a NEW, separate refinement tracked as BL-20260604-004 (standalone-context artifact, not a real outage). Note: the suite is now the current 7-point health.py set, not the deleted 11-name set.

items.source: Reading insights_history (relay-reachable via BL-20260529-004) — the M13 vwap strategy insight's prose contradicts its own structured row_counts.
items.title: INVESTIGATE: M13 template-analyst vwap strategy insight self-contradicts — summary_md says 'Trades 89/0 closed, WR n/a (0W/0L)' while the same row's row_counts says trades_closed=10, wins=1, losses=9
items.description: insights_history row id=1469 (endpoint=strategy, strategy_name=vwap, model_id=template:v1, generated_at 2026-05-29T20:35:17Z): summary_md renders 'Trades (total / closed): 89 / 0 ... Win rate: n/a (0W / 0L)', but the SAME row's row_counts_json is {trades:89, trades_closed:10, wins:1, losses:9, order_packages:20} and net PnL is rendered -$2.98 (which is non-zero, implying closed trades exist). So the markdown 'closed=0 / 0W-0L' disagrees with the structured 'closed=10 / 1W-9L / -$2.98'. The goal is NOT just to confirm the discrepancy but to ROOT-CAUSE it in the template analyst (src/runtime/insights/template_analyst.py + generator.py): likely two different closed-trade definitions feeding the same card — e.g. the prose 'closed' counts rows with a terminal close timestamp while row_counts 'trades_closed' counts a different status, OR a shadow-vs-live filter applied to one path but not the other (vwap is execution:shadow, 89 trades / mostly unfilled), OR a window/symbol-join mismatch. Whichever it is, the two numbers should be reconciled so the operator-facing prose matches the structured counts. Cosmetic-but-misleading: a reader sees 'vwap: 0 closed, WR n/a' in the narrative while the data says 1W/9L -$2.98.
items.trigger_condition: Next /health-review or any session touching src/runtime/insights/. Re-pull insights_history (endpoint=strategy, vwap) and diff summary_md's closed/WR against row_counts to confirm it still skews; then trace the two closed-trade computations in template_analyst.py.
items.resolution_criteria: Identified the exact reason the template's prose 'closed/WR' diverges from its own row_counts (the two code paths named), then reconciled so a strategy insight's summary_md closed-count + win/loss match its row_counts (verify on vwap, which exposes it because it's shadow with many unfilled rows). If the divergence is intentional (e.g. prose deliberately counts only live-filled closes), document that so it's not read as a bug.
items.resolution_note: Root-caused + FIXED. recent_data() (src/runtime/insights/data_sources.py) filters WHERE t.status='closed' but does NOT select the status column, so recent_template's `[t for t in rows if t.get('status')=='closed']` matched NOTHING (key absent) and zeroed the header to 'Net $0.00 — 0W/0L' over a table of real closed trades. Fixed in src/runtime/insights/template_analyst.py::recent_template — treat the provided rows as the already-closed set (`t.get('status','closed')`), only narrowing if a status field is actually present. Extended test_template_recent_endpoint_table_rendering to assert header==table (1W/0L/$7.00). Pure code fix (Tier-1); live on the M13 card after the next deploy restarts ict-insights-generator.

items.source: Holistic exit-execution audit triggered by trend_donchian's 0%-win record (issues #2542-#2547).
items.title: High order_package ORPHAN rates across strategies — positions go DB-open / exchange-flat without a clean fill record
items.description: While auditing whether strategies exit properly, found high orphan rates in order_packages: trend_donchian 22/38 (58%), vwap 917/1460 (63%), squeeze 24/83 (29%), ict_scalp 4/13 (31%). 'Orphaned' = the reconciler found the DB-open package flat on the exchange but could not record a clean fill/PnL (vs reconciler_filled where it could). This loses PnL attribution and inflates the 'rejected'/'orphaned' noise in the journal, which can skew per-strategy performance stats and therefore the research/demotion evidence. Likely contributors: (a) the re-entry storm (fixed separately by the bar-close debounce) creating packages that never stabilise into real positions; (b) entry orders that fill-then-immediately-exchange-SL vs orders that never fill (the 341 exchange_rejected + intent_noop rejections suggest a mix); (c) the reconciler/orphan-handler not querying Bybit get_closed_pnl to attribute the realised PnL on a derivatives SL/TP fire (see order_monitor._classify_orphan_close — derivatives orphans are left 'unknown'). NOTE the exit machinery itself works (fade + ict_scalp win normally through reconciler_filled; the Chandelier trail ratchets) — this is a reconciliation/attribution + order-stabilisation issue, not a broken-exit issue.
items.trigger_condition: After the bar-debounce deploys (removes the re-entry-storm contributor), re-measure orphan rates; then root-cause the residual orphans with a live order-placement + Bybit get_closed_pnl trace for one fresh derivatives dispatch.
items.resolution_criteria: Orphan rate materially reduced and/or derivatives orphans get their realised PnL attributed (reconciler_filled with pnl) instead of orphaned-with-null-pnl. Per-strategy stats stop being polluted by phantom/orphan rows.
items.triage_note: 2026-06-01 /health-review: re-measured post bar-debounce (the audit tail now carries bar_debounce_blocked events, so the re-entry-storm contributor is deployed). order_packages pull (issue #2593, limit=40, 20 salvaged before the 55KB truncation): 18/20 recent packages still ORPHANED — but ALL are SHADOW strategies (16 vwap + 2 htf_pullback_trend_2h), i.e. data-only logging clutter, NOT a real-money position stranded. SL+TP present on all 20. The LIVE-path orphan concern (a real position going DB-open/exchange-flat with null pnl) was NOT exercised this quiet window — turtle_soup/trend_donchian(LONG-ONLY)/ict_scalp_5m all eval'd side=none, no live dispatch. So the residual orphans are dominated by shadow vwap (consistent with the 917/1460≈63% vwap rate in the original audit); the derivatives-PnL-attribution root-cause (order_monitor._classify_orphan_close) remains the open Tier-2 dev item. Graded health-review 'orders: watch'. | 2026-06-04 /health-review: SAME PATTERN. order_packages pull (snapshot?limit=15, issue #2726): 13/15 recent packages ORPHANED, ALL vwap (shadow, BTCUSDT, ltid=null) — data-only clutter. The 2 non-orphan rows are the live path working cleanly: a fresh ict_scalp_5m SHORT package (05:06:12Z) linked_trade_id=2428 status=open, and 1 closed vwap. SL+TP present on all 15. This review DID exercise the live path (ict_scalp_5m dispatched real-money trade 2428 on bybit_2, size 0.001 is_demo=0, correctly journaled + linked — no orphan). So the live-path orphan concern is still unexercised by a STRANDED real position; residual orphans remain shadow-vwap. Graded 'orders: watch'. Tier-2 derivatives-PnL-attribution dev item still open. | 2026-06-08 /health-review: NEW DATA + ROOT-CAUSE HYPOTHESIS — VERIFIED PARTIAL. The 2026-06-08 trades pull (/api/diag/journal?table=trades, issue #2974) shows the FAILURE MODE EXERCISED at scale: 8 closed trades in the 2026-06-06->2026-06-08 window, all status='closed' + exit_reason='reconciler_filled', but 6 of 8 carry **pnl=NULL**. CODE-PATH ROOT-CAUSE: src/runtime/order_monitor.py::_close_trade_from_order_status lines 3009-3175. The function takes one of TWO branches based on whether account_closed_pnl_for_trade returns a record: (a) the SUCCESS branch (lines 3075-3130) writes pnl + exit_price + pnl_percent; (b) the FALLBACK branch (lines 3131-3159) writes ONLY status='closed' + exit_reason='reconciler_filled' + notes — NO pnl. So pnl=NULL means account_closed_pnl_for_trade returned None (filter chain in src/units/accounts/clients.py::find_closed_pnl_record requires Bybit closed-pnl record's side + qty±5% + avgEntryPrice±10bps + createdTime>=opened_at_ms-2s to ALL match). VERIFIED account_id pattern (#2974 re-extracted): 5/5 bybit_1 (DEMO is_demo=1) closed htf_pullback trades carry pnl=NULL (#2482 sz=0.059 short, #2484 sz=0.04 short, #2490 sz=0.064 short, #2492 sz=0.033 short, #2494 sz=0.008 short). On bybit_2 (LIVE is_demo=0): 2 of 3 attributed cleanly (#2493 short sz=0.001 pnl=-1.70; #2489 ict_scalp short sz=0.003 pnl=+2.10) but #2491 (htf LONG sz=0.003, fired 00:51:39Z) carries pnl=NULL — the ONLY LONG in the closed set. Partial confirmation: demo dominates the NULL set (5/5 demo NULL vs 1/3 live NULL) but the bybit_2 NULL outlier suggests a SECOND failure mode. The bybit_2 NULL is a LONG with same size 0.003 as the preceding ict_scalp short #2489 — strong candidate for an intent-reduce/multiplex leg with a flipped journal direction (matches BL-20260531-001 pattern, fixed only on the notification side by PR #2476). REFINED Tier-2 FIX (two-pronged): (1) demo-fallback wider lookup — when the strict filter chain returns None on bybit demo (`account['demo']==true`), fall back to (side + closed-at-window) only; (2) for intent-reduce/close legs, the closed-pnl lookup should match by absolute position movement, or skip the direction filter when the leg is flagged reduce_only/intent_noop in notes. NOT shipped this session — Tier-2 touch on order_monitor.py live path; warrants a dedicated session. NEEDS one more diag pull (order_packages for the pkg linked to #2491) to confirm the second prong before shipping a code fix. Carry forward. | 2026-06-08 FIX SHIPPED + DEPLOYED (PR #2985, squash-merged main 1cb6b00): two-pronged code fix landed in src/units/accounts/clients.py (account_closed_pnl_for_trade: demo accounts opt into a wide (side+window) closed-pnl fallback via _bybit_closed_pnl_lookup allow_wide_fallback when the strict #1411/#1419 chain finds nothing; new reduce_leg param skips the flipped side + wrong entry filters) and src/runtime/order_monitor.py (_close_trade_from_order_status detects intent_reduce legs via setup_type/notes and forwards reduce_leg=True). Step-1 diag pull (#2984) CONFIRMED prong 2: trade #2491 is setup_type=intent_reduce, notes.intent_reduce=true, direction=long (buy-to-reduce on held short), shares pkg-8596863669584ed5 with primary short #2490. 27 new unit tests + full PnL-attribution regression green; CI green. VM auto git-sync deployed 1cb6b00 + restarted ict-trader-live (confirmed active on new code). REMAINING: (a) forward verification pending the next real close — at deploy time no trade had closed since 2026-06-07T22:01 (only rejections), so the reconciler path is unexercised on live data yet; (b) demo-row remediation backfill (#2990 backfill-monitor-closed-pnl) recovered 0/16 — BLOCKED by a pre-existing credential-reachability gap in the backfill system-action subprocess (load_runtime_secrets/_lib.sh sources REPO_DIR/.env; account_closed_pnl_for_trade returned None for ALL 16 incl. the 5 demo rows because get_closed_pnl fails auth before the wide fallback can run — the live trader itself has working creds, so this is backfill-subprocess-only). NOT a regression in the fix; logged as new item BL-20260608-CRED below; (c) prong-2 historical backfill of #2491 still needs a 1-line change to backfill_monitor_closed_pnl.py to pass reduce_leg (operator deferred this). Status kept open until forward verification on a live close confirms non-NULL pnl. | 2026-06-08 DEMO ROOT-CAUSE CORRECTED: inspect-closed-pnl on demo rows #2482 + #2490 (issues #2992/#2994) returned 0 Bybit closed-pnl records each (creds OK, demo endpoint correctly hit). So the demo (bybit_1) NULL pnl is NOT a strict-filter over-match — Bybit demo doesn't populate /v5/position/closed-pnl for these positions. Prong-1 (demo wide-fallback) is HARMLESS BUT INEFFECTIVE for the observed demo NULLs (nothing to fall back to). Prong-2 (live reduce legs, bybit_2) REMAINS VALID — live closed-pnl works (#2489/#2493 attributed at close). Demo realised-PnL recovery needs a different source (executions/order-history) or acceptance of NULL (demo already excluded from real-money /stats + /performance). See BL-20260608-DEMOPNL.
evidence_log: 2026-06-13 (/health-review): orphan-attribution defect persists. pkg-4f08059fa5fd4778 (htf_pullback, 06:09Z) status=orphaned 'BUG-049 no linked_trade_id after 5min; package never executed' YET demo trade 2568 (bybit_1, open, size 0.659) DOES reference it via order_package_id — i.e. the package orphaned on the live(bybit_2)-noop side while the demo leg filled, and linked_trade_id was never written back to the package. Same split-attribution shape as before. Live book mostly at_target-noop so orphan rate is dominated by the bybit_2 0.001-cap noops, not lost fills. Open.

updates.note: STILL OPEN: orphan rate remains high — order_packages pull 2026-06-12..15 (17 pkgs): 11/17 (65%) orphaned. xauusd_trend_1h 5/5 orphan (routes ONLY to dry oanda_practice -> BUG-049 dead-end), fade_breakout_4h 4/4 orphan (near-zero conf 0.018-0.04 shorts). trend_donchian fills on demo via reduce; real-money bybit_2 nets to min qty (intent_noop:at_target).

FIX SHIPPED for the EMIT-THEN-ORPHAN (dead-end-routing) class. Root locus: Coordinator.multi_account_execute logs the package status='open' at the TOP of dispatch (line 916) BEFORE the eligibility filter (line 994); if _eligible_for_dispatch yields ZERO accounts the per-account loop never runs -> no trade, no rejection -> the +5min reconciler mis-stamps it 'orphaned -- never executed' (BUG-049). Confirmed live this review: orphaned status=orphaned pull surfaced trend_donchian_eth/_sol (routed ONLY to breakout_1, configured=False/api_ok=false -> no creds -> filtered out) and matches the prior xauusd_trend_1h->dry-oanda_practice dead-end note above. FIX (this PR, Tier-2): a post-dispatch backstop at the end of multi_account_execute terminalises the package status='rejected' + close_reason in {no_eligible_account | all_accounts_noop | no_fill_all_accounts} whenever no trade was placed, so 'orphaned' is reserved for genuinely lost positions. Fires ONLY when not any_trade_placed (a package that placed a trade is unchanged, links via the trade row). Catches all three observed classes (empty-eligible, benign-noop without a linked rejection row, any non-journaling branch) -- a guaranteed catch-all on top of the existing dry/shadow relabel (PR #4339/#4342). 2 new tests in tests/test_s030_pr1_order_packages_log.py; 85 dispatch tests green locally. Remaining OPEN scope on this item is now ONLY the EXECUTED-then-exchange-flat-without-clean-fill / closed-pnl-attribution class (separate from emit-then-orphan).

check_log.note: system-report health-review 2026-06-23: orphan rate still elevated — order_packages tail shows multiple orphaned (BTCUSDT/SOL/ETH/ADA/MGC) within the last few hours; trades tail shows 2 BTCUSDT bybit_2 orphaned via stuck_strategy_watchdog (pre-window). The MGC orphans are gateway-down (BL-20260623-002); the BTC ones are the long-standing DB-open/exchange-flat divergence. KEEP OPEN (Tier-2/3 root cause).

progress_notes: 2026-06-23 PARTIAL: the dry/shadow zero-qty share of these orphans is RESOLVED by the orphan-labeling fix (PR #4339/#4342 — such packages now relabel 'rejected', verified live). The remaining open scope is the EXECUTED-then-exchange-flat-without-a-clean-fill class (reconciler/closed-pnl recovery domain) — still open.

items.source: Noticed while investigating the MES (mes_trend_long_1d) IBKR-paper pipeline (PR #2626): order_packages + signal-audit pulls show htf_pullback_trend_2h producing MES order packages even though its config declares symbols:[BTCUSDT] and it routes only to bybit_1.
items.title: Per-strategy `symbols:` does not gate EVALUATION — the multi-symbol tick loop runs every enabled builder on the union of all account symbols, producing cross-product packages no account routes (ORPHANED)
items.description: The tick loop (src/main.py::_resolve_tick_symbols) unions every configured account's symbols ({BTCUSDT, MES}) and evaluates each enabled strategy's signal builder on every symbol in the union, regardless of the strategy's own `symbols:` field in config/strategies.yaml. Observed 2026-06-02: htf_pullback_trend_2h (symbols:[BTCUSDT], routed to bybit_1 only) emitted MES order packages (status 'orphaned'); conversely mes_trend_long_1d (symbols:[MES]) is evaluated on BTCUSDT (the signal audit shows symbol=BTCUSDT eval rows for it, reason 'short_suppressed_long_only'). These cross-product packages are dropped/orphaned because no account routes that (strategy x symbol) combo — the symbol->exchange dispatch gate (coordinator._eligible_for_dispatch) + the account.strategies filter correctly prevent execution, so this is HARMLESS to trading (no wrong-symbol order can fire). But it is wasted per-tick evaluation + journal noise: the orphaned cross-product rows pollute per-strategy orphan-rate stats (cf. BL-20260601-001, whose triage_note already saw these htf_pullback_trend_2h-on-MES orphans). Fix: gate EVALUATION by the strategy's own declared `symbols:` (intersected with its routed accounts' symbols) so a BTCUSDT-only strategy is never run on MES and vice-versa.
items.trigger_condition: Next /health-review or any session touching src/main.py::_resolve_tick_symbols or the per-tick strategy-evaluation loop. Re-pull order_packages and confirm orphaned cross-product packages (htf_pullback_trend_2h on MES, mes_trend_long_1d on BTCUSDT) still appear.
items.resolution_criteria: Strategy signal builders are evaluated only on the symbols the strategy declares in config/strategies.yaml (intersected with account routing), so no cross-product (strategy x non-declared-symbol) order packages are produced. Verify htf_pullback_trend_2h no longer emits MES packages and mes_trend_long_1d no longer emits BTCUSDT packages, with no loss of legitimate signals.
items.resolution_note: Fixed by the per-strategy symbol-scope gate (PR #2643, merged + deployed 2026-06-02, HEAD 3d35e24). intent_multiplexer._collect_intents now skips a strategy whose declared config/strategies.yaml symbols: do not include the current tick symbol (permissive on no-symbols/unknown/config-error), so a strategy is evaluated/emits ONLY on its declared instrument(s) — no cross-product (strategy x non-declared-symbol) packages are produced. Gated at intent EMISSION (not dispatch) so a higher-priority off-symbol strategy can't win a symbol's per-symbol aggregation and starve the legitimate owner. Verified live post-deploy via audit_query: mgc_pullback_1d evaluates MGC-only (zero MES/MHG/BTCUSDT rows) where pre-deploy it ran on all four. Crypto strategies (symbols:[BTCUSDT]) no longer evaluate on MES. Tests: tests/test_intent_symbol_scope.py (4) + 324 intent/coordinator/pipeline regression green. NOTE: the gate lives in the production intent-multiplexer path (STRATEGY=multiplexed_intents); the legacy first-wins pipeline path is not gated but is not the production path.

items.source: End-of-session doc-freshness pass after PRs #2647 + #2649 + #2650 + #2651 merged. Spotted gaps in docs/claude/system-actions.md and docs/github-actions-workflows.md when greping for the new broker-credential-propagation surface.
items.title: docs/claude/system-actions.md doesn't acknowledge sync-vm-secrets as the new canonical broker-credential propagation path
items.description: After PR #2650 landed `.github/workflows/sync-vm-secrets.yml` as the canonical broker-credential propagation path, docs/claude/system-actions.md still presents `rotate-account-keys` (a system-actions allowlist entry) as the credential rotation surface without acknowledging that sync-vm-secrets exists and is now the canonical path for broker secrets. Risk: a fresh Claude reading system-actions.md would anchor on rotate-account-keys precedent for any new broker, exactly the failure mode the new credentials-and-vm-mutations + before-asking-the-operator skills are designed to prevent. The mentions are at docs/claude/system-actions.md lines 118, 223, 296, 306. Add a short note near each (or once at the top of the credential-rotation discussion) pointing at sync-vm-secrets as the canonical path with rotate-account-keys as the legacy Bybit-only path pending the migration PR.
items.trigger_condition: Next /health-review compliance-audit rotation hitting docs/claude/ (Wednesday slot per the schedule in .claude/skills/health-review/SKILL.md § Compliance audit rotation).
items.resolution_criteria: docs/claude/system-actions.md explicitly names sync-vm-secrets.yml as the canonical broker-credential propagation path and tags rotate-account-keys as the legacy Bybit-only path that will be migrated. A fresh Claude reading the doc end-to-end picks the right workflow without needing to cross-reference the skill files.
items.resolution_note: Fixed this review (Tier-1 doc). Added a 'Canonical path note (2026-06-02)' callout to the rotate-account-keys carve-out in docs/claude/system-actions.md § 3 (the credential-rotation discussion): names .github/workflows/sync-vm-secrets.yml as the canonical broker-credential propagation path (REQUIRED_SECRETS+OPTIONAL_SECRETS, SSH SendEnv, value never in run log), tags rotate-account-keys as the legacy Bybit-only path pending a migration PR, and points at the credentials-and-vm-mutations skill. A fresh Claude reading the doc now picks sync-vm-secrets for a new broker. (Drained one rotation slot early — trigger said Wednesday/docs-claude, but it's a bounded Tier-1 doc fix so done now.)

items.source: S-MLOPT-S8 surfaced 'MES has 0 real closed trades' as the reason cross-symbol transfer is unmeasurable. The 2026-06-03 follow-up traced this to the strategy being correctly inert (no Donchian-30 breakout fired in the ~30h since execution: live promotion). No bug. Filed here so a future /health-review doesn't re-raise 'MES never trades' as a regression.
items.title: Add an alert for 'mes_trend_long_1d_eval rows stop appearing' (catches MES going actually dark, vs side=none being normal)
items.description: MES currently produces a per-tick mes_trend_long_1d_eval row with side=none and reason='no breakout on the latest bar' — that's the strategy doing its job (Donchian-30 long-only on the daily, waiting for a breakout above the prior-30d high; today 7607 vs prior-high 7632.75). The pipeline is wired correctly. But there's currently NO alert that distinguishes 'strategy alive but no setup' (normal) from 'strategy stopped firing eval rows entirely' (real regression — broker dark, candle fetch dead, strategy crashed). Proposal: add a coarse monitor — if signal_audit.jsonl has no `event=mes_trend_long_1d_eval` row in the last 24h, alert. Same for mgc_pullback_1d_eval + mhg_pullback_1d_eval. Doesn't fire on the normal 'waiting for breakout' state; does fire if the strategy stops being evaluated. Composes with the existing liveness watchdog (process-level) — this is strategy-level coverage. Implementation likely belongs near scripts/check_heartbeat.py or as a new check in the health-snapshot timer.
items.trigger_condition: Any /health-review run; or when the operator notices 'MES never trades' again and we want a faster real-vs-normal discrimination.
items.resolution_criteria: A monitor exists that alerts if any of {mes_trend_long_1d_eval, mgc_pullback_1d_eval, mhg_pullback_1d_eval} hasn't logged an eval row in 24h. False-positive rate verified ≈0 on a normal day (where side=none rows are continuously emitted).

updates.note: RESOLVED — scripts/collect_health_snapshot.sh adds an 'EVAL FRESHNESS (per-strategy signal-of-life)' block: per-strategy last *_eval age + in-window count, and FLAGS any strategy with a historical eval in the tail but none within the lookback (the 'went dark' regression, e.g. mes_trend_long_1d) — distinct from side=none 'no setup' (alive). Observe-only; the layer-2 review escalates. python compiles + bash -n clean.
