items.source: Thursday config/ compliance-audit rotation (per .claude/skills/health-review/SKILL.md). Bright-line scan of config/*.yaml + config/*.json against before-asking-the-operator + credentials-and-vm-mutations.
items.title: config/accounts.yaml Tradovate hookup checklist (steps 2-4) attributes credential-propagation + VM CLI runs to the operator, routing around sync-vm-secrets.yml
items.description: The tradovate_demo_1 'Hookup checklist (operator)' comment block (config/accounts.yaml ~L414-441, added in the 2026-06-02 Tradovate session) violates the current autonomy canon in three steps: (Step 2) 'Add the seven required env vars to GitHub Actions secrets AND to the live-trader systemd EnvironmentFile (the systemd unit picks them up on restart)' — the operator originates the secret VALUES (Actions secrets) but PROPAGATION to the VM .env/EnvironmentFile is NOT operator work; it's .github/workflows/sync-vm-secrets.yml's job (the canonical broker-credential propagation path, which already declares TRADOVATE_* in its secret set). Hand-editing the systemd EnvironmentFile is the bright-line 'on the VM, edit' pattern credentials-and-vm-mutations forbids. (Steps 3-4) 'Run `python -m src.units.accounts.tradovate.cli.list_accounts` on the VM' + 'Run the smoke test on the VM' — running a CLI on the live VM is runner-dispatchable (a system-action or a diag/trainer-style relay), not operator-attributed manual SSH work. Net: a fresh Claude reading this checklist would anchor on operator-does-VM-work precedent — exactly the failure mode the new skills prevent. HARMLESS to trading (the account is mode:dry_run, dry-run-guard:allow, inert phase-1). config/accounts.yaml is Tier-3 (operator-only) so /health-review cannot edit it.
items.trigger_condition: Any /health-review run; or whenever a Tier-3 PR touches config/accounts.yaml (e.g. the Tradovate go-live) and the checklist can be rewritten in the same diff.
items.resolution_criteria: The tradovate_demo_1 hookup checklist in config/accounts.yaml is rewritten so: step 2 says the operator adds the seven TRADOVATE_* VALUES to Actions secrets and then `sync-vm-secrets.yml` propagates them to the VM .env (no manual EnvironmentFile edit); steps 3-4's on-VM CLI runs are framed as Claude-dispatched (system-action / relay) rather than operator-attributed manual SSH. A fresh Claude reading it does not infer operator-does-VM-work. Operator-approved Tier-3 edit.
items.resolution_note: FIXED (Tier-3 config — pending operator merge). Rewrote the tradovate_demo_1 hookup checklist in config/accounts.yaml so it conforms to the autonomy contract: step 2 now has the operator ORIGINATE the seven TRADOVATE_* secret VALUES in Actions secrets (Claude pre-creates slots via init-actions-secrets) and PROPAGATION runs through sync-vm-secrets.yml (no hand-edited VM EnvironmentFile); steps 3-4 frame the on-VM CLI runs (list_accounts + smoke test) as Claude relay-dispatched, not operator manual SSH. Per-step [operator]/[Claude] tags added. Awaiting operator merge of the Tier-3 change.

items.source: Rina performance-review 2026-06-04 — the perf-review skill's documented pulls (/api/bot/trades/closed, /api/bot/order-packages, /api/bot/performance, /api/bot/insights/*) are unreachable from a relay-only session.
items.title: vm-diag-snapshot relay hardcodes /api/diag/ — perf-review's /api/bot/{performance,trades/closed,insights/*} pulls are unreachable from a Trusted-network (relay-only) session
items.description: .github/workflows/vm-diag-snapshot.yml strips a leading /api/diag/ (line ~134) and curls a hardcoded 'http://127.0.0.1:8001/api/diag/<path>' (line ~185), so the relay can ONLY hit /api/diag/* endpoints. The performance-review SKILL.md § 'Fetching trade data' documents pulling /api/bot/trades/closed?since=&limit=500, /api/bot/order-packages?since=, /api/bot/performance?window=, and /api/bot/insights/{summary,recent,strategy,health} — NONE of which the relay can reach. A relay-only (default Trusted-network) review session therefore cannot get the clean windowed aggregates (/api/bot/performance) or the closed-trade list (/api/bot/trades/closed); it must fall back to /api/diag/journal?table=trades (reject-dominated, 55KB-truncated ~46 rows) + /api/diag/journal?table=insights_history (the M13 'recent' card, which DOES surface the last-20 closed trades — the practical workaround this review used). Net: per-strategy realized-PnL aggregation is hampered relay-only. Fix options: (a) extend vm-diag-snapshot.yml to allow an /api/bot/ prefix passthrough for the read-only GET aggregate endpoints (performance, trades/closed, order-packages); or (b) add /api/diag/performance + /api/diag/trades_closed mirrors; or (c) document insights_history as the canonical relay-only closed-trade source in the perf-review skill. Mirrors the BL-20260529-004 insights-relay-reach fix (which added insights_history/insights_usage to _JOURNAL_TABLES).
items.trigger_condition: Any /performance-review or /health-review run from a relay-only session that needs /api/bot/ aggregate data; or next time someone extends the diag relay.
items.resolution_criteria: Either the vm-diag relay can return /api/bot/performance + /api/bot/trades/closed (passthrough or /api/diag mirror), verified by a relay pull; OR the perf-review SKILL.md is updated to use the relay-reachable substitutes (journal?table=trades + insights_history) as the canonical relay-only path so a future session doesn't assume the /api/bot/ pulls work.
items.resolution_note: FIXED — extended .github/workflows/vm-diag-snapshot.yml so the relay can reach an ALLOWLISTED set of read-only /api/bot/* Tier-1 GET endpoints (performance, trades/closed, order-packages, insights/*, health/*, shadow/*, etc.) in addition to /api/diag/*. Default upstream stays /api/diag/; an api/bot/* path is routed to /api/bot/ only if its base (query stripped) is in the explicit allowlist, else rejected. curl URL switched to ${diag_upstream_prefix}${diag_path_safe}. Verified the routing logic + YAML parse locally (diag unchanged; allowlisted bot paths route correctly; non-allowlisted bot paths reject). A future /performance-review can now pull /api/bot/performance + /api/bot/trades/closed cleanly relay-only. Pending merge.

items.opened_by: claude (/health-review — Rina, split from BL-20260529-002 close)
items.source: Closing BL-20260529-002 (deploy-restart gate fixed by #2595). Its SECONDARY gap is unaddressed and should not be lost on the close.
items.title: ict-web-api has no VM-side self-heal; the only autonomous recover lever (GitHub vm-web-api-recover) is webhook-flaky exactly when needed
items.description: BL-20260529-002's PRIMARY gap (docs-commit trader bounce) was fixed by PR #2595. Its SECONDARY gap remains: ict-web-api.service has no on-VM auto-restart-on-unhealthy guard, and the only autonomous recovery path (the GitHub-dispatched vm-web-api-recover workflow) is subject to the issues.opened webhook batch-drop (BL-20260527-001) precisely during a deploy bounce when the read surface is down — observed 2026-05-29 (#2297/#2299/#2300 all dropped) and the #2373 recover that ran mid-bounce failed. With the PRIMARY gap closed the read-surface blind windows are now rarer (docs commits no longer bounce anything), but a genuine web-api crash still has no VM-side self-heal. Fix: add a systemd watchdog/timer (mirror ict-liveness-watchdog / ict-ib-gateway-watchdog) that probes local 127.0.0.1:8001/api/health every ~2 min and restarts ict-web-api.service on N consecutive failures, with restart-cap + cooldown so a genuine crashloop alert-escalates rather than loops. Removes the webhook dependency for read-surface recovery.
items.trigger_condition: Any /health-review run; or next time a vm-web-api-recover dispatch is webhook-dropped or ict-web-api is observed down with no autonomous recovery.
items.resolution_criteria: ict-web-api gets a VM-side auto-restart-on-unhealthy guard (systemd timer probing local /api/health) that self-heals the read surface without a webhook-dependent GitHub dispatch; verified by inducing/observing one web-api outage that the watchdog recovers autonomously. Tier-2 deploy/runtime change — operator-approved PR.

check_log.note: FIX BUILT (pending merge+deploy+verify). scripts/check_web_api.py — stdlib-only watchdog probing http://127.0.0.1:8001/api/health every 2 min (ict-web-api-watchdog.{service,timer}); after 2 consecutive failures runs scripts/ops/restart_web_api.sh, guard-railed --max-restarts 3 + --cooldown-min 10 (crashloop -> alert-escalate, not loop). Decision state machine is byte-identical to the proven check_ib_gateway.py::decide. --auto-restart enabled in the unit (read-only API = low blast radius). Registered in test_s012 EXPECTED_SERVICES + deployment-ops.md; timer auto-enabled by install_systemd_units.sh. Tests: tests/test_check_web_api.py (9). POST-DEPLOY VERIFY: confirm the timer is active + a healthy probe in journalctl (healthy path); the restart path proves out on a real/induced wedge. Closes the gap where the only web-api recovery lever (GitHub vm-web-api-recover) is webhook-flaky.

NEW EVIDENCE of the gap this item targets: this session, /api/bot/order-packages + /api/bot/config returned HTTP 500 transiently around the ~06:19/07:16 trader restarts (the heavier bot routes erroring while /api/diag/*, the Streamlit routine-poll set, and the insights cache stayed 200). They SELF-RECOVERED (config 200 + 8 Streamlit routes 200 OK by 08:06-08:20, same web-api PID 386690 = no restart) — so no recovery lever was needed THIS time, but it confirms the heavier endpoints wobble around restarts and the only autonomous recover (vm-web-api-recover) is still webhook-flaky. The scripts/check_web_api.py VM-side watchdog this item proposes is still pending merge+deploy. Status unchanged (open).

updates.note: RESOLVED (already built, 2026-06-04+) — the ict-web-api-watchdog.{service,timer} exists and the timer was ACTIVE in the 06-25 snapshot: every 2 min it runs scripts/check_web_api.py --auto-restart --restart-after 2 --max-restarts 3 --cooldown-min 10, which probes :8001/api/health and runs scripts/ops/restart_web_api.sh (systemctl restart + health probe) on sustained failure — a VM-side autonomous self-heal independent of the GitHub vm-web-api-recover webhook lever. The item's premise ('no VM-side self-heal') is obsolete.

items.opened_by: claude (/health-review — Rina, post-deploy verify of BL-20260529-005)
items.source: The revived ict-health-snapshot writer's first live runs grade `accounts_api` as failing (M13 card 6/7, grade watch) while the trader is healthy + trading — surfaced verifying BL-20260529-005.
items.title: check_accounts_api fails in the standalone health-snapshot writer context (account_balance() returns None without the trader's live exchange clients) — card shows a perpetual accounts_api 'watch'
items.description: src/runtime/health.py::check_accounts_api reuses src.bot.data_loaders.account_balance() per account and WARNs on any None. In the trader PROCESS this works (it holds the live exchange/IB clients). But ict-health-snapshot.service runs write_health_snapshot.py as a SEPARATE oneshot that has no such clients (e.g. the local smoke logged 'account_balance(ib_paper): ib_insync is not installed' and Bybit balance needs a live socket), so account_balance() returns None -> accounts_api WARN -> the M13 health card sits at 6/7 'watch' with accounts_api failing even though the live trader's accounts are fine. This is a CONTEXT artifact of running the check outside the trader, not a real outage (verified: the trader dispatched real trade 2428 + is healthy). Net effect: the card never shows fully-green when it should. Fix options: (a) point check_accounts_api at the CONNECTION-FREE balance snapshot (runtime_logs/balance_snapshots.json — the same source /api/bot/accounts/balances uses, 'never opens an exchange socket'), so it reflects the last recorded balance instead of trying to connect; or (b) have the standalone writer skip/soften the accounts_api check (env flag) when not in-process; or (c) accept the warn as a known standalone limitation and document it. (a) is the cleanest — it makes the check connection-free + context-independent. Tier-1 code (observability); not a money-path change.
items.trigger_condition: Any /health-review run; or whenever the dashboard health card shows accounts_api failing while the live trader is confirmed healthy.
items.resolution_criteria: check_accounts_api reports ok in the standalone ict-health-snapshot writer context when the trader's accounts are healthy (e.g. by reading the connection-free balance snapshot), so the M13 health card grades `good`/ok on a healthy system instead of a perpetual accounts_api `watch`. Verify via insights_history (endpoint=health) after the fix deploys.

items.source: /health-review 2026-06-06: /api/diag/journalctl relay calls for ict-trader-live.service (lines=90) AND ict-liveness-watchdog.service (lines=70) both failed (run failed / curl --fail/--max-time), while journalctl?unit=ict-web-api.service (lines=120), status, config, journal, and log_file all succeeded.
items.title: Observability gap: /api/diag/journalctl fails for high-volume units (ict-trader-live) under the relay's curl --max-time, blocking restart-cause root-cause exactly when needed
items.description: When investigating the 2026-06-06 trader restart churn (BL-20260605-001), the diag journalctl pulls for ict-trader-live.service and ict-liveness-watchdog.service both returned 'run failed' via the vm-diag relay, while the lighter ict-web-api.service journalctl (and status/config/journal/log_file) succeeded. The trader logs a large volume per tick, so a journalctl tail of 70-120 lines likely exceeds the relay's `curl -sS --fail --max-time 40` budget (slow/large response -> curl exit 28/timeout -> workflow reports failure), OR the response byte size trips the same ~414KB cap noted elsewhere. Net effect: the single most useful pull for diagnosing a restart loop (the trader + watchdog journals) is the one that fails, so the autoheal-vs-crash-vs-IB-wedge trigger could not be captured this session. Candidate fixes (Tier-1/2): (a) raise the journalctl curl --max-time in vm-diag-snapshot.yml for journalctl paths; (b) add `since=`/`until=` narrowing support guidance so callers pull a small time-window instead of N lines; (c) add a dedicated lightweight 'restart-cause' diag (e.g. systemctl show --property=NRestarts,ActiveEnterTimestamp + the last N ExecStart lines) that returns small output; (d) gzip/cap journalctl output server-side. Until fixed, restart-cause investigation must use systemctl show timestamps + smaller line counts.
items.trigger_condition: Any /health-review run that needs the trader or watchdog journal to root-cause a restart/stall; or the Tuesday compliance-audit rotation hitting .github/workflows/.
items.resolution_criteria: A /api/diag/journalctl pull for ict-trader-live.service returns successfully via the relay at a useful line/time window (or a dedicated small restart-cause diag exists), so a future health-review can capture the autoheal/crash trigger without the pull failing.

items.source: Session 2026-06-07: built the cancel-queued-runs workflow (#2935/#2938) to clear 23 stale 'queued' Actions runs dated 2026-05-15. The runner (with actions:write) found all 23 but cancelled 0 — both `gh run cancel` and the force-cancel endpoint return HTTP 500 for every one.
items.title: 23 zombie 'queued' Actions runs (2026-05-15) are un-cancellable — GitHub returns HTTP 500 on both cancel and force-cancel
items.description: 23 workflow runs have been stuck in 'queued' since 2026-05-15 (ids 25906694928..25907269254). They never got a runner and consume no compute, but clutter the Actions list. The cancel-queued-runs workflow added this session (runner-path, since the PM-side MCP token lacks actions:write) lists them correctly and attempts normal cancel then force-cancel (POST repos/.../actions/runs/{id}/force-cancel) — both return `HTTP 500` for all 23, so the workflow cancels 0. A 500 (not 403/422) means it is a GitHub server-side failure on these specific orphaned runs; nothing a runner or the Actions UI (same endpoints) can do. The cancel-queued-runs workflow itself is sound and reusable for any FUTURE cancellable queued backlog — these particular runs are the exception. Resolution is GitHub-side: GitHub may garbage-collect them eventually, or GitHub Support can purge zombie runs. No code change in this repo will clear them. Note runs can't be DELETED either (the delete-run endpoint only accepts completed runs, not queued). Tracked here only so a future health-review doesn't re-investigate the same inert clutter.
items.trigger_condition: A future health-review or session notices the 23 stale queued runs and considers re-attempting cancellation, or wonders whether cancel-queued-runs is broken.
items.resolution_criteria: The 23 stale 2026-05-15 queued runs no longer appear in the Actions list (GitHub-side GC or Support purge). No repo change is expected to satisfy this; close when the runs are gone or the operator decides to leave them.

items.source: backfill-monitor-closed-pnl run #2990 (issue 2990) recovered 0/16 rows.
items.title: backfill-monitor-closed-pnl (and likely sibling backfill/inspect closed-pnl actions) can't reach Bybit creds in the system-action subprocess
items.description: The backfill-monitor-closed-pnl system-action skipped 100% of candidates (16/16, incl. demo bybit_1 rows #2482/#2484/#2490/#2492/#2494 and live rows) with 'account_closed_pnl_for_trade returned None'. The script's own heuristic warns the cause is 'exchange CREDENTIALS NOT REACHABLE from this process's env — silent auth failure'. _lib.sh::load_runtime_secrets sources REPO_DIR/.env; the live ict-trader-live.service reaches Bybit fine (actively trading), so the gap is specific to the system-action subprocess env (likely .env at REPO_DIR missing/incomplete vs the trader's systemd EnvironmentFile, and/or bybit_1 demo creds not present). This blocks any closed-pnl backfill/inspect action regardless of the BL-20260601-001 fix. Pre-existing; surfaced while remediating BL-20260601-001.
items.trigger_condition: Next time a closed-pnl backfill/inspect action is needed, or proactively to unblock BL-20260601-001 demo-row remediation.
items.resolution_criteria: backfill-monitor-closed-pnl (and inspect-closed-pnl) reach Bybit with valid creds from the system-action subprocess and recover/inspect at least the demo rows; root-cause the env mismatch between the wrapper's load_runtime_secrets and the trader's systemd env.
items.triage_note: 2026-06-08: opened from BL-20260601-001 deploy session. Needs VM diagnosis of REPO_DIR/.env contents + how ict-trader-live/ict-web-api load creds (systemd EnvironmentFile vs .env) + whether bybit_1 demo keys are configured.
items.resolution_note: MISDIAGNOSIS — NOT a credential gap. inspect-closed-pnl (Tier-1, read-only) on demo rows #2482 and #2490 (issues #2992, #2994) proved: bybit_client_for(cfg) built the demo client (demo=True → api-demo.bybit.com), get_closed_pnl executed WITHOUT error, and Bybit returned 0 records for each (within the 7-day window). So bybit_1 demo keys ARE configured and reachable from the system-action subprocess (load_runtime_secrets sources the same /home/ubuntu/ict-trading-bot/.env that ict-trader-live uses). True cause of the backfill 0/16: Bybit DEMO doesn't populate /v5/position/closed-pnl. The 'credentials not reachable' WARNING is a misleading 100%-skip heuristic. Follow-up tracked in BL-20260608-DEMOPNL.

items.source: BL-20260608-CRED diagnosis: Bybit demo /v5/position/closed-pnl returns 0 records (verified #2482/#2490).
items.title: Bybit DEMO realised PnL is unrecoverable from closed-pnl; build a demo-PnL source + fix the misleading backfill warning
items.description: bybit_1 (demo) closed trades carry pnl=NULL because Bybit demo trading does not populate /v5/position/closed-pnl — verified via inspect-closed-pnl on #2482 and #2490 (0 records, creds OK). (1) backfill_monitor_closed_pnl.py prints 'credentials not reachable' on any 100%-skip — soften to distinguish client-None/auth-exception from a successful empty response. (2) OPERATOR REQUESTED building a real demo-PnL source: recon shipped (inspect-closed-pnl now also dumps /v5/execution/list + /v5/order/history) to determine which endpoint the demo venue populates; then implement a demo fallback in account_closed_pnl_for_trade (reconstruct exit avg-price + realised PnL from closing executions) + backfill. If demo populates NEITHER endpoint, fall back to computing PnL from entry/exit fills captured at close time.
items.trigger_condition: In progress (operator-requested 2026-06-08). Next: run the enhanced inspect-closed-pnl on a demo trade post-deploy to see execution-list/order-history output.
items.resolution_criteria: Demo closed trades carry non-NULL realised pnl from a working source; AND the backfill warning distinguishes empty-result from auth-failure.
items.triage_note: 2026-06-08: opened from BL-20260608-CRED diagnosis; recon tooling shipped same session. | 2026-06-08 RECON + DEFERRED: enhanced inspect-closed-pnl run on demo #2490 (issue #2997) — /v5/position/closed-pnl=0 rows, BUT /v5/execution/list=6 rows and /v5/order/history=3 rows ARE populated on demo. Key finding: the demo account NETS every strategy fill into a single position (each htf 'trade' is an incremental Sell with closedSize=0; the whole ~0.505 short was flattened by ONE Buy @63483.4), so there is NO per-trade close to attribute — a real demo-PnL source needs a position-accounting/allocation layer (FIFO/avg across constituent opens), not just a different endpoint. Sub-findings: demo opens carry no SL/TP (reduceOnly=False, empty stopOrderType) so positions accumulate vs live bybit_2 which closes per-trade; and the reconciler marked these closed while the net position was still open. OPERATOR DECISION 2026-06-08: DEFER — added to ROADMAP.md 'Items Under Consideration'. Recon tooling (inspect-closed-pnl execution/order dump) is shipped (PR #2996). The backfill false-'creds' warning softening (sub-item 1) also remains open. | 2026-06-08 SL/TP ROOT-CAUSE INVESTIGATED (operator-requested): root cause confirmed = one-way-mode position-level SL/TP OVERWRITE on same-direction re-entry + reconciler closing on NET-flat (not per-trade) + monocle release → accumulation cycle. SL/TP ARE attached on demo entries (execute.py:659-660) — CORRECTION to the earlier 'demo opens carry no SL/TP' note, which was an over-read (attached position-level SL/TP don't show as separate stop ORDERS in order/history). Evidence: audit_query #3002 (33 sell+28 short htf signals in 9h, only 3 bar_debounce_blocked), inspect #2997 (incremental Sell opens → one Buy 0.505 close), code (no positionIdx/hedge anywhere = one-way; order_monitor.py:2017-2029 net-flat close). Money-relevant on live bybit_2 too (currently masked). Full analysis + Tier-3 remediation proposal (recommend Option A: suppress same-dir re-entry while position open + harden reconciler against transient net-flat; behind a kill-switch + walk-forward) in docs/audits/position-netting-sltp-2026-06-08.md. Awaiting operator decision on the proposal before any Tier-3 code. | 2026-06-08 Option A SHIPPED + LIVE (PRs #3023 guard + #3031 account-scope, deployed f81cf9b): the position-netting / per-trade SL/TP guard is now active on both Bybit accounts (POSITION_NETTING_GUARD_ENABLED=true, POSITION_NETTING_GUARD_ACCOUNTS=bybit_1,bybit_2) after a demo-only soak on bybit_1. Monocle suppresses same-direction netted adds; reconciler requires an extra grace tick before closing on a net-flat snapshot. This restores per-trade=per-position, the prerequisite for closed-pnl to repopulate as positions close discretely. Validated: 36 unit tests + net-vs-suppress walk-forward (real BTCUSDT 5m, 4 windows, no trade-count/win-rate regression + lower max-DD). Kept OPEN because the bybit_1 DEMO venue still does not populate /v5/position/closed-pnl, so demo realised-PnL recovery for already-netted historical rows remains limited — forward demo trades should now close per-trade and attribute. Rollback = unset POSITION_NETTING_GUARD_ENABLED (or narrow POSITION_NETTING_GUARD_ACCOUNTS) + restart, no redeploy.

updates.note: TIER-2 PREP → PROPOSED (awaiting operator OK before ship). Root cause: Bybit's closed-pnl endpoint doesn't return reliable per-trade records for the DEMO account (bybit_1 testnet); _bybit_closed_pnl_lookup returns shared/mismatched records (the sibling BL-20260620-CLOSEDPNL-LOOKUP-MISMATCH-DEMO: two SOL paper trades booked an identical -864.45). PROPOSED FIX (Tier-2, paper-only, real-money untouched): for is_demo Bybit trades, route realised-PnL resolution through the LOCAL compute path (src/runtime/local_pnl.py, multiplier-aware via config/instruments.yaml contract_value_usd) instead of the Bybit closed-pnl lookup — mirroring the local_compute path already used for IBKR/Alpaca/OANDA realised PnL (order_monitor._sweep_local_pnl_for_unpriced). Net: demo realised PnL becomes entry/exit/qty-derived (deterministic, no shared-record bleed); the misleading backfill that pulls a wrong Bybit record for demo is bypassed. Scope: the demo branch of _bybit_closed_pnl_lookup + the backfill. Needs operator OK (touches the close/PnL resolution path, paper accounts).

RESOLVED (operator-approved build) — account_closed_pnl_for_trade now returns None EARLY for demo accounts, the single chokepoint both demo-PnL writers funnel through (the monitor close path + _sweep_pending_pnl_from_bybit). Demo's unreliable Bybit closed-pnl lookup (shared/mismatched testnet records — the BL-20260620 -864.45 double-book) is skipped; realised PnL now resolves via the universal local-compute sweep (_sweep_local_pnl_for_unpriced: entry×exit×qty×contract_value_usd, multiplier-aware, or mark-to-market exit) — the same path IBKR/Alpaca/OANDA use. Removed the now-dead demo wide-fallback wiring at the call site. Real-money Bybit untouched (strict broker-truth contract). 3 new tests (demo→None + client-not-called; truthy variants; live→reaches client) pass; py_compile clean. On bot PR #4503; ships on merge+deploy. Sibling BL-20260620-CLOSEDPNL-LOOKUP-MISMATCH-DEMO is subsumed by this (demo no longer queries the lookup at all).

items.source: Session-end observation: PR #3007 (branch claude/vibrant-babbage-9Wbls, head SHA e286c4ea) shows total_count=0 on get_status for 7+ hours after push. No check runs fired at all.
items.title: PR #3007 CI never triggered after push (0 check runs in 7+ h on SHA e286c4ea)
items.description: After opening PR #3007 against main from claude/vibrant-babbage-9Wbls, mcp__github__pull_request_read method=get_status returned state='pending', total_count=0 across 4+ polls over a 7-hour window. No workflow runs reference the head SHA in the Actions list for the period. Either (a) a path/branch filter excluded this PR's changes, (b) the push event was accepted but didn't fan out to workflow triggers, or (c) the runners were idle/wedged. Worth investigating because the same trigger anomaly would silently let other PRs from claude/* branches merge without CI. First step next session: check Actions for any run referencing e286c4ea; if absent, inspect the workflow trigger filters in .github/workflows/*.yml against the changed paths in #3007. An empty commit on the branch would kick the trigger as a workaround if the configs are correct but state is stale.
items.resolution_note: Not a CI bug. All 11 checks ran green on commit 1 (e286c4ea); commit 2 (61c64816) had 0 runs because the PR was dirty — the backlog-JSON appends conflicted with main, which blocks GitHub from building the merge ref, so the synchronize push triggered nothing. get_status/get_check_runs report only the head SHA, hence total_count=0. The path/branch-filter hypothesis is ruled out (no CI workflow has a path filter). Fixed by resolving the conflict and pushing 61c6481..2be6549; 11 checks re-fired green; #3007 merged. See docs/audits/s9-yz-shadow-promotion-2026-06-09.md §1.
check_log: 2026-06-10 /health-review: CPU half RE-CONFIRMED resolved — vm_health cpu=0% (mem 67%, disk 36.8%) across the status pull; the regime-bar-scoring fix (PR #3082) is holding, no recurrence. liveness-watchdog.timer active/armed. Item remains open ONLY on (2) autoheal-on-real-hang verification + (3) 48h no-multi-min-stall soak. Priority low.

2026-06-10 07:07Z LIVE INCIDENT (recurrence, COLD-START variant): a trader restart (07:02:50, enabling the netting guard) re-wedged the VM — cpu 72%->100%, heartbeat froze at 07:03:49, no tick since 07:01:56. Root cause IDENTIFIED + distinct from MB-20260609-001: the regime_bar_scoring fetch-gate + dedup caches are PER-PROCESS and EMPTY on a fresh restart, so the first post-restart tick fetched every (symbol,timeframe) group (incl. blocking IB/MES fetches) AND scored all ~20 shadow heads in ONE synchronous mega-tick on the main loop -> heartbeat (written between ticks) froze. MB-20260609-001 bounded steady-state per-tick cost but NOT the cold-start burst. Operator-approved remediation: set-env REGIME_BAR_SCORING_DISABLED=1 + restart (#3211, 07:10:56Z) -> cpu 100->52% + heartbeat resumed advancing (07:12:52, label running) = recovered. FIX shipped this PR: REGIME_BAR_SCORING_BUDGET_S (default 6s) caps per-call wall time, deferring remaining whole groups to the next tick. Regime-bar-scoring is DISABLED on the VM pending the fix deploy + re-enable (see BL-20260610-002).

check_log.note: 2026-06-11 /health-review: clean window. status (#3382) cpu=0%, mem 65.9%, heartbeat running (31s), tick fresh; trader restarted 14:40Z (deploy) cleanly WITH regime bar scoring on (BL-20260610-002 resolved on this evidence). No stall occurred in the window, so criterion (2) (autoheal fires on a real >8-min hang) remains unobservable; (3) 48h-no-wedge soak progressing. Keep open on (2)+(3).

items.source: Self-inflicted incident: vm-web-api-recover run 27150313822 (issue #3026) restarted a healthy ict-web-api.service, then the wrapper's 15-second post-restart health probe failed because cold-start exceeded 15 s, leaving the unit in unknown state and emitting a misleading 'unit did not return to a serving state' error.
items.title: vm-web-api-recover wrapper's 15 s post-restart health probe is too tight for cold-start of ict-web-api.service
items.description: scripts/ops/restart_web_api.sh probes /api/health for ~15 s after the systemctl restart returns. Observed timeline 16:02:23–16:03:01 UTC: systemd stopped uvicorn cleanly (16:02:23–16:02:41), check_data_dir.sh ran (16:02:43–16:02:44), systemd reported 'Started ICT Trading Bot — dashboard API' at 16:02:44, but uvicorn didn't bind port 8001 within the wrapper's 15 s probe window (curl exit 7 at 16:03:01). At 16:13:49 /api/diag/status returned 200 OK — so uvicorn DID come up, just slowly. The 15 s probe ceiling needs widening (suggest 60 s) or the wrapper should poll until ExecStartPost completes systemd-side. Secondary issue: the wrapper's failure message implies the unit is wedged, which led this session to escalate to the operator unnecessarily. Both behaviours wasted operator attention. Lower priority than BL-20260609-001's CI trigger bug but worth a small PR.

[a] ibkr_mes_pull.jsonl last wrote 2026-06-01 — but that is the OFF-VM dataset-history pull, NOT the live per-tick candle fetch; the live 1d strategies evaluate, so the live candle round-trip works. Treating [b] as the canonical daily proof.

Only backtest sweeps were stale because the generator scripts/ops/run_backtest_sweep.sh is on-demand (no timer/cron), last run 2026-05-17. Recommendation: add a weekly ict-backtest-sweep.timer if recurring freshness is wanted; otherwise accept as on-demand by design. See docs/audits/s9-yz-shadow-promotion-2026-06-09.md §4.

items.source: Full-system audit (3-repo structural-compliance + bug/optimization sweep, 2026-06-10). Deferred from the audit PRs (bot #3233, ict-trader-dashboard#88, ict-trader-android#43) to keep those diffs scoped/safe.
items.title: regime_bar_scoring budget can starve later predictor groups (no per-tick rotation)
items.description: emit_regime_bar_predictions (src/runtime/regime_bar_scoring.py:~348-359) iterates groups in fixed insertion order and breaks when the per-tick wall-clock budget (REGIME_BAR_SCORING_BUDGET_S) is hit; the budget check runs before the fetch-gate skip, so the same groups are always considered first each tick. Pathological case: one group whose single fetch always consumes the whole budget could indefinitely defer the groups after it (low practical risk - an already-fetched group is a cheap continue on most ticks). Fix: rotate the group start index per tick, or order by most-overdue-for-a-fetch. Observe-only path, Tier-1.

Fix: cache a ro connection across decisions, or fold the per-account reads into one grouped query. Observe-only writer, Tier-1, minor.

items.source: MES restore on the isolated-gateway topology (2026-06-10). Connectivity fixed via the OCI SL rule (#3313); the post-connect liveness probe then false-tripped the breaker. Shipped an opt-out (PR #3318) and disabled the probe via env to restore MES; this is the follow-up to do it properly.
items.title: Re-enable the IB liveness probe: retry-tolerant fix shipped, live env flip (IB_PROBE_TIMEOUT_S=5) still pending
items.description: The IB post-connect liveness probe (IBClient._probe_liveness, src/units/accounts/ib_client.py) runs loop.run_until_complete(asyncio.wait_for(ib.reqCurrentTimeAsync(), IB_PROBE_TIMEOUT_S)) on the persistent loop. Over the isolated gateway (10.0.0.251, socat-relayed cross-host) this never resolves even though the connection is fully healthy (connect + 'Logged on server v176' + 'Market data farm connection is OK' + 'Synchronization complete', and the read-path client which skips the probe works) - verified timing out at BOTH 5s and 15s while sync completes in <1s. As a workaround MES is currently running with the probe DISABLED via IB_PROBE_TIMEOUT_S=0 on the live VM (the <=0 skip added in PR #3318); IB_FETCH_TIMEOUT_S=8 is the backstop. Proper fix: make the probe resolve over this path - likely use ib_insync's sync wrapper ib.reqCurrentTime() (which drives the loop the way connectAsync does) instead of the manual run_until_complete(reqCurrentTimeAsync()), or otherwise pump the reader during the probe; add a regression test mirroring tests/test_ib_integration.py TestEventLoopResilience. Then set-env IB_PROBE_TIMEOUT_S back to 5 (restores the cheap dead-session catch on top of the per-fetch timeout). Related: BL-20260610-004 Fix 3 (IBMarketData.get_ohlcv should also consult the breaker). Tier-3 (live IB connection path); PR + verify on the live gateway.
items.resolution_note: IB_PROBE_TIMEOUT_S set back to 5 on the live VM via the set-env system-action (issue #5685) and live-verified against a real forced cold reconnect (vm-ib-gateway-recover, issue #5686: gateway container restarted 09:14:25, IBC login completed 09:14:48). In the minutes after, the retry-tolerant probe hit four separate cold-TCP-flow misses (MES, MGC x2, MHG) between 09:17:42-09:18:10, each logging "attempt 1/2 timed out ... retrying once after 1.5s" and recovering cleanly on the retry with real candle data fetched right after — zero circuit-breaker trips, zero IBConnectionError/ConnectionError in that window (confirmed via journalctl pulls, issues #5688 + #5689). One genuine breaker trip DID fire at 09:14:26, the moment the gateway container was actually down mid-restart (ConnectionError: Socket disconnect) — correct behavior, not a false trip, since it was not a first-attempt asyncio.TimeoutError.

check_log.by: claude (IB pipeline stability review follow-up, 2026-07-06, operator-requested)
check_log.note: Shipped a code fix (PR TBD): IBClient._probe_liveness now retries the reqCurrentTime round-trip ONCE after IB_PROBE_RETRY_GAP_S (default 1.5s) before condemning the connection, instead of failing on a single timeout. Rationale: the probe fires as the FIRST message on a freshly-established connection, right after connect() — a cold TCP flow over the cross-host socat relay can miss that one round-trip even on a genuinely healthy session (every OTHER call — reqHistoricalData, accountSummary — is proven reliable over this exact relay per today's live selftest + diag pulls). A real wedge still never answers either attempt, so detection is unaffected; only a one-off cold-start miss on a healthy session is absorbed. Added 2 regression tests (tests/test_ib_integration.py: cold-miss-then-recovers stays connected + breaker closed; still-hung-after-retry trips the breaker, bounded). This does NOT yet restore protection on the live VM — IB_PROBE_TIMEOUT_S is still 0 (disabled) in the live .env. Re-enabling it (set to 5, restart ict-trader-live.service) is a deliberate follow-up step that should be verified against a real cold connect over the relay first, not flipped blind alongside the code merge.

RESOLVED + live-flipped. IB_PROBE_TIMEOUT_S=5 is enabled on ict-bot-arm; the retry-tolerant probe (ee522ca) has been proven against a real cold gateway restart with zero false breaker trips. See resolution_note for the full verification trail.

Cross-ref: today's cron health snapshot accounts_api check reports oanda_practice + alpaca_paper API probes failing (M15 soak watch item). Carry; the hourly snapshot writer needs a dedicated fix session.

evidence_log: 2026-06-13 (operator-directed root-cause): ROOT CAUSE = DATA_DIR drop-in coverage gap. ict-hourly-snapshot.service (scripts/send_hourly_now.py -> hourly_report.account_snapshots() -> _save_balance_snapshots -> runtime_logs/balance_snapshots.json) is NOT in install_systemd_units.sh's data-dir.conf auto-install list (which covers trader-live/web-api/telegram-bot/health-snapshot/claude-bridge/insights) and sets no Environment=DATA_DIR in its unit. fix_data_dir.sh STRIPS DATA_DIR from .env, so the hourly writer resolves runtime_logs_dir() to the REPO-relative path (paths._resolve_root unset->repo fallback) and writes the fresh balance file to <repo>/runtime_logs, while ict-web-api (has the drop-in) READS /data/bot-data/runtime_logs -> frozen at the data-dir migration (~5/25). Identical writer/reader path-split as the web-api 2026-05-11 incident and the ict-health-snapshot BL-20260529-005 fix. No code change 5/22-5/29 touched the writer chain (rules out a code regression); confirmed entirely from in-repo evidence (drop-in coverage list + unit file + _resolve_root). FIX STAGED (branch claude/health-strategy-ml-review-ceaa21, PR #3466): (1) install_systemd_units.sh installs data-dir.conf for ict-hourly-snapshot.service (mirrors the health-snapshot block); (2) diag.py _CANONICAL_UNITS gains ict-hourly-snapshot + ict-health-snapshot service+timer so the writer is observable (the 3-week stall hid because nothing watched it). Tier-2 deploy via pull-and-deploy pending operator OK; verify post-deploy that /api/bot/accounts/balances age_seconds drops + the hourly unit shows active on /api/diag/services.

updates.note: Superseded by the WC-5 DB-authoritative balances path. /api/bot/accounts/balances is now sourced from trade_journal.db::balance_snapshots (json_fallback only); db_info shows balance_snapshots = 6484 rows (healthy, actively written), so the dashboard Accounts tab + M15 balance probe are no longer blinded by the stale runtime_logs/balance_snapshots.json. The legacy JSON-writer staleness is no longer load-bearing. Closing as superseded; if the JSON-writer fix is still wanted as hygiene, re-open scoped to that file only.

items.source: Candidate observability/resilience-unit install audit (ict-bot-arm).
items.title: ict-shadow-log-rotate.service has no EnvironmentFile -> rotates repo-relative shadow log, not the DATA_DIR one
items.description: deploy/ict-shadow-log-rotate.service sets only ExecStart=python3 scripts/ops/rotate_shadow_log.py --gzip with NO EnvironmentFile and NO Environment=DATA_DIR. Without DATA_DIR the rotator resolves runtime_logs() to the repo-relative path and would rotate <repo>/runtime_logs/shadow_predictions.jsonl (an inert/stale leftover under the DATA_DIR regime) instead of the live /data/bot-data/runtime_logs/shadow_predictions.jsonl. Pre-existing (affects the block-volume micro too, not just the candidate). Consequence: the real shadow-prediction log is never rotated and can grow unbounded; the timer is currently NOT installed on the candidate (deliberately deferred this session to avoid improvising on the money box). Fix: add EnvironmentFile=-/home/ubuntu/ict-trading-bot/.env to the unit (so it inherits DATA_DIR), OR add it to install_systemd_units.sh's data-dir drop-in list (now mount-aware), then enable the timer on the candidate. Tier-2 (unit-file + service enable).
items.resolution: Fixed: rotate_shadow_log.py now resolves its default --log via src.utils.paths.runtime_logs_dir() (DATA_DIR-aware, stdlib fallback), and ict-shadow-log-rotate added to install_systemd_units.sh data-dir drop-in list (mount-aware flavor) so the unit receives DATA_DIR. Rotates the real /data/bot-data log now.

items.source: ict-health-snapshot first run on the Ampere candidate (ict-bot-arm).
items.title: ib_insync not installed on the Ampere live candidate -> IB/MES balance + execution path unavailable
items.description: The first health-snapshot run on the candidate logged: account_balance(ib_paper): 'ib_insync is not installed ... pip install ib_insync (or ib_async) to enable the IB execution path.' The Bybit money path is live on the candidate (retCode 0, open positions), but the IB/MES leg (ib_paper account -> IB Gateway VM 10.0.0.251) appears unwired: the venv lacks ib_insync/ib_async. Need to confirm whether MES is expected to trade from the candidate and, if so, install the IB client lib in the candidate venv + verify connectivity to the gateway VM over the private subnet. Until then MES is dark on the candidate. Tier-2 (dependency install on the live VM + connectivity verification).
items.resolution: FALSE ALARM on the premise: ib_insync 0.9.86 IS installed in the trader venv and MES/MGC/MHG run every tick with live IB data (verified, e.g. MES 7497.5, MGC 4240.3). The MES leg is NOT dark. The "ib_insync not installed" line came from ict-health-snapshot running under /usr/bin/python3 (system), not the venv -> see BL-20260614-HEALTHSNAP-PY. Investigation surfaced a real, pre-existing bug: the order monitor fetched IB-futures candles from Bybit -> candles=None -> monitor() short-circuited, so bot-side exits never ran for IB positions (IBKR bracket still held the stop). Fixed in PR #3597 (per-symbol connector routing).

items.source: Spun off from BL-20260614-CANDIDATE-IB investigation.
items.title: ict-health-snapshot runs under /usr/bin/python3 (system), not the trader venv -> ib_paper balance always 'unavailable'
items.description: deploy/ict-health-snapshot.service ExecStart uses /usr/bin/python3, which lacks the venv packages (ib_insync etc.). write_health_snapshot.py's account_balance(ib_paper) then logs 'ib_insync is not installed' and reports the ib_paper balance unavailable -- even though the trader venv has ib_insync 0.9.86 and trades IB fine. Cosmetic but a scary false-positive. Fix: point the unit's ExecStart at /home/ubuntu/ict-trading-bot/.venv/bin/python. Tier-2 (unit-file).

items.source: Live diag snapshot (issue #3720) showed ict-heartbeat.service state=failed; journalctl (#3721) gave the cause.
items.title: ict-heartbeat.service dead since the Ampere cutover -> daily operator digest never sent (stale .env.live EnvironmentFile)
items.description: The daily 13:00 UTC operator status digest (deploy/ict-heartbeat.service -> scripts/daily_heartbeat.py) has failed every trigger since the 2026-06-14 cutover: 'Failed to load environment files: No such file or directory'. ROOT CAUSE: ict-heartbeat.service was the ONLY unit pointing at a non-optional EnvironmentFile=.../.env.live; every sibling unit uses .env, which is the canonical live-creds file the cutover replicated. No .env.live exists on the clean candidate -> the oneshot hard-fails before daily_heartbeat.py runs. Pre-existing latent bug the clean rebuild exposed. Not money-path-critical (the per-minute liveness watchdog is a separate, healthy unit) but the operator's daily digest has been silently dark since cutover. FIX in PR #3724 (branch claude/vm-cutover-audit-retrospective-kkcj3y): point the unit at -.../.env (optional + canonical) and make daily_heartbeat.py prefer .env (with .env.live back-compat). Resolves on merge + git-sync deploy. Tier-2 (unit file).
items.resolution: Resolved alongside BL-20260615-HEARTBEAT-SVC: the 2026-06-23 daily digest arrived, confirming the heartbeat unit recovered post-Ampere-cutover.

items.source: Operator: 'put that on the health log for tomorrow' (micro decommission).
items.title: Decommission the retired x86 micro (158.178.210.252) — operator-approved; terminate-instance returned not_found, needs the OCI display name (or an OCID-based terminate path)
items.description: Operator approved termination 2026-06-15. Cutover to ict-bot-arm verified healthy (memory 9.5%, bybit_2 reconciling, no ErrCode 10010), so the soak is clean. ATTEMPT 2026-06-15 (issue #3725): fired terminate-instance with display_name='instance-20260414-1555' (the micro's OS hostname, recovered from its own pre-cutover systemd journal, issue #3454) -> result 'not_found'. The exact-match + single-match guard in scripts/ops/terminate_instance.py correctly refused to act, so NO instance was touched. not_found means the micro's OCI *display name* differs from its OS hostname (OCI default hostnames are instance-<date>-<time>, but a custom display name may have been set), OR the micro was already terminated. BLOCKER: cannot enumerate OCI instance display names autonomously from a web session (no read-only list-instances workflow exists on main; adding/triggering one needs a merge to main). The micro is stopped + disabled + Bybit-frozen + inert (zombie already killed, #3684) + $0 (separate AMD Always-Free allocation), so deferral is safe. RESOLUTION PATH (either): (a) operator pastes the micro's OCI-console Display Name (the stopped instance at 158.178.210.252, not ict-bot-arm/ict-trainer-vm/ict-ib-gateway) and Claude re-fires terminate-instance; or (b) add an instance_id: input to terminate-instance.yml + terminate_instance.py so the micro's OCID (captured in the cutover-live run logs) can be used directly, surviving any rename. Runbook: docs/runbooks/live-vm-migration-ampere.md.
items.resolution: Terminated the retired x86 micro (display_name=ict-bot, OCID ocid1.instance.oc1.eu-paris-1.anrwiljrnpsiupacbpuojiksc3dl4twmqlxndtc36jgx5sjq3452vflqr32q, 158.178.210.252) on 2026-06-16 via terminate-instance BY OCID (lifecycle TERMINATED confirmed, issue #3780). The earlier hostname-derived attempt returned not_found because the micro's OS hostname (instance-20260414-1555) != its OCI display name (ict-bot); the terminate-instance workflow gained a read-only mode:list + an instance_id (OCID) path to remove the human from instance ID-ing/termination (PR #3777). Captured in the new vm-migration skill.

items.source: git-sync enablement on the candidate (split-topology wart).
items.title: install_systemd_units.sh blanket enable --now activates IB-gateway timers on the trader box
items.description: The timer-enable loop at the end of scripts/install_systemd_units.sh does `enable --now` on EVERY deploy/*.timer it finds, including ict-ib-gateway-watchdog.timer and ict-ib-gateway-reset.timer. Post gateway-isolation (2026-06-10) those belong on the dedicated IB-gateway VM, NOT the trader -- on the trader they probe a non-existent local container / unreachable IB and spam alerts. Pre-existing design wart (predates the split topology). Worked around on the candidate this session by `systemctl mask`-ing both timers so the blanket enable is a no-op for them. Durable fix: make the enable loop topology-aware (skip gateway timers unless the host IS the gateway VM, via a marker file or unit allowlist). Tier-2 (deploy tooling).

items.opened_by: claude (operator-reported bybit IP-mismatch errors after the Ampere cutover)
items.source: Operator pasted live ict-trading-bot ERROR logs: bybit_get_positions_failed / bybit_get_order_status_failed -> 'Unmatched IP, please check your API key's bound IP addresses (ErrCode: 10010)' on account=bybit_2, starting ~18:37 UTC (after the 2026-06-14 live->Ampere cutover).
items.title: Bybit API-key bound-IP allowlist not updated for the new Ampere live VM -> ErrCode 10010 on bybit_2 (real-money money-path outage)
items.description: ROOT CAUSE: the live->Ampere cutover (2026-06-14) moved the trader's egress IP from the micro 158.178.210.252 to the Ampere VM ict-bot-arm 141.145.193.91, but the Bybit API key BYBIT_API_KEY_2 (real money, mainnet api.bybit.com) still has its bound-IP allowlist set to the OLD micro IP. Bybit rejects every authed request from the new IP with ErrCode 10010 'Unmatched IP'. Effect: bybit_2 get_positions + get_order_status (and almost certainly order placement) fail every tick -> the real-money account is blind (cannot reconcile / monitor / open new trades). Native exchange-side SL/TP brackets still protect open positions (they fire on Bybit, not via the client). The cutover checklist in docs/runbooks/live-vm-migration-ampere.md NEVER included 'update the Bybit API-key IP allowlist' -- documented now as follow-up item 6. FIX (operator-only, broker-side, no workflow can do it): on Bybit -> API Management, add 141.145.193.91 to the bound-IP list for BYBIT_API_KEY_2 (keep 158.178.210.252 during the rollback soak; drop it at micro decommission). Also check/repeat for BYBIT_API_KEY_1 (demo, api-demo.bybit.com). NOTE the new VM's egress IP is its OCI public IP (1:1 NAT) = 141.145.193.91; if Bybit still rejects after binding, confirm the exact egress empirically before re-editing. Tier: operator broker-side action + Tier-1 doc fix (this entry + runbook item 6).
items.resolution: RESOLVED 2026-06-15. The operator bound 141.145.193.91 on the Bybit key, but binding the IP is necessary-not-sufficient: the running ict-trader-live + ict-web-api processes hold BYBIT_API_KEY_2 in os.environ from their last start and never re-read creds at runtime, so they kept failing 10010 until RESTARTED (restart-bot-service + vm-web-api-recover reloaded the corrected .env; new-VM processes then authenticate retCode 0 OK, verified by reproducing the exact call inside the trader's /proc/<pid>/environ). LESSON added to runbook item 6: after rebinding the IP on Bybit, RESTART the trader + web-api. NOTE: a SEPARATE, longer-lived source of the same 10010 spam was the zombie bot fleet left running on the retired micro (158.178.210.252) calling Bybit from its now-unbound IP -- tracked + resolved as BL-20260615-MICRO-ZOMBIE.

items.opened_by: claude (operator-driven live IBKR login validation session)
items.source: Operator wanted to validate the real-money IBKR live login + 2FA works (ib_live, U25907316). Built vm-ib-gateway-live-login-test (throwaway read-only live container on the gateway VM, paper untouched). Four runs (#3631/#3633/#3635/#3640) all reached IBC 'Authenticating...' and timed out with NO push. Operator's IBKR-Mobile screenshot showed the IB Key 'Generate Response' (challenge/response) screen.
items.title: Live IBKR login blocked: bot user's IB Key 2FA is challenge/response (Seamless Authentication OFF) -> headless gateway can't complete a live login
items.description: Paper logins are 2FA-exempt so ib_paper is unaffected and healthy. LIVE logins require 2FA; the bot user's IB Key is in CHALLENGE/RESPONSE mode (Seamless OFF), so IBKR issues no push and the headless gateway (Xvfb) can neither read the on-screen challenge nor type the response -> hangs at 'Authenticating...' and times out. Pinning IBC SecondFactorDevice='IB Key Security via IBKR Mobile' changed nothing (no device-selection dialog ever appeared), confirming the block is upstream of device selection (no push challenge issued). FIX (operator-only, broker-side): enable SEAMLESS (push) authentication for the bot user in IBKR (account-specific; confirm exact steps with IBKR client services -- it is NOT the IBKR-Mobile Security page; involves Client Portal Secure Login + the bot user being registered for IBKR-Mobile push). Seamless push is REQUIRED for live anyway because the gateway re-logs in unattended daily after IBKR's reset; challenge/response cannot be automated. Then re-run via the vm-ib-gateway-live-login-test label and tap the push. NOT URGENT: ib_live is mode:dry_run (not trading); this is a pre-flight for a future capability. Tooling is built + verified. Full runbook: docs/runbooks/ib-live-login-2fa.md. Tier: operator broker-side action + Tier-1 doc (this entry + runbook).

items.source: After BL-20260614-BYBIT-IP was resolved (trader+web-api restarted on the new Ampere VM, key verified), bybit_2 ErrCode 10010 'Unmatched IP' Telegram alerts kept firing every few minutes (fresh ErrTimes, e.g. 13:21:22, 14:21:56). Exhaustive diags proved the NEW VM (141.145.193.91) was clean: every process holds the working key lDRH...CX, reproduce (even inside the trader's exact /proc/<pid>/environ) returns retCode 0 OK, no proxy, and none of the failures appeared in the new VM's outcomes.jsonl.
items.title: Retired x86 micro (158.178.210.252) left running a ZOMBIE bot fleet post-cutover -> spammed bybit_2 ErrCode 10010 from its unbound IP + served stale data to the Android app
items.description: ROOT CAUSE: the 2026-06-14 live->Ampere cutover never actually shut the bot down on the retired micro. ict-trader-live + ict-web-api + a full fleet of TIMERS (ict-hourly-snapshot, ict-insights-generator(-strategies), ict-heartbeat, ict-health-snapshot, ict-web-api-watchdog, ict-git-sync, ict-liveness-watchdog, ...) kept running on the micro with the real bybit_2 key (its .env was unchanged). The micro egresses from 158.178.210.252, which was dropped from the Bybit API-key allowlist at cutover (only 141.145.193.91 is bound), so every authed bybit_2 call (get_positions via the timers' account_snapshots, get_wallet_balance) returned ErrCode 10010 and the micro's outcomes.report fanned it to the SHARED Telegram channel -- indistinguishable from a new-VM failure. The micro's web-api also still answered :8001, so the Android app (default BOT_API_URL = the micro) read live status+MES off it while BTCUSDT came back no_data (micro Bybit-frozen) -- the 'app shows data from the micro' symptom. WHY IT WAS HARD: api_call failures route to outcomes.jsonl + Telegram, NOT stdout, so journalctl was blind; and the new VM always reproduced OK, so the source had to be a different machine. RESOLUTION (operator-approved 'stop services, keep VM for rollback'): built .github/workflows/stop-micro-zombie.yml (issue label stop-micro-zombie) -- SSHes the micro and stop+disables ALL ict-*.timer + ict-*.service units (two passes; the web-api-watchdog + git-sync.timer resurrected the trader through the first two narrow passes -- they MUST be disabled first). Verified fully dark + zero residual python bot processes at 14:45 and again at 14:53 (stable). Micro VM left UP as the rollback target. PROCESS GAP: the cutover/decommission runbook (live-vm-migration-ampere.md Phase 3/4) must STOP+DISABLE the entire ict-* unit fleet on the old VM (or power it off) at cutover -- not just leave it 'stopped'. Rollback = re-enable the micro units + re-add 158.178.210.252 to the Bybit allowlist. Tier: ops action DONE + Tier-1 doc (this entry + runbook Phase 3/4 update).

items.source: Live-trade management contract (ARCHITECTURE-CANONICAL 2026-06-16 row). P3 wired IB/Alpaca close; trailing-SL modify was deferred (operator decision 2: close-first).
items.title: Wire IB/Alpaca SL/TP modify (trailing-stop) to the strategy verdict path
items.description: Strategy {'sl'|'tp'} verdicts still return unsupported_op:modify for IB/Alpaca (EXCHANGE_MANAGEMENT_CAPS has close+open_positions, not modify). IB: re-arm the GTC OCA bracket at the new level via IBClient.place_protective (cancel old + place new). Alpaca: replace bracket legs. Then add 'modify' to the caps. e.g. MGC #2597's trailing-SL ratchet currently no-ops. Baseline-ON, no kill-switch.
items.trigger_condition: Next live-trade-management sprint (S-LIVETRADE-MGMT-S2).
items.resolution_criteria: account_supports_management(ib_cfg,'modify') True; _send_modify_to_exchange reaches IB/Alpaca; tests pin Bybit unchanged + IB re-arm.
items.resolution: Merged #3801 (S-LIVETRADE-MGMT-S2). IBClient.modify_protective (cancel resting OCA legs + re-arm GTC OCA via place_protective) + AlpacaClient.modify_protective (PATCH the resting bracket legs) wired through execute.modify_open_order (side/qty/cur_sl/cur_tp merge so the unchanged leg isn't dropped); 'modify' added to interactive_brokers/ib/alpaca caps; Bybit set_trading_stop byte-unchanged. tests/test_ltmgmt_modify_wiring.py.

items.source: Live-trade management contract P4 + operator directive: keep real/paper performance strictly separate (no blended totals).
items.title: P4 — real/paper performance metric separation (bot + clients)
items.description: /api/bot/stats openTrades is real-money-only; add a separate paper open-count rather than a merged number. Ensure every aggregate (win rate, expectancy, realised/unrealised PnL, equity, per-strategy PnL%) is computed/displayed per funding class, never blended. Dashboard/Android already label paper rows in position lists; the aggregate separation is the remaining work.
items.trigger_condition: S-LIVETRADE-MGMT-S2 or a dashboard-metrics sprint.
items.resolution_criteria: No combined real+paper totals anywhere; paper has its own surfaced aggregates; openTrades KPI accompanied by a distinct paper count.

items.source: Part-2 audit (bot code review). PropRiskManager.__init__ (src/units/accounts/prop_risk.py) calls super().__init__ WITHOUT account_id, so the base RiskManager runs in-memory only — the journal-based daily-risk-state self-healing rebuild is disabled for prop accounts; daily-loss/drawdown caps reset to 0 on every restart. Split out of PR #3865 because passing account_id changes daily_pnl sourcing and breaks 4 prop-breach tests that set daily_pnl in-memory.
items.title: Tier-3: PropRiskManager misses account_id — daily-risk-state persistence off for prop accounts
items.description: ACTION FOR CLAUDE: own PR. Add account_id=account_name to PropRiskManager.__init__'s super() call, then rework the 4 prop-breach tests to seed daily_pnl via the journal/fixture rather than the now-overridden in-memory field. Tier-3 (risk-state for a live account class) — operator approval to merge. Latent: prop accounts are not live yet (Telegram-ticket execution), so no urgency.
items.trigger_condition: Next prop-accounts work, or next /performance-review touching prop risk.
items.resolution_criteria: PR merged with account_id wired AND full pytest-run green AND prop-breach detection confirmed still firing under journal-sourced daily_pnl.
items.resolution: Added account_id=account_name (or "") to PropRiskManager.__init__'s super() call (src/units/accounts/prop_risk.py), so prop accounts get the same journal-based daily-risk-state self-healing rebuild every regular account gets. Reworked the 4 in-memory prop-breach tests (test_accounts_integration.py, test_coordinator_flow.py) to seed the breach as a real today-dated closed trade in an isolated temp journal (prop_journal fixture + _seed_breach_trade helper), and added a TestJournalSourcedDailyRisk regression guard in test_prop_risk_manager.py (account_name->account_id wiring, cap engages from journal PnL, survives a restart, clean journal still allows). Verified green on the prop/risk/accounts/coordinator domain (270 passed) + CI pytest-run; shipped via PR #3868 (Tier-3, operator-approved).

items.source: Part-2 audit (dashboard code review). In ict-trader-dashboard streamlit_app.py, _lc_candle_data/_lc_ema_data/_lc_volume_data call int(ts.timestamp()) on tz-NAIVE timestamps (host-local epoch), while _lc_markers uses tz-aware UTC. On a UTC host (Streamlit Cloud prod) they agree; on any non-UTC host candles/EMA/volume shift vs signal/trade markers by the UTC offset.

items.source: journal table=trades — pkg-81602a3b78e14f
items.title: Reverse reconciler re-adopts ONE orphan exchange position as N trades (19x MGC -$23,773 + 3x MHG; episodic on IB-gateway flap)
items.description: One mgc_trend_1h MGC position (order_package pkg-81602a3b78e14f) appears as 18 CLOSED trade rows, ALL with identical entry_price 4318.872 + position_size 8, ALL notes.adopted_by=reverse_reconciler, adopted_at spanning 06-17 18:48->19:20 UTC, each booking a phantom sl_cross loss (sum -$20,127.68) on ib_paper. ROOT CAUSE: _reconcile_orphan_exchange_positions (src/runtime/order_monitor.py) creates a NEW orphan_adopt trade each time it sees the position unmatched, instead of recognising the prior adoption's open row as already covering it — so a flapping IB-gateway portfolio (empty->present->empty during the 06-17 evening IBKR reset window) spawns a fresh adopted trade per blip. ADOPT-side sibling of BL-20260614-ORPHANBLIP (which fixed the CLOSE-side re-orphan via the 2-observation close-confirm). Pollutes mgc_trend_1h stats; risks N-way realised-PnL double-count if it hits real-money bybit_2.
items.suggested_fix: Before creating an orphan_adopt row, check for an EXISTING open trade on the same (account,symbol,side,~entry/size) and re-attach/skip rather than adopt-again; or key adoption idempotency on the exchange position id. Add a regression test replaying empty->present->empty gateway snapshots. Consider a 2-observation confirm on the ADOPT side too.

confirmed.episodic: adopted_orphan counts by day: 06-14 n=2, 06-17 n=20, 06-18 n=0 — fires only during IB-gateway reset/flap windows, NOT ongoing
confirmed.residue: id 2683 MGC still status=open + id 2682 MGC status=orphaned on ib_paper (06-17) — lingering rows to reconcile/clean
confirmed.fix_status: FIX IMPLEMENTED in c897eec (re-adopt flap guard, RECONCILER_READOPT_GUARD_SECONDS) — pushed, NOT yet deployed (Tier-2 PR pending)

items.source: Live VM journalctl during the 2026-06-18 D2a restart (issue #3968): repeated `WARNING | src.runtime.closed_flat_invariant | closed_flat_invariant: trades query failed: malformed JSON`. Pre-existing, unrelated to the cross-asset work.
items.title: closed_flat_invariant trades query fails with 'malformed JSON' on the live VM
items.description: The closed-flat invariant check logs 'trades query failed: malformed JSON' each tick on the live trader. Likely a row in trade_journal.db with a malformed JSON column (meta/notes/exit_plan_state) that the invariant query json-parses, or a query that hit a partial write. Non-fatal (warning, the tick continues) but it means the closed-flat invariant isn't actually validating. Investigate: find the offending trade row(s) + harden the parse (skip/repair malformed JSON) so the invariant runs. Pre-existing — noticed during the D2a restart, not caused by it.

items.opened_by: claude (dashboard/android redesign session — follow-up to win-rate fix #3992)
items.source: Live diag 2026-06-19: /api/bot/stats real-money winRate read 6.3% vs /api/bot/performance?window=all 25.6% (92 wins / 360 RESOLVED). The /stats denominator bug was fixed in PR #3992 (now excludes pnl IS NULL). The remaining item is the underlying VOLUME of NULL-pnl real-money CLOSED rows: /performance counts only 360 resolved while /stats saw ~1460 non-open real-money rows, implying ~1100 closed real-money trades carry pnl IS NULL.
items.title: High volume of NULL-pnl real-money CLOSED trades (unresolved realised PnL)
items.description: ACTION FOR A FUTURE /health-review or /performance-review: investigate why a large share of real-money CLOSED trades have pnl IS NULL. Win-rate is now correct (#3992 excludes them from the denominator), but unresolved realised PnL also means totalPnL / expectancy / the equity curve undercount real performance. Likely cause: the non-Bybit (IBKR/Alpaca/OANDA) local-compute sweep (order_monitor._sweep_local_pnl_for_unpriced) not backfilling these rows, or reconciler_incomplete closes leaving pnl NULL. Quantify via Data Explorer / diag journal: COUNT(*) FROM trades WHERE status!='open' AND pnl IS NULL AND COALESCE(is_backtest,0)=0 grouped by account_class / exit_reason / account; then backfill realised pnl where resolvable and harden the sweep so future closes resolve.
items.resolution: Investigated + drained 2026-06-19. The '~1100' was a large overcount (stats/perf snapshot mixed already-resolved + paper + pre-account-class-backfill rows). Authoritative live count via the ict-db-integrity checker (INV-2, /api/diag/journalctl): 102 closed pnl-IS-NULL rows, recent=0 (none in the last 48h — the live multiplier-aware sweep keeps current closes clean). PR #4017 hardened scripts/ops/backfill_pnl_nulls.py to delegate to the canonical src.runtime.local_pnl helpers (contract_value_usd multiplier) — it previously undercounted IBKR futures (MGC 10x / MES 5x / MHG 2500x) via a raw (exit-entry)*size formula; real-money bybit_2 (crypto, cvu=1) was unaffected. Ran backfill-pnl-nulls (#4023) -> 0 recomputable candidates (the live sweep had already drained every closed row that has an exit_price), and mark-reconciler-incomplete (#4024) -> 0 reconciler_filled residual. The residual 102 rows all LACK an exit_price (so local compute is impossible and marking to today's price would be wrong) and are old (recent=0); they are correctly NULL and already excluded from /performance + /stats aggregates, so headline totalPnL / expectancy / equity are correct. The sweep's 14-day window is correct as-is (mark-to-market only makes sense for recent closes), so no sweep change is warranted. RESIDUAL OPTION (not done; low value): a historical-candle close-time mark could best-effort recover some of the 102 old (mostly paper, some orphan-adopted) rows, but it would be approximate and several are unlinked orphans (INV-3 overlap) with uncertain fills.

items.source: system-action backfill-closed-null-pnl (issue #4037, 2026-06-20): of 103 closed+NULL-pnl candidates, the 84 'recoverable' rows shown were all on bybit_1 (DEMO) and resolved to IDENTICAL per-side values (every long exit=63787.8 pnl=+3847.2941; every short exit=62353.0 pnl=+3545.7803) despite distinct entries/sizes — account_closed_pnl_for_trade returned a single shared Bybit closed-pnl record for many trades, and the recovered pnl SIGN contradicted the entry->exit move (a +3847 'profit' on a long that fell ~18%). The --apply wrote 0 rows. The 19 skipped candidates (lookup returned None) were not enumerated by account in the run output and may include real-money non-Bybit / 7-day-expired rows.
items.title: backfill closed-pnl recovery returns a mismatched/shared Bybit record for bybit_1 (demo) trades
items.description: MITIGATED (symptom): added a sign-consistency guard in scripts/ops/backfill_orphan_pnl.py::_plan_row (shared by backfill_closed_null_pnl) that refuses to write a recovered closed_pnl whose sign contradicts the trade's own >0.2% directional entry->exit move — a mismatched record can no longer fabricate a profit/loss into the journal. ROOT CAUSE OPEN: why does account_closed_pnl_for_trade (src/units/accounts/clients.py) return a shared record for distinct bybit_1 trades despite the qty(5%)/entry_price(10bps) disambiguators? Hypotheses: Bybit DEMO closed-pnl history is sparse so the filters fall through to a single latest record; or the demo venue endpoint behaves differently. HEALTH-REVIEW ACTION: pull bybit_1 raw closed-pnl via diag, confirm lookup-vs-demo-data fault, and decide whether to exclude demo accounts from closed-pnl recovery entirely (they are paper — pnl can be local-computed or left honest). Also enumerate the 19 skipped rows by account to confirm whether any real-money rows are genuinely stranded. Cross-ref BL-20260619-NULLPNL-REALMONEY-CLOSED.

items.source: order_monitor reconciler-filled close path writes Bybit updatedTime (raw epoch-MILLISECONDS string, e.g. "1781839121796") straight into the trades.closed_at COLUMN, which every other writer fills with ISO-8601. clients.account_closed_pnl_for_trade returns closed_at as the ms string (its docstring says so); order_monitor._close_trade_from_order_status (lines ~4254/4271) and _bybit_closed_pnl_recovery (lines ~3504/3523) persist it verbatim to both the column and notes.closed_at. Verified live: ALL 12 reconciler_filled rows in the diag journal pull (issue #4065) carry an all-digit ms closed_at; the only ISO one was a backfill_closed_pnl_recovery row.
items.title: Reconciler-filled close writes epoch-ms (not ISO) into trades.closed_at column
items.description: The trades.closed_at column contract is ISO-8601 (every consumer does datetime(closed_at), which returns NULL for a bare ms epoch). The reader-side dashboard endpoint /api/bot/trades/closed was fixed in the trades-closed-reconciler-visibility PR to normalise ms->ISO so buried rows resurface, but that only patches ONE consumer. The TRUE root cause is the writer: order_monitor should normalise the Bybit ms timestamp to ISO before writing the closed_at column (and notes.closed_at), so every consumer (pnl_history, performance, closed_flat_invariant json_extract, the re-adopt flap guard which ORDER BY closed_at DESC, etc.) sees a consistent ISO value and existing ms rows can be one-shot backfilled. This is Tier-2 order-path/monitor code — propose the exact change + a migration for the already-written ms rows; do not merge without operator approval.
items.resolution: Already fixed: order_monitor close paths normalise closed_at via src.utils.closed_at.normalize_closed_at_value at every write site that takes a broker timestamp (lines ~3620, ~4371, ~4430, citing this BL id); other sites write datetime.now().isoformat(). trades.closed_at is ISO going forward. The read-side guards (_closed_at.py / M13 fix) remain for any pre-fix rows.

check_log.by: claude (system-report → operator-reported missing real bybit_2 closes)
check_log.note: Read-side guard EXTENDED to the remaining consumer endpoints. Confirmed live from a real bybit_2 Bybit transaction log: 6 real CLOSE fills in 24h (e.g. trade #2768 closed 11:37Z +1.30) were dropped from /api/bot/performance (totalTrades=0) and /api/bot/stats pnl24h ($0) while lifetime totals still showed them — the epoch-ms closed_at fails datetime()/substr(). Centralised the epoch-ms-aware normalisation in src/web/api/_closed_at.py and wired /performance, /stats pnl24h, and /pnl/history (substr day-bucket) to it; re-pointed trades_closed.py at the shared module to kill the duplicate. STILL OPEN (Tier-2, operator-gated): the WRITER-side fix in order_monitor (normalise Bybit ms→ISO before persisting closed_at + a one-shot migration of existing ms rows), and the closed_flat_invariant json_extract(notes.closed_at) consumer.

items.source: Real bybit_2 BTCUSDT positions (journal ids 2762/2757/2746) were orphaned with NULL pnl + #2765 closed NULL-pnl because the Bybit closed-pnl matcher (account_closed_pnl_for_trade / _bybit_closed_pnl_lookup) returned None: a scaled-in / partially-closed position has no single closed-pnl record matching the full qty (strict qty±5%). PR #4182 added a partial-close aggregation fallback (allow_partial_aggregate, LIVE non-reduce) + a diagnostic log on the no-strict-match path. The fix addresses the INFERRED qty-mismatch cause — not yet confirmed against the actual Bybit closed-pnl API response for these trades.
items.title: VERIFY the closed-pnl partial-close aggregation actually resolves the bybit_2 orphan-with-NULL-pnl (soak)
items.description: KEEP FOLLOWING UP until confirmed resolved. On the NEXT live bybit_2 (or any live-money) stuck/orphan event, (1) read the new 'closed_pnl no strict single-match: … rej_side/rej_time/rej_entry/rej_qty …' diagnostic in the trader journal to confirm the real failing filter is qty (the inferred cause) vs entry/time; (2) confirm '_recover_close_from_broker_pnl' now recovers a clean close (status='closed' with non-NULL pnl + exit_price, exit_price_source bybit_closed_pnl, _aggregated_legs>1) instead of orphaning; (3) confirm the watchdog 'recovered_closed' counter increments and the orphan rate on bybit_2 drops. If the diagnostic shows the failing filter is ENTRY (not qty), the aggregation won't help and the fix needs revisiting (relax/repair entry matching). Cross-refs: BL-20260620-CLOSEDPNL-LOOKUP-MISMATCH-DEMO, BL-20260601-001 (high orphan rates). Resolve only after >=1 confirmed live recovery (or a clean multi-day window with zero NULL-pnl real-money closes).
