items.source: Live VM diag (issue #4203 journalctl ict-liveness-watchdog.service): the liveness watchdog (Type=oneshot, fires every ~60s) intermittently fails at the systemd STDIN-setup step with `Failed to set up standard input: No such file or directory` / `status=208/STDIN` (~8% of runs; observed 16:08:08 and 16:12:29 UTC), BEFORE check_heartbeat.py runs. Each failure self-heals on the next minute's run, so the unit's `failed` state is transient (the snapshot at 16:12:32 happened to land 3s after a failed run). The repo canonical unit (deploy/ict-liveness-watchdog.service) sets NO StandardInput (default null -> /dev/null, which cannot ENOENT), so the deployed unit/drop-in has drifted OR a git-sync `git reset --hard` race transiently breaks the spawn.
items.title: VERIFY the liveness-watchdog 208/STDIN intermittent exec failure is gone after the StandardInput=null pin reinstall
items.description: The dead-man switch must spawn reliably on every tick; an ~8% per-run exec failure erodes the 3-strike (~8 min) autoheal margin and a sticky `failed` state masks a genuinely-dead watchdog. Remediation: PR pins `StandardInput=null` explicitly in deploy/ict-liveness-watchdog.service (BL-20260622-WATCHDOG-STDIN), which on the next runtime git-sync forces install_systemd_units.sh to recopy the (now-differing) unit, reconverging any drift. FOLLOW UP: after the reinstall is confirmed (deployed unit == repo), pull `journalctl?unit=ict-liveness-watchdog.service&lines=200` and confirm ZERO `208/STDIN` occurrences across a >=30-min window. If 208/STDIN PERSISTS after a confirmed reinstall of the explicit-StandardInput unit, the cause is a drop-in (/etc/systemd/system/ict-liveness-watchdog.service.d/*.conf) or a git-sync `git reset --hard` spawn race (NOT main-unit drift) — escalate to a targeted system-action that `systemctl cat`s the deployed unit + drop-ins and purges any rogue drop-in. Note: the `/etc/ict-vm-role: No such file or directory` log line in deploy_pull_restart.sh line 91 is cosmetic/expected on the trader (empty role = not gateway); NOT a drift to fix.
items.resolution: PR #4206 pinned StandardInput=null on deploy/ict-liveness-watchdog.service; merged 2026-06-22T16:36:59Z (main baeec23e) and deployed via ict-git-sync (full path: install_systemd_units.sh recopied the now-differing unit + one trader bounce; VM confirmed on git_sha baeec23e, trader healthy post-restart). SOAK VERIFIED: watchdog journal 16:41:01-17:17:04 UTC (~36 min, ~36 consecutive per-minute runs, diag issue #4210) shows EVERY run clean (Starting -> Deactivated successfully -> Finished); ZERO 208/STDIN / "Failed to set up standard input" after the reinstall, vs ~8% failure rate before. Root cause confirmed as MAIN-UNIT DRIFT (reconverged by the idempotent reinstall) — NOT a drop-in or git-sync reset --hard race, so no escalation/purge action needed. The /etc/ict-vm-role "No such file" deploy-log line was confirmed cosmetic/expected on the trader.

items.source: Hourly ict-db-integrity timer (06:00Z) flagged INV-2: 1 recent (103 total) — closed trade pnl IS NULL past grace; id [2783]. #2783 = bybit_2 (real_money) BTCUSDT ict_scalp_5m, opened 16:55:46Z, 'closed' by the reconciler the same instant (closedAt<=openedAt) with exit_price NULL + pnl NULL. An open-then-instant-reconciler-close artifact reached a terminal 'closed' status without a realised pnl on a REAL-money row.
items.title: [write-path] real-money trade #2783 closed with pnl IS NULL past the 6h broker-sweep grace (INV-2)
items.description: A closed real-money row must carry a realised pnl (broker-truth or local_compute sweep). The reconciler close path that produced #2783 set status='closed' without resolving pnl, and the 6h order_monitor._sweep_local_pnl_for_unpriced grace then elapsed with it still NULL. Investigate the instant-close path (closedAt<=openedAt, null exit) in order_monitor — likely a netting/flat reconcile that booked a close without an exit fill. Fix: either resolve pnl on that close path (local_compute from contract_value_usd) or don't mark such a phantom flat as 'closed'. Tier-2 (order_monitor close path).
items.trigger_condition: next time an instant-reconciler-close produces a null-exit closed row, OR a fix to the close path lands
items.resolution_criteria: the close path resolves pnl (broker/local) before status='closed', OR phantom-flat reconciles are not booked as closed; INV-2 recent count returns to 0 and stays.

check_log.note: Fix landed (order_monitor close/sweep path, Tier-2): the gap was the local-compute sweep deferring broker-reader (Bybit) rows the full 7-day _BROKER_PNL_RECOVERY_MS window while the Bybit closed-pnl sweep could never fill them (no realised closed-pnl record exists for a same-instant net-flat). Between the 6h INV-2 grace and day 7 nothing touched the row -> closed/NULL -> INV-2. Fix: new _LOCAL_PNL_BROKER_DEFER_MS (6h = DEFAULT_PNL_GRACE_HOURS) shortens the local-sweep defer so a broker row the reader still can't price converges to local_compute (pnl~=0 truthful for a same-instant flat) right after the grace. Broker truth still wins within the grace. +3 tests in tests/test_local_pnl.py. The larger 'should it be booked closed at all' question logged for follow-up if instant-flat reconciles recur.

items.resolution: Shortened the local-pnl-sweep broker defer to the INV-2 6h grace so stranded Bybit closed/NULL rows converge to local_compute; INV-2 should return to 0.

items.source: Telegram health 06:00Z: accounts_api 3/7 down: ib_paper, ib_live, breakout_1. ib_paper/ib_live down = IB Gateway unreachable; MES/MGC/MHG cannot trade. Also gates the MES regime-model promotions (live_agreement can't accrue with no MES trades). vm-ib-gateway-recover fired this session (issue #4242).
items.title: [gateway] recurring IB Gateway wedge — ib_paper/ib_live API down at 06:00Z (MES/MGC/MHG dark)
items.description: The reactive gateway watchdog was re-armed 2026-06-22 (BL-20260622-GATEWAY-MIDDAY-WEDGE) but the gateway was still down at 06:00Z — check whether the watchdog exhausted its --max-restarts 3 budget or parked on an IBKR 2FA/credential re-login (the recover workflow's known operator-handoff failure mode). If 2FA, that is a genuine operator action. Track recurrence cadence; if the gateway keeps wedging, escalate the root cause (IBC nightly auto-restart unreliable, BL-20260527-003). RECURRENCE 2026-07-03: ib_paper positions=null at 17:00Z (mid-day wedge, ~11h after the 06:05Z daily reset; MGC order rejected 02:02Z so likely wedged most of the day). vm-ib-gateway-recover fired autonomously (issue #5511) by /health-review 2026-07-03. The reactive on-gateway watchdog did not clear it — check its restart budget / 2FA parking on the recover log. CHECK-IN VERDICT 2026-07-03 20:00Z: recover #5511 relogin VERIFIED at the gateway (login completed 17:01:44Z, API port up); trader loop journal at 20:00Z shows zero IBConnectionError/breaker fast-fails; ib_paper holds no open IB positions and CME was closed (Jul-3 early close). The persistent diag exchange_positions null is the conservative empty-snapshot + net_liquidation-unverifiable-over-cross-host-socat read path (clients.account_open_positions IB branch; BL-20260610-009 class) — an observability limitation, NOT evidence of a continuing outage. Definitive close-out: confirm MES/MGC/MHG evals produce signals/fetches next CME session (2026-07-06).
items.trigger_condition: next ib_paper/ib_live accounts_api-down alert, or the #4242 recover result
items.resolution_criteria: gateway stays logged-in across the IBKR daily reset without manual recovery for a full week; or the root-cause re-login reliability is fixed.

check_log.note: system-report health-review: live order_packages show 3 mgc_trend_1h MGC pkgs orphaned in-window (06:53/07:51/08:00Z) + no IBKR fills; mes/mgc/mhg *_eval ARE firing (signals generated) so it is broker-session-down, not strategy silence. Gateway still wedged. KEEP OPEN; tracking recurrence per criteria (1 clean week).

Recurred again 2026-07-02T06:05Z (ib_paper positions=null at the same ~06:00Z window). Dispatched vm-ib-gateway-recover (issue #5404) — container was already up 36min (not crashed, just the trading session had wedged); docker restart + IBC re-login completed cleanly at 06:07:01Z (login_completed=yes, api reachable=yes). Re-verify at 06:13:20Z still showed positions=null (breaker/reconnect not yet caught up ~6min post-restart) — consistent with the documented ~2min breaker-clear + the trader's own reconnect cadence, not a new failure. Cadence keeps confirming the ~06:00Z daily wedge pattern; root cause (IBC nightly auto-restart unreliable, BL-20260527-003) still not fixed at the source.

Follow-up check, 4 days after the 2026-07-02 06:05 UTC retime + --suppress-window-utc 03:45-05:45 fix. Pulled the live liveness-watchdog journal for 05:17-07:03Z today — clean, no CRITICAL/restart entries, no IB mentions at all. Also ran vm-ib-gateway-selftest live: gateway container has RestartCount=0 and unbroken uptime since its 2026-06-10 creation, 4 clean 'Login has completed' re-auth cycles in the last 2h (3 in the last 30m — normal cadence, not flapping), and the reactive watchdog auto-healed a CONTROLLED stop-then-detect test in ~10s (on the 2nd consecutive wedge check, exactly as designed). No recurrence of the 06:00Z wedge observed since the retime. Leaving open one more full week before closing outright to be sure — full findings in docs/research/ib-pipeline-stability-review-2026-07-06.md.

RECURRENCE 2026-07-07 ~07:55:14Z: ict-web-api.service journal (diag #5829) shows 'IBClient: account/portfolio warm-up timed out twice (8s+8s retry) for 10.0.0.251:4002 (account=...5724) -- gateway likely logged out' + 'circuit breaker tripped ... consecutive failures=16'; concurrent exchange_positions?account_id=ib_paper (diag #5831) reads positions=null (could-not-read). This is OUTSIDE the 03:45-05:45 UTC daily-reset suppress window, so it is a genuine mid-morning wedge, not the expected daily reset. Raised as the mandatory broker-reachability flag for this review (see anomalies/flags). Recommend vm-ib-gateway-recover if not already auto-fired by the reachability-alert latch.

ROOT-CAUSE FINDING: system-actions.yml's gateway-logs action has been silently pointed at the wrong host since the 2026-06-10 gateway-isolation redesign -- it SSHes to VM_SSH_HOST (141.145.193.91, the live trader) and runs 'docker ps/logs ib-gateway' there, but the ib-gateway container has lived on the dedicated gateway VM (10.0.0.251) since that redesign. Confirmed live (issue #5834): 'docker ps -a' returned empty and 'docker logs ib-gateway' returned "No such container: ib-gateway" -- there's no gateway container on the trader VM to inspect. This means every attempt since 2026-06-10 to use gateway-logs for root-causing a wedge (including the open BL-20260527-003 investigation) silently got no real data. FIXED this session (system-actions.yml): gateway-logs now routes via ProxyJump through the trader to GATEWAY_HOST (10.0.0.251), mirroring the pattern already used by vm-ib-gateway-recover.yml/vm-ib-gateway-selftest.yml. Also confirmed the current wedge self-recovered on its own between 07:56Z and 08:39Z (exchange_positions?account_id=ib_paper went from positions:null to positions:[],error:null) without an operator/Claude-triggered recover -- so the reactive watchdog or a passive retry cleared it, timeline TBD once gateway-logs is re-run post-fix.

OPERATOR DECISION: explored two mitigation paths for the recurring IB-gateway instability -- (1) IBKR's own Client Portal Web API (CPAPI) as a same-account transport swap to escape the Xvfb/IBC GUI-automation layer, and (2) migrating futures to a broker with a cleaner native API (Tradovate, Ironbeam direct API, Rithmic via AMP/Ironbeam). CPAPI is closed off: it requires an IBKR Pro account (confirmed via IBKR's own docs -- Lite is unsupported for API access, no SSO-only carve-out), and the operator does not have Pro and cannot get it in a relevant timeframe. On reviewing the broker-migration options (Tradovate/Ironbeam/Rithmic), the operator decided none of them are worth pursuing right now. DECISION: drop both lines of investigation for now -- stay on the current TWS-socket/ib_insync/Xvfb/IBC integration. The gateway-logs system-action routing fix (this session, see the linked PR) remains valuable regardless -- it unblocks root-causing future wedges on the CURRENT integration, which is now the only path being pursued for IB reliability. Revisit CPAPI/broker-migration only if the account tier changes or the wedge frequency/impact gets materially worse.

items.source: Telegram health 06:00Z accounts_api lists breakout_1 among '3/7 accounts API down'. breakout_1 is a manual-bridge prop account with NO broker API by design (it executes via Telegram/FCM tickets), so an 'API down' for it is structurally meaningless and inflates the down-count (real IB outage is 2/7, reported as 3/7).
items.title: [health-check] breakout_1 (manual-bridge prop) counted as accounts_api down — false positive
items.description: The accounts_api health probe should skip accounts with no broker API (prop/manual-bridge, exchange=breakout) rather than count them as down — otherwise a healthy prop account permanently shows as an API error and masks the real IB count. Tier-1/2 health-check refinement (scripts/health snapshot accounts probe).
items.trigger_condition: next accounts_api health line listing breakout_1 as down
items.resolution_criteria: the probe excludes manual-bridge/prop accounts (no broker API) from the API-up/down tally.
items.resolution: Fixed in src/runtime/health.py::check_accounts_api — skips exchanges with an explicitly-empty EXCHANGE_MANAGEMENT_CAPS set (the breakout manual-bridge stub) so a no-broker-API account is no longer counted as an outage; unknown exchanges still probe (fail-open). Verify on the next health snapshot that breakout_1 drops out of the accounts_api down-count. Deploys to live via ict-git-sync on merge (Tier-2).

items.source: Telegram daily heartbeat: 'News layer: enabled-no-key'. NEWS_SOURCE defaults to newsapi which is active only with NEWS_API_KEY; with no key the layer is a neutral no-op (never vetoes/sizes). Per CLAUDE.md the keyless real-time path is NEWS_SOURCE=rss.
items.title: [config] news layer inert: enabled-no-key (NEWS_SOURCE=newsapi, no NEWS_API_KEY)
items.description: If the news layer is meant to be live, set NEWS_SOURCE=rss on the live VM (keyless, real-time, feeds in config/news_feeds.yaml) — Tier-2/3 env change via the VM env. If it is intentionally inert during soak, no action; but the 'enabled-no-key' status is misleading (reads as enabled when it is effectively off).
items.trigger_condition: operator decision on activating the news layer
items.resolution_criteria: NEWS_SOURCE=rss set (layer active) — or a decision to leave it inert is recorded and the status string clarified.

items.source: Telegram health 06:00Z: [WARN] git_drift: git rev-parse HEAD failed. The health snapshot could not read the repo HEAD on the live VM (the ict-web-api runs from /opt/ict-trading-bot -> /home/ubuntu symlink; git_drift likely ran in a cwd/safe.directory context where rev-parse fails), so the deploy-drift check is blind.
items.title: [health-check] git_drift: 'git rev-parse HEAD failed' in the health snapshot
items.description: Resolve the git_drift check's working directory / git safe.directory config so rev-parse HEAD succeeds, restoring the deploy-drift signal. Low severity (other checks OK; git_fetch reported 0.4m ago) but it blinds the 'is the VM on the merged main' check. Tier-1/2 health-check fix.
items.trigger_condition: next health snapshot still showing git_drift WARN
items.resolution_criteria: git_drift reports the live HEAD sha cleanly (matches origin/main).

check_log.note: Defensive fix committed: check_git_drift now runs git with -c safe.directory=* (the overwhelmingly common cause of 'rev-parse HEAD failed' when a service user reads a repo it doesn't own / via the /opt symlink). Kept OPEN: the live stderr was truncated, so if git_drift still WARNs after deploy the cause is a wrong cwd / not-a-repo context, not ownership — re-diagnose from the next snapshot's stderr.

system-report health-review: live /api/diag/status.git_sha resolves cleanly as 7b5139b3 (== merged HEAD 7b5139b) post safe.directory=* fix — strong evidence rev-parse now succeeds. KEEP OPEN until the next ict-health-snapshot artifact specifically shows git_drift OK (the snapshot writer's cwd/context differs from the diag status path).

items.source: pull-and-deploy #4263 (2026-06-23) deploy log: 'scripts/deploy_pull_restart.sh: line 91: /etc/ict-vm-role: No such file or directory'. The live trader VM has no /etc/ict-vm-role marker, so the deploy script's role-detection read errors (non-fatal stderr warning; deploy still completed).
items.title: deploy_pull_restart.sh role-detection errors on the live VM (missing /etc/ict-vm-role)
items.description: The role-marker file /etc/ict-vm-role (consumed to gate role-specific logic, e.g. auto-enabling ict-ib-gateway-watchdog only where role==gateway) is absent on the live trader VM, so line ~91 of scripts/deploy_pull_restart.sh emits a 'No such file or directory' warning every deploy. Cosmetic today (the read is non-fatal and the live VM correctly should NOT enable the gateway watchdog), but it's log noise and a latent footgun if future logic hard-depends on the marker. Fix: either write /etc/ict-vm-role=live on the live VM (via a set-env-style action / install script) OR make the script tolerate a missing file ([ -r /etc/ict-vm-role ] guard, default role=live).
items.trigger_condition: next deploy / next health-review touching the deploy scripts
items.resolution_criteria: deploy_pull_restart.sh no longer warns on the live VM (marker present or read guarded); role detection is explicit on all three VMs.

updates.note: RESOLVED — scripts/deploy_pull_restart.sh guards the /etc/ict-vm-role read with `[ -r ]` first (the input-redirection error leaks before the command's 2>/dev/null on a missing file), so the live trader VM (no marker, correctly non-gateway) no longer emits 'No such file or directory' every deploy. bash -n clean.

items.source: Operator rotated Alpaca LIVE API keys (ALPACA_API_KEY_ID_LIVE / ALPACA_API_SECRET_KEY_LIVE) on 2026-06-26 after old paper-trading keys caused api_ok: false on alpaca_live. New keys synced to VM via sync-vm-secrets (issue #4644, exit 0, ~06:15Z). Trader restarted (issue #4649, exit 0, ~06:21Z, new PID 783300). Process started clean — zero auth errors in journalctl. Definitive api_ok confirmation deferred to next hourly snapshot (ict-hourly-snapshot.timer) running after the restart; the concurrent /system-review session should see updated balance_snapshots.
items.title: Verify alpaca_live: {api_ok: true, balance: ~150} after 2026-06-26 LIVE key rotation
items.description: ACTION FOR /system-review or /health-review: confirm the rotated Alpaca LIVE keys are working by checking /api/bot/accounts/balances for alpaca_live. Expected: api_ok=true, balance ~$150 (the live account seed balance), source='db', as_of after 2026-06-26T06:21Z (post-restart hourly snapshot). If api_ok is still false or the balance is clearly wrong (e.g. $0 or the paper balance ~$100k), the LIVE keys may not have propagated correctly — re-check the sync-vm-secrets run log and confirm ALPACA_API_KEY_ID_LIVE / ALPACA_API_SECRET_KEY_LIVE are in the VM .env. Note: runtime_status.live.alpaca_live=false is expected until US equity market open (13:30Z) since no alpaca_live strategies fire outside market hours; api_ok in balance_snapshots is the correct check.
items.trigger_condition: next /system-review or /health-review (the concurrent system-review session on 2026-06-26 should pick this up)
items.resolution_criteria: A health or system review confirms /api/bot/accounts/balances shows alpaca_live: {api_ok: true, balance: ~150} from a snapshot timestamped after 2026-06-26T06:21Z.
items.resolution: CONFIRMED NOT A KEY ISSUE. alpaca_live.api_ok=true in live status snapshot (account connects and authenticates successfully). Root cause: balance ~$150, insufficient for 1-share sizing at 1% risk on any ETF strategy (GLD ~$374/share, SPY ~$550, QQQ ~$480). The misleading error message in execute.py fires "check ALPACA_API_KEY_ID/ALPACA_API_SECRET_KEY" whenever balance() returns None, but this fires on ANY balance-read failure — not just auth failures. Every rotation since the live flip has been correct keys; the problem is insufficient capital. Operator action required: fund alpaca_live account to ≥$2,000 to allow any ETF fill.

updates.note: Ran sync-vm-secrets (issue #4746). Result: exit 0, all 12 secrets 'unchanged (skip write)' — confirmed credentials ARE on the VM and match GitHub Actions secrets exactly. This rules out a sync failure. Then confirmed via diag #4747 that 'alpaca balance: request is not authorized' persists in ict-hourly-snapshot logs. Root cause refined: the keys are present but rejected by the live Alpaca endpoint (https://api.alpaca.markets). Most likely cause: paper-account keys stored under the ALPACA_API_KEY_ID_LIVE / ALPACA_API_SECRET_KEY_LIVE secret names — paper keys are accepted by paper-api.alpaca.markets but rejected by api.alpaca.markets. Genuine operator hand-off: operator must verify at Alpaca console that both secrets are from the LIVE (funded) account, not the paper account. If wrong, update the secret values and re-run sync-vm-secrets. Stays OPEN until api_ok: true confirmed in /api/bot/accounts/balances.

items.opened_by: claude (vol-gate go-live session — log monitoring/verification items before wrap)
items.source: This session took the Design-A vol-gate live on real-money bybit_2 (use+enforce; MB-20260628-VOLGATE-GOLIVE) and hardened the regime router to baseline-on + REGIME_ROUTER_DISABLED kill-switch (PR #4920). Three live behaviours still need positive verification from production data rather than 'should work'.
items.title: VERIFY live: (1) first BTC vol-axis hard-gate fire, (2) #4920 gate-fragility deploy health, (3) ETH/SOL soaks accruing rows

Until seen, the vol-axis enforce is unproven end-to-end on real signals — no fire observed as of go-live because the (strategy,trend,volatile) candidate combos are infrequent, NOT because the label is frozen (resolution is per-symbol, BTC resolves the 15m advisory head). (2) #4920 DEPLOY HEALTH — once #4920 merges, ict-git-sync deploys it (restarts the trader); confirm heartbeat age / ticking / CPU healthy after. The change is a behavioural no-op (REGIME_ROUTER_ENABLED=true already set) so the only risk is the restart; the live VM's REGIME_ROUTER_ENABLED var is now REDUNDANT (baseline-on) and can be removed later (harmless). (3) SOAK ACCRUAL — confirm shadow_predictions.jsonl is getting eth-regime-{5m,15m} + sol-regime-{5m,15m} rows (the per-bar scorer is actually scoring the new shadow heads) so post-soak RG4 (MB-20260628-REGIME-SOAK-READINESS) will have a sample.

items.resolution_criteria: (1) at least one regime_hard_gate vol_gated:true+vol_label_source:ml row observed (or, if none after ~1wk of enforce, a note explaining the candidate-frequency reason); (2) trader confirmed healthy post-#4920-deploy; (3) eth/sol regime shadow rows confirmed present in shadow_predictions.jsonl.

updates.note: VERIFIED live via diag relays. (1) FIRST ENFORCED FIRE confirmed: regime_hard_gate audit row id 632682 @2026-06-29T12:13:13Z — trend_donchian BTCUSDT long, vol_regime=volatile, vol_label_source=ml, vol_gated=true, vol_enforced=true, vol_cell=off, enforced=true (the BTC 15m advisory head drove the ML vol label, per-symbol resolution). items.source: docs/audits/order-packages-zero-qty-2026-06-26.md; coordinator.py _log_new_order_package(:936) serializes pkg.meta at package CREATION, before the per-account sizing loop (sized_qty_by_account bound empty at :855, populated at :1481). The DB row is never re-written post-sizing, so order_packages.meta.sized_qty_by_account is always {} and aggregated_target_qty always 0 (the sentinel) for EVERY package.
items.title: [observability] order_packages.meta serialized pre-sizing → sized_qty_by_account always empty in the journal
items.description: The field that exists to show per-account sizing is dead `{}` in every persisted order_packages row because meta is snapshotted at creation, before sizing runs. This led the 2026-06-26 system-report (RPT-20260626-060200) to read `aggregated_target_qty:0 / sized_qty_by_account:{}` as evidence of a zero-sizing bug when both are normal at-creation states (target_qty=0 is the RiskManager-decides sentinel; sized_qty_by_account is just unpopulated at log time). The real per-account cause lives only in the trades rejection rows. DRAFT FIX (branch claude/order-packages-zero-qty-eaofnv, coordinator.py): best-effort re-persist of the post-sizing meta after the dispatch loop — additive, observability-only, no execution change. Tier-3 (touches the order-path file) → draft, awaiting operator approval. Also consider stamping the dominant per-account refusal cause (zero_balance vs netting-guard vs hold-policy) into the no_fill roll-up so consumers needn't join trades.

items.source: docs/audits/order-packages-zero-qty-2026-06-26.md § Thread (b). RiskManager.position_size whole-share sizing for alpaca (risk.py:631-651 round-up-to-1-share).
items.title: [latent] whole-unit rounding-to-zero only bites a sub-$1.3k real-money alpaca account — confirm once funded
items.description: Whole-unit (alpaca) sizing refuses a sub-1-share ideal unless 1-share risk <= 1.5x (equity x risk_pct). At risk_pct=0.01 and the observed per-share risks (GLD $7.52, SPY $10.23, QQQ $19.51), refusal only occurs below ~$501/$682/$1301 of equity; above that the round-up-to-1-share fires and a single share is taken. NOT the cause of the 2026-06-25 alpaca_live zeros (those were zero_balance=$0, a funding/creds fetch failure owned by the key-rotation session). Action: once alpaca_live has working keys + real funding, confirm one ETF signal sizes a whole share (not refused). No code change proposed.
items.resolution: OPERATOR DECISION 2026-07-03 (chat, /health-review follow-up): leave alpaca_live UNFUNDED as-is. The latent behaviour fired as designed on 2026-07-03 13:30Z — TLT sized to 0 whole shares and was refused per-trade (correct Prime-Directive shape). Accepted consequence: alpaca_live can only take trades whose 1-share risk fits its small balance (it holds IEF x1); most of its 9 symbols will refuse. No funding, no symbol-list trim requested. Future reviews should NOT re-raise this as a finding.

items.title: [risk] crypto/fx sub-min-lot refusal uses account-default min_qty, not the per-instrument platform lot (ETH 0.01 gap)
items.description: PR #4969 (merged) made a sub-min-lot risk-based size a per-trade REFUSAL on the crypto/fx (non-whole-unit) path instead of bumping it up to min_qty (the over-risk #3910 flagged). It was scoped MINIMALLY: the refusal threshold is the ACCOUNT-configured min_qty (RiskManager.min_qty, default 0.001), NOT the per-instrument platform lot from config/instruments.yaml. For BTCUSDT that's correct (real lot == 0.001), but ETHUSDT's real Bybit lot is 0.01 (instruments.yaml min_qty/qty_step 0.01) — so a risk-based ETH size in [0.001, 0.0099) currently passes the refusal (>= account 0.001) yet the exchange would reject it as sub-lot. Pre-existing (the old bump was equally wrong there) and NOT worsened by #4969 — bybit_2's live ETH position (0.01) sizes legitimately today. FOLLOW-UP (the part of #3910 deliberately left out of #4969 to keep the real-money PR surgical): add an instrument-aware floor — instrument_min_qty_step_for(symbol) reading (min_qty, qty_precision from qty_step) from config/instruments.yaml — and resolve eff_min_qty/eff_precision from it on the non-whole path, falling back to the account min_qty when a symbol has no profile. The reference implementation is in the closed #3910 diff (src/units/accounts/risk.py). Tier-3 (sizing on the live order path) + needs the test reworks for profiled-symbol precision (IWM-on-bybit synthetic tests in test_alpaca_whole_share_sizing.py).
items.source: PR #4969 (merged 744ac178) § 'Why minimal (vs full #3910)'; src/units/accounts/risk.py::position_size non-whole-unit refusal branch; config/instruments.yaml ETHUSDT min_qty/qty_step 0.01 vs RiskManager default min_qty 0.001.

updates.note: THIRD sibling of this defect found + fixed on branch claude/order-qty-rounding-issue-wj1d1v (Tier-3, draft PR pending operator approval). The intent-mode netting-DELTA guard in coordinator.py::multi_account_execute (~line 1900) also compared delta.qty_delta against account.risk_manager.min_qty (0.001), NOT the venue lot. A live ETHUSDT/bybit_2 top-up (sized 0.111, held ~0.102 -> delta 0.009) slipped the guard and hit the _submit_order pre-flight ('below the exchange lot minimum after step-alignment') -> bybit_place_order_failed error ping on every top-up/trim signal. Fix folds venue_min_qty_for into the delta guard (max(account_min, venue_min); None -> account_min unchanged), converting the noisy ping into a clean intent_sub_min_qty_delta noop. Regression tests in tests/test_intent_delta_dispatch.py::TestIntentModeVenueMinDelta. The risk.py::position_size path this item primarily tracks is STILL OPEN (separate location).

items.resolution: RESOLVED by the sizing/qty-legalization consolidation (docs/sizing-legalization-DESIGN.md, operator-approved). The recurring root cause — four divergent copies of 'the venue minimum' — was collapsed onto ONE seam, src/units/accounts/qty_legalize.py::legalize_qty, resolving (qty_step, min_qty) from the per-instrument InstrumentProfile (config/instruments.yaml) first, live Bybit lot rule second, passthrough third. Phase 2 wired the seam into the two coordinator guards (sized-qty ~1503, intent-delta ~1900) + the execute._submit_order Bybit pre-flight (#5720). Phase 3 (#5732, merged 581b45e, Tier-3 operator-approved) closed the risk.py::position_size half this item primarily tracked: the non-whole path now resolves eff_min_qty/eff_precision from qty_legalize.instrument_lot(symbol) when 0 < step < 1 (ETHUSDT 0.01, SOLUSDT 0.1), so a risk-based ETH size in [0.001, 0.0099) is now REFUSED per-trade at the sizing layer instead of passing risk.py's own 0.001 account-min. Whole-share sizing stays exchange-gated by whole_units (BL-20260622), never inferred from the instrument; BTC (step 0.001 == account default) unchanged. Tests: tests/test_instrument_min_sizing.py (11), tests/test_qty_legalize.py, tests/test_intent_delta_dispatch.py. Phase 4 (a qty-legalization CI guard forbidding raw-min comparisons outside the seam) is the remaining Tier-1 follow-up to prevent a fifth copy re-appearing.

items.title: [order-path] Alpaca positions can sit NAKED (no SL/TP) — naked-autoprotect + reconciler-adopt are IB-only; no re-arm for non-IB
items.description: Operator observed alpaca_paper showing 6 open ETF positions (TLT/SPY/SLV/QQQ/IWM/GLD) but only 2 working orders -> ~4 positions are naked (no resting SL/TP bracket leg). Alpaca entries DO attach an atomic bracket at entry (alpaca_client.place order_class=bracket, alpaca_client.py:121-129), so these lost their protective legs afterward (suspected: reverse-reconciler adopt/reattach of an orphan, or a bracket child-leg cancel/reject) and nothing re-armed them: order_monitor naked-position auto-protect (_check_naked_positions/_attempt_naked_autoprotect/_rearm_broker_protection_after_recovery) is IB-ONLY (order_monitor.py ~5159-5190); non-IB accounts only get a one-shot alert. AlpacaClient ALREADY has modify_protective + open_orders, so the building blocks for a non-IB re-arm exist. FIX (Tier-2/3, order-protection path, operator-gated): extend the naked-position sweep to re-arm an Alpaca bracket via AlpacaClient (place a fresh protective OTO/bracket or modify_protective) when an open Alpaca position has no resting SL/TP leg; cover the reconciler adopt/reattach path too. Verify on alpaca_paper first. Applies to real-money alpaca_live as well (currently flat).
items.source: operator screenshot 2026-06-29 + code read

updates.note: RE-ARM SHIPPED + LIVE-VERIFIED. The broker-naked Alpaca sweep (_check_broker_naked_equity_positions) + AlpacaClient.place_protective (GTC OCO) merged (#5018) and deployed (cbceb5d); the OCO request shape was corrected (#5046 — nested take_profit.limit_price, after a live 'oco orders require take_profit.limit_price' refusal that failed safe). VERIFIED 2026-06-29T14:01Z: re-armed GTC OCO on broker-naked alpaca_paper SLV (no refusal), idempotent skip thereafter. REMAINING (this item stays OPEN, monitoring): (a) SOAK-VERIFY the re-arm keeps the ETF positions protected across RTH closes/weekends over ~1 week (no naked gaps, no OCO stacking, no churn); (b) ENTRY-TIME GTC half (operator chose 'Both') — now DE-RISKED since the GTC OCO shape is confirmed accepted; build it (market entry + immediate GTC OCO, or flip the bracket leg TIF) only once the soak confirms the re-arm path is clean. Next check: ~2026-07-06 (1wk soak) or sooner if a naked gap recurs.

items.resolution: VERIFIED ALREADY IMPLEMENTED (2026-07-04 audit during the operator-picked backlog drain; the fix landed in a prior session but this item was never closed). Evidence: (a) order_monitor._attempt_naked_autoprotect has a per-broker Alpaca branch (GTC OCO via AlpacaClient.place_protective) alongside the IB OCA path — its docstring cites this BL id; (b) the detection gap this item described (journal keeps sl/tp while the day-TIF broker legs die at the RTH close) is closed by the dedicated _check_broker_naked_equity_positions sweep (AlpacaClient.has_protective_orders per open Alpaca position; read-failure => skip, never a blind re-arm), wired unconditionally into the monitor tick (order_monitor.py:6503); (c) tests/test_alpaca_naked_rearm.py — 8/8 pass. Bybit/OANDA remain deliberate no-ops (atomic SL/TP at entry, no session-boundary expiry).

items.title: [alerting] A broker account being DOWN/unreachable warrants its own loud Telegram warning + a remediation trigger (gap: IB-dark went unflagged across reviews)
items.description: OPERATOR-RAISED 2026-06-29: the IB gateway was DOWN (ib_paper/ib_live positions=null, MES/MGC/MHG dark) across one or more /system-review runs and it was NOT surfaced loudly — it landed in the report body, not as a can't-miss alert, and the gateway watchdog only Telegrams after EXHAUSTING its restart budget (a terse restart ping easy to skim past). There is no dedicated 'account X unreachable' warning and no remediation trigger. REQUIREMENT: when a SUPPOSED-TO-BE-LIVE broker account reads unreachable (diag exchange_positions positions=null / accounts/balances api_ok=false / IB net_liquidation=None) for >= N consecutive checks, fire its OWN high-priority Telegram [ALERT] (latched: one on cross-into-down, one [OK] on recovery — mirror the daily-cap latch, no per-tick spam), and recommend the fix (vm-ib-gateway-recover / open a /health-review session). DESIGN PROPOSAL: (a) a small latched per-account 'broker reachable' check on an existing periodic path (hourly-report or a dedicated ~5-15min watcher), scoped to accounts declared live — EXCLUDE intentionally-shelved accounts (oanda_practice, ib_live-2FA-blocked) via an allowlist/skip flag so they don't spam; (b) make the health-review + system-review skills treat any down live account as a MANDATORY flags_raised item that fires a standalone high-priority ping (not buried in the report). Optional stretch: the alert auto-opens a remediation issue/session. Tier-1/2 (observability + a live-VM check/alert); operator wants it built — confirm scope (IB-only vs all live accounts; include auto-open-session vs alert-only) before shipping.
items.source: operator request 2026-06-29 (IB-dark went unflagged)
items.resolution: Confirmed resolved: src/runtime/account_reachability_alert.py is shipped and wired into src/main.py (per-tick call at line ~698); documented in CLAUDE.md env-var table (ACCOUNT_REACHABILITY_CHECK_SECONDS et al). The requested standalone loud DOWN/OK Telegram + FCM alert exists.

items.title: [infra] live trader VM /dev/null clobbered into a regular file — blocks all operator-action wrappers (pull-and-deploy / restart / reboot) and every '>/dev/null' redirect
items.description: DISCOVERED 2026-06-29 while deploying #5061: the pull-and-deploy system-action FAILED with 'ERROR: /dev/null is not writable on this host — it has been clobbered into a regular file, so every ">/dev/null" redirect (and most operator-action wrappers) fail.' On the LIVE trader VM (ict-bot-arm, 141.145.193.91), /dev/null is a regular file instead of the char-special device (1,3). IMPACT: (a) every operator-action wrapper that preflight-checks or redirects to /dev/null aborts — pull-and-deploy, restart-bot-service, reboot-vm are all blocked (so the relay can't even self-repair this), (b) any process doing '>/dev/null' fails, (c) a future systemd restart of ict-trader-live whose ExecStart/redirects touch /dev/null could fail to come up. NOT currently fatal: ict-git-sync still auto-deployed #5061 (trader confirmed on git_sha d661a0ec, uptime ~104s, heartbeat running, ticks healthy) and the trader is alive — but this is a latent live-VM hazard. ROOT FIX (needs root ON the VM; no autonomous path — the action wrappers that would run it are themselves blocked by the same /dev/null breakage, so this is a genuine operator hand-off): `sudo rm -f /dev/null && sudo mknod -m 666 /dev/null c 1 3`. After repair, re-dispatch pull-and-deploy to confirm the operator-action path works again. FOLLOW-UP: find what clobbered /dev/null (a stray '>' to the literal path /dev/null from a process that ran with /dev not mounted, or a script bug writing to '/dev/null' as a file) so it doesn't recur; consider a cheap health-check assertion that /dev/null is char-special (1,3).
items.source: pull-and-deploy failure 2026-06-29 (issue #5069) + diag confirm

items.title: [infra root-source] stop the OCI host agent clobbering /dev/null on the live VM (the recurring source behind the guard + self-heals)
items.description: The live trader VM's /dev/null is repeatedly clobbered by an OS-level OCI host agent (suspected oracle-cloud-agent oci-wlp / Cloud Guard Workload-Protection FIM 'remediating' world-writable files, incorrectly including /dev/null) — see docs/runbooks/devnull-guard.md. As of 2026-06-29 we now have THREE self-heal layers (ict-devnull-guard.timer 60s, deploy_pull_restart.sh, and _lib.sh::require_systemctl — the last two added this session) plus the guard %-specifier bugfix, so consumers no longer break on a transient clobber. REMAINING: kill it at the source so /dev/null isn't being rm'd/chmod'd at all. AUTONOMOUS path to try first (SSH+sudo via a runner, NOT operator-manual): `sudo auditctl -w /dev/null -p wa -k devnull` then after it next flips `sudo ausearch -k devnull` to positively identify the writing process/agent; if it's a local oracle-cloud-agent plugin, disable that plugin's /dev remediation via its on-VM config. OPERATOR/OCI-CONSOLE path (only if the rule is tenancy-level): exclude /dev from the OCI Cloud Guard / Workload-Protection FIM profile attached to the instance — needs OCI console/tenancy access (a legitimate category-1 operator/external action). OPTIONAL mitigation regardless: tighten ict-devnull-guard.timer OnUnitActiveSec 60s -> ~20-30s to shrink the exposure window for any non-self-healing consumer (e.g. the trader's own >/dev/null subprocess plumbing).

items.title: [verify/soak] confirm the intent-reduce partial-close fix (#5101) actually stops the open->reconciler-close churn on the live trader
items.description: FIX DEPLOYED 2026-06-29: #5101 (merged + deployed, live trader HEAD 2b723d42) reworks an intent-mode reduce_only leg from a phantom status='open' opposite-direction row (which the reverse reconciler immediately closed as reconciler_filled, snapping the DB net back and looping the same reduce every tick) into a FIFO PARTIAL-CLOSE of the parent position (decrement open parent rows + one closed setup_type='intent_reduce' audit row, exit_reason='intent_reduce_executed'). 8 unit tests + adjacent suites green. NEEDS LIVE SOAK VERIFICATION (next reduce cycle was ~every 28 min on bybit_1 BTCUSDT htf_pullback_trend_2h; pre-fix examples DB ids 3041/3043): confirm via diag /journal?table=trades that (a) NO new intent_reduce rows close as 'reconciler_filled' (the churn signature is gone), (b) a real reduce now appears as a parent-row decrement + an 'intent_reduce_executed' closed audit row, and (c) the DB net (current_net_position_qty) converges toward the exchange-truth net (pre-fix the DB showed BTCUSDT net 0.761 while the exchange held 0.17). Also confirm no spurious open/close ping spam recurs. If the churn persists, re-open the order-accounting investigation. Owner note: PnL on reduce-closed chunks is deferred-NULL by design (pnl_source='deferred_intent_reduce') — the local-PnL sweep / Bybit closed-pnl lookup is the SSOT; sanity-check it marks those chunks without double-counting during the soak.

items.source: generate-strategy-review-packets action (#5243)
items.title: generate-strategy-review-packets print_packets:true cats wrong dir (DATA_DIR path mismatch)
items.description: The system-action wrapper's print_packets step looks at relative 'runtime_logs/strategy_reviews/<date>/' and prints '(no packets written - review_dir absent)', but the packets are written under $DATA_DIR -> /data/bot-data/runtime_logs/strategy_reviews/<date>/. So print_packets returns nothing inline on the live VM (DATA_DIR=/data/bot-data); a sandbox session must fetch each packet via /api/bot/strategies/{name}/review instead. FIX: point the wrapper's cat at the resolved runtime_logs_dir()/DATA_DIR path.
items.trigger_condition: print_packets:true on the live VM returns no inline packets
items.resolution_criteria: wrapper cats the correct DATA_DIR path; inline reasons appear
items.resolution: Fixed: scripts/ops/generate_strategy_review_packets_action.sh now resolves REVIEW_DIR via load_runtime_env (RUNTIME_LOGS_DIR/DATA_DIR), matching scripts/ml/strategy_review_packet.py runtime_logs_dir() default, instead of the wrong REPO_DIR-relative path.

items.source: The RPT-20260702-061700-since-last /system-review report flagged btc-regime-1h-lgbm-yz-v1 and btc-regime-5m-lgbm-yz-v1 as advisory-stage models that had stopped producing shadow_predictions rows since 2026-06-23 / 2026-06-25. Diagnosis (vm-diag-request shadow_stats?model_id=...&stage=shadow on both, 2026-07-02T07:43Z) found both models ACTIVELY logging shadow-stage predictions with last_seen within minutes of the query (630 / 10169 records, healthy non-degenerate score distributions) — not stale at all.
items.title: ml-review staleness check should track a model's CURRENT registry stage, not the stage it was at when first observed
items.description: Root cause of the false alarm: both models were demoted advisory→shadow around 2026-06-23 / 2026-06-25 (MB-20260623-001 / MB-20260625-001, degenerate live-score reasons — the yz vol_bucket-edge calibration gap documented in src/runtime/regime_bar_scoring.py's own module docstring). ShadowPredictor logs whatever `stage` value it was instantiated with, so predictions before the demotion carry stage="advisory" and predictions after carry stage="shadow" for the SAME model_id. The staleness check that fed the system-review evidently kept querying (or reasoning about) the OLD stage label instead of re-resolving the model's current registry stage before judging it stale — so a clean, expected demotion looked identical to a silent failure. No code fix needed for the models themselves (they are healthy); the fix is in the review methodology: any future staleness/liveness check for a shadow model should look up its CURRENT target_deployment_stage from the registry first, then query shadow_stats for that stage specifically — never assume the stage recorded at first-flagged-stale time is still current.
items.trigger_condition: Next /ml-review or /system-review session that flags a shadow/advisory model as stale or silently dead.
items.resolution_criteria: The staleness-check step in the ml-review / system-review skill (wherever it lives) re-resolves each candidate model's current registry stage before querying shadow_stats, rather than reusing a stage captured earlier in the session or assumed from a prior finding.
items.resolution: Diagnosed as a false alarm, not a bug in the models or the shadow-logging pipeline (Track C of the 2026-07-02 system-review follow-up plan). Both btc-regime-1h-lgbm-yz-v1 and btc-regime-5m-lgbm-yz-v1 confirmed actively producing shadow-stage predictions in real time. Logged here as a methodology note for the next /ml-review or /system-review session to avoid re-flagging the same false positive — no code changed.

items.source: gpu-burst-train.yml run 28772601594 (arm-B burst issue #5642) was CANCELLED while queued: the workflow triggers on EVERY issues.opened (non-labeled ones conclude 'skipped'), and its single `concurrency: group: gpu-burst-train` means each new issue's run displaces the previously PENDING run in the group - so an unrelated trainer-diag/diag-request issue opened while one burst runs silently cancels the next queued burst.
items.title: gpu-burst-train concurrency group lets ANY new issue cancel a queued burst
items.description: Queued (pending) gpu-burst runs are silently cancelled by any subsequent issue in the repo because all issues.opened events share the one concurrency group. A cancelled burst looks like 'never ran' unless someone inspects the Actions history. Observed 2026-07-06 (arm B #5642 cancelled by the #5643 trainer-diag issue's skip-run); worked around by firing bursts one at a time.
items.suggested_fix: Gate the concurrency group on the label match (e.g. move the label check to a first job whose output gates the burst job, and key concurrency as gpu-burst-${{ github.event.issue.number }} on the burst job only, or set the group at job level only for labeled issues). Keep spend-gate serialization by having the burst job itself take a repo-level lock (the ledger commit step already serializes on push).
items.trigger_condition: Next health-review / CI-touching session.

items.source: gpu-burst-train run 28773344221 (arm-A retry, issue #5647): the end-of-job ledger commit push to main was rejected 'fetch first' because main advanced during the ~8-min burst (PR #5645 merged mid-run). The workflow only warns - the $0.0257 row was silently dropped. The 06:51Z preflight also showed month-to-date $0.04, i.e. the earlier failed-run row (gpu-28772600180, $0.0167) was ALSO missing - same race with #5635's merge.
items.title: gpu-burst ledger persistence to main is BROKEN (branch protection rejects the direct push; races also observed)
items.description: THREE rows dropped 2026-07-06 (gpu-28772600180 $0.0167 failed-run, gpu-28773344221 $0.0257, gpu-28773756599 $0.0334; reconciled by hand same morning - July now $0.116/$10). Two failure modes observed: (a) 'fetch first' stale-ref race when main advanced during the burst; (b) GH006 'Protected branch update failed - 9 of 9 required status checks are expected' on run 28773756599 - main's protection now blocks the workflow's direct push OUTRIGHT, so every future burst run will drop its ledger row until this is fixed. The ledger is the budget GATE input; silent under-counting defeats it.
items.suggested_fix: Direct push to main can no longer work under the protection rules. Options: (1) have the workflow open/append to a ledger PR with auto-merge (checks are cheap and would pass); (2) write the row to a ledger BRANCH consumed by summarize_spend from both main+branch; (3) grant the Actions bot a bypass-list entry for the protection rule (operator decision). Until fixed, reconcile rows by hand from the run logs after each burst (record_run.py locally with the logged cost facts).

items.source: Operator-reported GitHub Actions billing screenshot: 2000/2000 free minutes exhausted mid-cycle (reset 2026-08-01). Investigation found ~427 issues + ~123 PRs opened July 1-6 (~5.5 days); ~90% of issues are vm-diag-request/trainer-vm-diag-request/system-action relay calls (one single weekly-review session alone opened 33 separate relay issues), and every PR push fans out across 24 unfiltered CI workflow files. Operator added $20 budget as an interim top-up.
items.title: [ops] Monitor GitHub Actions minutes burn-rate for real after the CI short-circuit + diag-relay-batching fix (PR #5789) lands
items.description: PR #5789 landed two fixes aimed at cutting Actions minutes consumption: (1) a changed-files short-circuit on the 9 required + 7 non-required CI workflows so a docs-only push skips expensive pip-install/run steps (est. ~25-35 min/push -> ~10-12 min/push), and (2) multi-path batching support in vm-diag-snapshot.yml so a review session can pull several diag paths in one issue/one SSH session instead of one issue per path (relay batching is opt-in via the issue BODY carrying multiple paths -- the single-path title fallback still works, so adoption is not automatically enforced). Combined estimate was directional only (~100-230 min/day, ~3,000-7,000 min/month) since GitHub Actions was billing-blocked at investigation time and none of it could be exercised live. Operator decision (2026-07-06): hold off on the bigger VM-ops-API build (a direct authenticated interface on the live VM bypassing GitHub Actions for reads/mutations) and watch the REAL numbers for a few days first before deciding if it is still needed. ACTION FOR THE NEXT FEW /health-review, /performance-review, /ml-review, and /system-review runs: check Settings -> Billing -> Actions usage (or ask the operator to share the day-by-day minutes graph, since no billing API is exposed to these tools) and report the actual post-fix daily/monthly burn rate against the ~100-230 min/day estimate. Also spot-check whether recent review sessions are actually using the new batched vm-diag-snapshot.yml pattern (one issue, multiple paths in the body) instead of the old one-issue-per-path pattern documented pre-fix -- if not, note that adoption gap explicitly, since the relay-batching saving is opt-in only. Close this item once 3-5 real days of post-fix data confirms the estimate held (or note where it diverged) and the operator has decided whether the VM-ops-API build is still warranted.

items.source: diag relay issues #5827/#5829/#5831 (2026-07-07): GET /api/bot/health/latest and /api/bot/health/services both returned non-2xx (curl --fail -> 'fetch_failed') on TWO independent pulls this session, and /api/bot/accounts/balances failed the same way once, while every sibling /api/bot/* route in the SAME ssh batch (stats, positions, order-packages, trades/closed, strategies/*/review, candles, config, etc.) succeeded. Code review: src/web/api/routers/accounts.py::get_account_balances is `async def` but calls `_from_db()` -> `Database().get_latest_balance_snapshots()` synchronously (a blocking sqlite3 read against a 433MB trade_journal.db under constant live-trader write load), not wrapped in run_in_threadpool/asyncio.to_thread.
items.title: GET /api/bot/health/{latest,services} and /api/bot/accounts/balances intermittently return non-2xx -- likely a blocking DB call stalling uvicorn's single event loop under load
items.description: Reproduced twice this session (diag #5827 first batch, #5829 targeted retry) for health/latest + health/services; accounts/balances failed once (#5831), succeeded in other pulls. Hypothesis: `accounts.py::_from_db()` runs a synchronous sqlite3 read directly inside an `async def` route with no thread-pool offload -- if that read blocks (WAL lock contention / long fsync under the live trader's constant write volume), it freezes uvicorn's whole single-worker event loop, starving every other in-flight request (which is why health/latest and health/services, both otherwise-trivial file/subprocess reads, failed in the same window). This co-occurred with a live IB-gateway circuit-breaker trip (BL-20260623-002 recurrence) and a burst of ~80 near-simultaneous dashboard/Android requests in the same ~90s window (ict-web-api journalctl, diag #5829) -- consistent with event-loop contention, though not proven without a direct trace. A similar class of bug was just fixed for IB account/position reads in commit 1f286ee (BL-20260706-IBCONCURRENCY, 'offload IB account/position reads off uvicorn's event loop') -- this may be the same pattern in a different router. FIX (Tier-1, API code, no live-trade-path risk): wrap `Database().get_latest_balance_snapshots()` in `asyncio.to_thread(...)` (or equivalent) inside `accounts.py::get_account_balances`; audit `health_snapshots.py::get_services`'s `subprocess.run` similarly (it already has timeout=3 but is still a blocking call inside an async route). Verify by re-pulling health/latest + health/services + accounts/balances via the diag relay several times back-to-back and confirming no repeat 'fetch_failed', ideally during/after a deliberately-induced DB-busy window.

items.source: trainer-vm-diag-request issues #5828 and #5830 (2026-07-07, two independent attempts ~2 min apart): both returned 'Connection timed out during banner exchange / Connection to 158.178.209.121 port 22 timed out' -- the relay's SSH client never got a TCP/SSH banner from the trainer VM at all.
items.title: Trainer VM (158.178.209.121) unreachable via SSH -- two consecutive connection timeouts during banner exchange, ict-trainer.service health could not be verified this review
items.description: This is a network-level failure (no SSH banner at all, not an auth/command failure), so the trainer VM is either down, rebooting, or its OCI security list / SSH daemon is wedged. Not a live-trading blocker (trainer VM carries no order authority) but it does block the mandatory (light-touch) trainer_service health check this review, and blocks the trainer-VM-dependent skills (/ml-review, model-training) entirely until resolved. FIX/NEXT STEP: retry the trainer-vm-diag relay next session; if it still times out, this needs an OCI-console-level check (the one genuinely operator-only action per the trainer VM's autonomous-territory contract) -- Claude has no reboot/console-access workflow for the trainer VM analogous to the live VM's reboot-vm system-action. Resolution: a subsequent trainer-vm-diag-request issue succeeds (SSH banner received, command output returned).
