items.source: journal?table=trades (diag relay #5836), trade id 3224: 2026-07-07T02:15:10Z, mgc_pullback_1d short MGC on ib_paper, status=exchange_rejected. entry_reason: 'RuntimeError: IB _submit_order: IBClient: Gateway at 10.0.0.251:4002 (account=…5724) answered the liveness probe but never delivered account/portfolio data within 8s (likely logged out or no account resolved). Tripping circuit breaker so IB calls do not block the trader loop.'
items.title: IB account/portfolio-warmup timeout recurred on ib_paper the day after BL-20260706-IBWARMUP / BL-20260706-IBACCTUPDATES-COLLISION landed — confirm the fix is actually deployed + effective
items.description: This is the exact failure signature (`IB_ACCOUNT_WARMUP_TIMEOUT_S` probe-then-condemn path in `IBClient.connect()`/`_warm_account_data`) that BL-20260706-IBWARMUP and its BL-20260706-IBACCTUPDATES-COLLISION follow-up were supposed to fix on 2026-07-06. Seeing it trip again on 2026-07-07T02:15Z blocked a legitimate mgc_pullback_1d short (confidence 0.55, trending regime ADX 37.9 — a decent setup that never got a chance to fill; see performance-review scoring for pkg-8d2b5d6cffbc4552). Two possibilities: (a) the fix reduces but does not eliminate cold-TCP-flow misses (the retry-then-condemn design accepts SOME residual failures by construction, per IB_PROBE_TIMEOUT_S's own docs — in which case this is expected background noise, not a regression); or (b) the fix hasn't actually rolled to the live VM (check `/api/diag/version` git_sha against the PR merge commit) or isn't retrying as designed. FIX: pull `/api/diag/version` + `/api/diag/journalctl?unit=ict-trader-live.service` around 2026-07-07T02:14-02:16Z to see whether `_warm_account_data` actually attempted its documented retry before condemning the connection; if the retry never fired, the deploy or the retry logic itself needs a second look. If it did retry and still failed, this is within the documented residual-failure envelope and just needs a frequency count over a longer window before deciding whether IB_ACCOUNT_WARMUP_TIMEOUT_S/IB_PROBE_RETRY_GAP_S need raising.
items.trigger_condition: Next health-review or VM investigation session; also re-check if this recurs multiple times in a short window (would argue for (b) over (a)).
items.resolution_criteria: Either confirmed as expected residual noise within the documented retry-tolerant design (close as won't-fix, cite the retry evidence), or confirmed as a deploy/logic gap and fixed with a follow-up PR.
items.resolution: NOT A REGRESSION — outcome (a): the retry-tolerant warm-up worked exactly as designed and correctly condemned a GENUINELY-WEDGED gateway. Definitive evidence from the narrow journalctl window (diag relay #5847, 2026-07-07T02:13-02:16Z on ict-trader-live.service): the gateway (10.0.0.251:4002, clientId 497) failed the LIVENESS PROBE repeatedly 02:13:05-02:14:47 ('liveness probe attempt 1/2 timed out after 5s ... retrying once'), then on the mgc_pullback_1d dispatch at 02:14:52 the probe passed but the WARM-UP 'timed out TWICE (8s + 8s retry)' at 02:15:10 -> 'gateway likely logged out' -> breaker tripped (consecutive failures=1) -> order rejected. So the documented retry (BL-20260706-IBWARMUP / BL-20260610-009) DID fire on both the probe and the warm-up; the gateway genuinely wasn't answering either attempt. Deploy confirmed current at the time (/api/diag/version git_sha=3690317e, well after the 2026-07-06 fix commits). This was an UNSCHEDULED gateway wedge (02:15Z is before IBKR's ~03:45-05:45 reset window), and the gateway recovered on its own (a live exchange_positions read on ib_paper succeeded at 10:05Z). CONCLUSION: raising IB_ACCOUNT_WARMUP_TIMEOUT_S / adding retries would be the WRONG fix — the gateway was DOWN, not slow, and a longer timeout would only block the trader loop longer. The warm-up/breaker path is correct: it isolated the dead gateway from the loop, which is its job. The two GENUINE gaps this exposed are NOT in the warmup code: (1) gateway-wedge RECOVERY LATENCY (reactive gateway-VM watchdog ~10-15min + daily 06:05 reset), and (2) DROPPED DAILY SIGNALS — a 1d strategy fires once, so a wedge at the exact fire moment loses the signal entirely with no retry. (2) is the real hardening opportunity (retry a breaker-open-rejected order across the next few ticks) and is surfaced to the operator as a Tier-2/3 design option; if pursued it gets its own item, not this one. Observability for all of this now ships via /api/diag/ib_state (PR #5845, merged e08fe3a).

items.source: journal?table=trades (diag relay #5836), trade ids 3226/3227 (mgc_trend_1h short MGC, ib_paper, 2026-07-07 05:25/06:00Z): `entry_reason` reads 'REJECTED: dry_run_no_order_placed | mgc_trend_1h signal' and `notes.reason` is 'dry_run_no_order_placed' with `notes.is_dry: false` — but the linked order_package's `meta.rejected_reason` says 'risk-refused (no fill placed); reconciled from the journalled rejection row' and `meta.rejected_by: monitor_reconciler`.
items.title: mgc_trend_1h rejections carry a stale/mismatched 'dry_run_no_order_placed' entry_reason text while the order-package meta records the real cause as risk-refused (reconciled)
items.description: Two data points disagree about WHY the same rejected trade happened: the `trades.entry_reason`/`notes.reason` literally say 'dry_run_no_order_placed' (which would imply `is_dry: true`), yet the same row's `notes.is_dry` is `false` and the linked order_package's `meta` says the real cause was a risk-manager refusal later reconciled by `monitor_reconciler`. This is confusing for anyone (human or /performance-review) reading `trades.entry_reason` at face value — it doesn't match the account's actual dry-run state or the order package's own recorded rejection cause. Likely a stale/leftover reason string written at an earlier point in the rejection path that never got updated when the reconciler backfilled the real cause into `meta`. FIX (Tier-1, observability/data-integrity, no live-trade-path change): trace where `entry_reason='dry_run_no_order_placed'` gets written for a `mgc_trend_1h`/ib_paper rejection (likely `src/runtime/orders.py` or the risk-refusal path in `RiskManager`) and confirm it reflects the account's actual `is_dry` state, or have the reconciler backfill the same corrected text into `trades.entry_reason` (not just `order_packages.meta`) so the two never disagree again.
items.trigger_condition: Next health-review or a session touching the risk-refusal/reconciler rejection-reason write path.
items.resolution_criteria: Either the root cause is found and the reason text is corrected (both fields agree), or the discrepancy is explained as intentional (e.g. two independent 'reason' fields with legitimately different meanings) and documented so it stops looking like a bug.

updates.note: ROOT FRAME CLARIFIED (narrows the fix, item stays open): the 'dry_run_no_order_placed' entry_reason is NOT a stale/wrong string — mgc_trend_1h is config execution:shadow (config/strategies.yaml L785), so the STRATEGY-level shadow gate folds into effective_dry in Coordinator.multi_account_execute even though the ACCOUNT (ib_paper) is mode:live and notes.is_dry (the account-level flag) is correctly false. So 'dry_run_no_order_placed' is the CORRECT disposition for a shadow-suppressed package; there is no is_dry contradiction. The ONE genuine inconsistency left is cosmetic: the reconciler backfills order_packages.meta.rejected_reason='risk-refused (no fill placed); reconciled from the journalled rejection row' for these shadow-suppressed packages, which is misleading — they were shadow-suppressed, not risk-refused. FIX (Tier-1, cosmetic): have the reconciler label a shadow/dry-suppressed no-fill as such (e.g. 'shadow_suppressed_no_order' / 'dry_run_no_order_placed') rather than the generic 'risk-refused' when the underlying journal row's reason is dry_run/shadow. Downgraded in practical severity — nothing is mis-executing; a reader just needs to know shadow==effective_dry.

items.title: Config-only PRs can merge with a RED full test suite — the filtered pytest-run skips the config-assertion tests
items.description: Twice this session, config/docs-heavy PRs (#5850 turtle_soup demote + ib_paper prune; #5851 pull-ibkr-history system-action) merged GREEN via a fast/filtered pytest-run that did NOT execute the full suite, leaving main's full-suite CI RED: test_strategy_execution_gate (turtle_soup now shadow), test_ib_integration x2 (ib_paper strategy list), test_intent_delta_dispatch (turtle_soup-as-live premise), test_system_actions_workflow + test_notify_run (pull-ibkr-history allowlist/priority). The failures were only exposed when the NEXT PR (#5853) touched real code and triggered the full 7192-test run. A config-only change that breaks a config-assertion test should NOT be able to merge green. Likely the pytest-run job path-filters to changed files/affected tests and a config/yaml/workflow change resolves to 'no affected tests'. FIX OPTIONS: (a) always run the full suite on changes to config/**, .github/workflows/**, docs that back tests; or (b) map config/strategies.yaml + config/accounts.yaml + the workflow allowlist to their assertion tests in the affected-test selector; or (c) a nightly full-suite run on main that alarms on red. All 5+ tests were fixed in #5853, so main is green now — this backlog item is about the PROCESS gap that let it go red.
items.trigger_condition: Next CI/tooling pass, or when a config PR merges green but a later PR's full suite goes red.
items.resolution_criteria: A config-only PR that breaks a config-assertion test is caught before merge (full-suite-on-config-paths, an affected-test mapping, or a main nightly full run).

items.title: ROOT CAUSE CONFIRMED (bot-caused, not broker-side): a mass reconciler false-close on 2026-07-07T14:15:56Z hid ~$218k of still-open, uncapped concurrent notional exposure on alpaca_paper — see BL-20260707-RECONCILER-MASS-FALSE-CLOSE for the mechanism
items.description: Investigated a 'ALL accounts failed to dispatch' alert (spy_pullback_1h/SPY, alpaca_paper + alpaca_live). alpaca_live's risk_refused was plain underfunding — not a bug. alpaca_paper's zero_balance (shared Alpaca paper login with alpaca_options_paper, both ~-$67,946) took several rounds to trace because the trades journal's own pnl was actively misleading (see below). FINAL MECHANISM, confirmed via a fresh diag pull at 2026-07-07T16:19-16:28Z, minutes after it happened: at 2026-07-07T14:15:56.749880Z, `position_snapshot_reconciler` closed MULTIPLE alpaca_paper positions across DIFFERENT symbols simultaneously (same closed_at timestamp on trades 3131 SPY 125sh, 3077 SPY 142sh — wait, distinct order_package_ids but same closed_at batch also covers 3060 SLV 1360sh, 2868 GLD 33.6sh, 2774 TLT 231sh, and more), each stamped `pnl_source: local_compute` / `exit_price_source: local_markprice` (a FABRICATED mark-to-market, not a real fill) — almost certainly because the reconciler's exchange-positions read came back empty/misread at that moment and it concluded every open position was flat (the same failure class as the historical 'AlpacaClient.positions() returning [] instead of None on a read failure' bug this repo has hardened against elsewhere, e.g. RECONCILER_SNAPSHOT_MIN_FILL_AGE_S). The individual fabricated PnLs looked small/benign (+$805, -$441, +$1706, -$434, -$176, etc.) — which is why an earlier pass through this backlog entry incorrectly concluded the journal 'didn't show a loss anywhere near -$67,946' and pointed the operator at Alpaca's own activity screen instead. ~2 hours later, at 2026-07-07T16:19:38-45Z, the REVERSE reconciler discovered the SAME positions were STILL genuinely open on the broker (they never actually closed) and re-adopted all of them fresh as `adopted_orphan` rows: SPY 125sh (~$92.6k), SLV 1360sh (~$73.5k), TLT 10sh, QQQ 16sh (~$11.9k), IWM 9sh (~$2.7k), IEF 8sh (~$753) — all simultaneously open on the SAME $100k paper account, ~$180k+ of concurrent notional exposure stacked across independently-sized strategies (spy_pullback_1h, slv_trend_1h, qqq_pullback_1h, iwm_trend_long_1d, ief_pullback_1d, tlt_pullback_1d/1h), each individually risk-bounded at trade-open time but with NO portfolio-level aggregate cap across strategies on one account. THIS is genuinely bot-caused: (1) the reconciler bug corrupted the journal's view of reality (false closes with fabricated PnL), which is why searching the journal for 'the loss' kept coming up empty; (2) the real financial exposure was always sitting on the broker the whole time, continuously, as an uncapped multi-strategy stack — exactly the mechanism BL-20260707-ALPACA-PULLBACK-NOTIONAL-SIZING flagged for a single trade, but worse in aggregate across concurrently-open positions. The operator's report that 'the old account reset itself to $100k' does NOT match this diag pull (accounts/balances as_of 2026-07-07T16:01Z still reads -$67,945.12 for both alpaca_paper and alpaca_options_paper) — worth the operator double-checking which account/view showed the reset (possibly the NEW dedicated alpaca_options_paper account, which would legitimately start at $100k when first created).
items.trigger_condition: Next /health-review or /system-review; also whenever alpaca_paper/alpaca_options_paper dispatch is checked.
items.resolution_criteria: Fix BL-20260707-RECONCILER-MASS-FALSE-CLOSE (the reconciler defect) and BL-20260707-ALPACA-PULLBACK-NOTIONAL-SIZING (the per-trade + portfolio-level sizing gap) so this class of incident can't recur; separately the shared Alpaca paper login's equity needs an operator-side reset via the Alpaca paper dashboard (Claude has no Alpaca dashboard access) once the currently-open positions are accounted for. The dedicated alpaca_options_paper login (PR #5879 part B, placeholder secrets ALPACA_API_KEY_ID_OPTIONS / ALPACA_API_SECRET_KEY_OPTIONS) fixes the SHARED-BLOCKING symptom but not this root cause.

items.title: position_snapshot_reconciler mass-closed multiple alpaca_paper positions simultaneously with fabricated local-mark PnL, then the reverse reconciler re-adopted them ~2h later still genuinely open on the broker — the positions never actually closed
items.description: At 2026-07-07T14:15:56.749880Z, `position_snapshot_reconciler` closed several alpaca_paper trades across different symbols in the same batch (confirmed same `closed_at` timestamp on at least SPY id 3131 (125sh, pnl local-computed +$805.63), SLV id 3060 (1360sh, +$1706.80), GLD id 2868 (33.6sh, -$434.53), TLT id 2774 (231sh, -$176.77) — likely more not yet enumerated), each with `notes.pnl_source: local_compute` + `exit_price_source: local_markprice` (the '(symbol, side) confirmed absent from the exchange open-positions snapshot across two observations' closed_reason). At 2026-07-07T16:19:38-45Z (~2h5m later), the REVERSE reconciler (`_reconcile_orphan_exchange_positions`) found the exchange reporting the SAME symbols still open with matching or near-matching entry prices/sizes and re-adopted them fresh as `adopted_orphan` rows (SPY 125sh @740.67, SLV 1360sh @54.043, TLT 10sh @85.54, QQQ 16sh @720.63, IWM 9sh @298.53, IEF 8sh @94.14) — i.e. the positions were open continuously the whole time; the 14:15:56 batch-close was WRONG. Hypothesis (not yet confirmed against logs — needs journalctl/audit around 14:15:56Z): the exchange-positions read that fed the close-confirm at that tick returned an empty/degraded snapshot (Alpaca API blip, rate limit, or a client.positions() read failure that returned [] instead of None — the exact failure class RECONCILER_SNAPSHOT_MIN_FILL_AGE_S and the AlpacaClient/OandaClient None-on-failure hardening were built to prevent, but evidently a gap remains for `position_snapshot_reconciler`'s specific close-confirm path, distinct from the reverse reconciler's orphan-adopt path those fixes covered). CONSEQUENCE: the fabricated small per-trade PnLs from the false-close made the trades journal actively misleading during the -$67,946 root-cause investigation (BL-20260707-ALPACA-PAPER-NEGATIVE-EQUITY) — every individual number looked benign while the REAL uncapped multi-position notional exposure sat unaccounted-for on the broker the whole time. FIX (Tier-2, order_monitor.py / position_snapshot_reconciler close-confirm path — needs investigation before a specific patch is proposed): (1) find and harden whatever exchange-positions call feeds this reconciler's close-confirm for Alpaca (does it correctly distinguish 'read failed' from 'genuinely flat', matching the pattern already fixed for AlpacaClient.positions()/account_open_positions elsewhere?); (2) consider whether the 2-observation confirm this path already claims to require was actually satisfied, or whether a single degraded read slipped through; (3) audit `runtime_logs/signal_audit.jsonl` / journalctl around 2026-07-07T14:10-14:20Z for the actual read that triggered this to confirm the failure mode before patching blind.
items.trigger_condition: Next /health-review or /system-review; investigate before any real-money account routes through the same close-confirm path (alpaca_live currently has only 1 tiny position, low exposure, but this is a correctness bug that would apply there too).
items.resolution_criteria: Root cause of the 14:15:56Z false-close batch is confirmed from logs (not just inferred from journal state) and a fix lands so the close-confirm path can't mass-close genuinely-open positions on a degraded/empty exchange read. Regression test: a simulated empty/failed positions() read during the close-confirm path must NOT close an open trade.

updates.note: Root cause found by static analysis of src/units/accounts/clients.py::account_open_positions (the single function position_snapshot_reconciler calls for its exchange-truth read), not from logs — journalctl around 14:15:56Z was repeatedly requested via the diag relay but the issue-comment reply is capped at ~55KB and the trader logs too verbosely per tick to fit a usable window; log-level confirmation was not achieved despite 3+ attempts with progressively narrower windows. The code-level bug is nonetheless conclusive: the Alpaca branch's empty-snapshot verification used `if not bal:` to decide whether client.balance() proved the account genuinely reachable before trusting an empty positions() read as flat. A negative float is TRUTHY in Python, so alpaca_paper's ~-$67,946 balance sailed straight through as 'verified live' and the empty read was trusted as [] (flat) — exactly the mechanism this entry hypothesized. FIX LANDED (not yet pushed/deployed): commit bf0c0fe on branch claude/spy-dispatch-failures-ir2ktj changes the guard to `bal is None` (correctly means 'read failed') plus a new explicit `bal < 0` check (anomalous account state — don't trust the empty read even though it's a successful call) — both now return None (skip, no false-close) instead of falling through to return [] (flat). `bal == 0.0` now correctly returns [] where it previously also incorrectly returned None under the old truthiness bug — an intentional, low-risk behavior change (a genuinely-zero-balance account is separately blocked from new orders by the zero_balance gate). Regression tests added in tests/test_accounts_clients_open_positions.py: test_alpaca_empty_balance_negative_returns_none, test_alpaca_empty_balance_zero_returns_flat. Full suite green (20/20 in that file; 218 passed across the broader reconciler test set). Status stays 'open' until this is pushed, PR'd, merged, deployed to the live VM, and verified live.

Deployed and live-verified. PR #5901 merged (squash sha 0297fff) with operator OK in chat. `ict-git-sync` auto-deploy confirmed via `/api/diag/version`: live VM git_sha advanced from b7fcc87f (pre-merge) to 3a41031b (post-merge, includes 0297fff in ancestry, both trader + web-api services active) within ~10 minutes of merge. Closing resolved — the code-level defect is fixed and running; genuinely-open Alpaca positions can no longer be mass-closed on a negative-or-unreadable-balance empty-snapshot read. Note: while investigating a follow-on alert (QQQ close failing with 'insufficient qty available'), confirmed via a fresh /api/diag/exchange_positions pull that alpaca_paper's positions (QQQ, SPY, IWM, IEF, TLT, USO) were never actually flat/reset — they're still genuinely open on the broker, consistent with this fix's diagnosis. A separate, unrelated bug was found in the active-close path (AlpacaClient.close() doesn't cancel resting bracket legs before flattening) — tracked as a new task, not this backlog item.

items.title: spy_pullback_1h / qqq_pullback_1h (etc.) on Alpaca can size a single trade's NOTIONAL to ~100% of account equity because a thin stop-distance-to-price ratio is divided into the risk budget with no independent leverage/notional cap
items.description: While investigating BL-20260707-ALPACA-PAPER-NEGATIVE-EQUITY, found alpaca_paper trades id 3077 (142 shares SPY, ~$105,700 notional) and id 3131 (125 shares SPY, ~$92,600 notional) — both from spy_pullback_1h, both DOLLAR-RISK-BOUNDED to ~1.5% of equity at the stop (RiskManager.position_size correctly computed qty = risk_budget / (entry-stop)), but because the 1h pullback stop sits only ~0.6-0.8% away from entry on an expensive instrument (SPY ~$740-750/share), the resulting SHARE COUNT and therefore NOTIONAL is enormous relative to equity (~90-105% of the ~$100k paper account on ONE trade). position_size's only ceiling on this is the margin/buying-power cap (`available_usd * effective_leverage * _MARGIN_SAFETY_BUFFER`), which is large enough on a healthy account to permit it. Dollar-risk-at-the-tracked-stop is correctly bounded, but real-world execution risk (slippage on a 100+ share market order, a fast move that gaps through the stop, or the stop-loss leg not actually resting on the broker as assumed) scales with NOTIONAL, not with the intended risk budget — so a nominally-1.5%-risk trade can realize a loss far larger than 1.5% if the stop doesn't execute cleanly. This is a plausible contributing mechanism to how the shared alpaca_paper/alpaca_options_paper login could have gone deeply negative, though it was NOT confirmed as the literal explanation for the -$67,946 figure (see BL-20260707-ALPACA-PAPER-NEGATIVE-EQUITY — the specific trades checked netted near-breakeven; the real loss is earlier in Alpaca's history). PROPOSED FIX (Tier-3, src/units/accounts/risk.py::position_size — order-sizing path, needs operator approval before any change): add an independent max-notional-as-fraction-of-equity clamp (e.g. a `max_position_notional_pct` risk-config knob, applied after the existing risk-budget and margin-cap sizing, floor-rounded like the existing whole-unit clamp) so a thin-stop instrument can never commit near-100% of equity to one position regardless of how the risk-budget/stop-distance division works out. Applies to any account/strategy combination with a tight stop on an expensive instrument, not just alpaca_paper/spy_pullback_1h — worth an account_compat_matrix-style check across the other *_pullback_1h cells (qqq/gld/slv/tlt) once a fix direction is agreed.
items.trigger_condition: Next /performance-review or /system-review, or when the operator is ready to review a proposed RiskManager notional-cap change.
items.resolution_criteria: Operator reviews and either approves a specific fix (e.g. a max_position_notional_pct clamp) that ships via a normal Tier-3 PR, or explicitly decides the current dollar-risk-only design is acceptable and this is closed as won't-fix.

updates.note: Closed as won't-fix. Before drafting the proposed max_position_notional_pct clamp, found that PR #4442 (2026-06-24, commit 0c6b7881d02a770e771ba3604d89460194d1fa57, operator-approved Tier-3) already deliberately REMOVED all position notional/quantity ceilings from RiskManager.position_size — the current module docstring in src/units/accounts/risk.py states explicitly: 'There is NO position-notional ceiling ... so it does not exist (operator directive 2026-06-24). Size is bounded only by the risk budget, the daily-loss budget, the margin/buying-power ceiling, and the exchange's lot size.' Implementing this backlog item's proposed fix would have directly reversed that prior explicit operator directive. Surfaced the conflict to the operator via AskUserQuestion (options: reconsider-and-add-the-cap / leave-as-is-drop-the-fix / something-narrower); operator chose 'Leave sizing as-is, drop this fix.' No code change made. The large-notional mechanism this item flagged for a single trade is still real in aggregate — see BL-20260707-RECONCILER-MASS-FALSE-CLOSE, which is a genuine bug (not a sizing-design question) and is being fixed separately.

items.title: AlpacaClient.close() rejected with "insufficient qty available for order" because it never cancels the position's resting bracket SL/TP leg before flattening
items.description: Discovered while investigating a live '🛑 Position CLOSE failing' alert for QQQ/alpaca_paper (12 consecutive close failures, error 'insufficient qty available for order (requested: 16, available: 0)'). A fresh broker-truth pull (`/api/diag/exchange_positions`) confirmed QQQ (and SPY/IWM/IEF/TLT/USO) are genuinely open on the broker — this is NOT another instance of BL-20260707-RECONCILER-MASS-FALSE-CLOSE, it's a different bug in the ACTIVE-CLOSE path. Alpaca's exact error phrasing (`available: 0` while `existing_qty` matches the full position) is the documented signature of shares already `held_for_orders` by another resting order. Every Alpaca position here was opened as a bracket order (entry + SL + TP legs placed atomically, per AlpacaClient.place()'s docstring), so the original protective stop leg is still resting on the broker holding the full qty. `AlpacaClient.close()` (src/units/accounts/alpaca_client.py) just issued `DELETE /v2/positions/{symbol}` with no cancel-first step, unlike `IBClient.close()` (which explicitly cancels resting protective legs before the opposing order) and `AlpacaClient.place_protective()` (which already calls the same `_cancel_open_orders_for_symbol()` helper before re-arming, to avoid OCO stacking). Since nothing ever cancelled the stale leg, the close retried the identical failing DELETE every monitor tick indefinitely, leaving the DB row permanently open. FIX: `close()` now calls `self._cancel_open_orders_for_symbol(symbol)` before the flatten DELETE, mirroring the IB contract and reusing the helper `place_protective` already relies on. Regression tests added in tests/test_alpaca_wiring.py (`test_client_close_cancels_resting_orders_first`, `test_client_close_no_resting_orders_is_single_call`) pin that cancels happen, target the right order ids, and happen BEFORE the flatten call; existing `test_client_close_idempotent_on_404` still passes unchanged. This is a real-money order-path fix — `close_open_position` → `AlpacaClient.close()` is shared by alpaca_paper AND alpaca_live.
items.trigger_condition: Already fixed this session; re-open only if a close still fails with the same error after this fix deploys.
items.resolution_criteria: Fix ships, deploys to the live VM, and a subsequent close attempt on an affected symbol (QQQ or any of SPY/IWM/IEF/TLT/USO, whichever exits next) succeeds instead of repeating 'insufficient qty available'.

updates.note: PR #5918 merged (squash sha 2a8a91f), confirmed in main's ancestry. Deploy verification folded into the next entry's deploy check (both landed close together).

items.title: AlpacaClient.close() treated Alpaca's HTTP-accept as a confirmed flatten — a close accepted right after market close never actually filled, fabricating a phantom-closed row with a made-up PnL, then re-adopted 7 minutes later as a fresh orphan; the re-adopt guard had zero protection for this because it only matched setup_type='adopted_orphan'
items.description: Live incident: `_close_unattributable_orphan` (exit_coverage_resolver) closed an orphaned SLV short (1360sh @ $53.94) at 2026-07-07T20:33:45Z (4:33pm ET, just after the US equity close). `AlpacaClient.close()`'s DELETE /v2/positions/SLV returned HTTP 200 (accepted); the code treated that as confirmed-flat, fabricated a local mark-to-market PnL (-$693.60, pnl_source=local_compute), and marked the trade row closed. The position never actually flattened on the broker. 7 minutes later the reverse reconciler found SLV still fully open (same 1360sh, same $53.94 entry) and bare-adopted it as a brand-new orphan trade row — TWO DB rows now exist for the SAME broker position, the phantom-closed one carrying a fabricated realized PnL that is never corrected. Root cause: `close_open_position`'s Alpaca branch (src/units/accounts/execute.py) and AlpacaClient.close() had no fill-confirmation step, unlike IBClient.close()'s IB_CLOSE_CONFIRM_S contract (poll the live position, require it to actually reach flat within a bounded window before declaring success). Compounding factor: the just-shipped resting-order-cancel fix (BL-20260707-ALPACA-CLOSE-RESTING-ORDER-BLOCK, PR #5918) cancels the protective bracket BEFORE the flatten attempt, so a phantom-closed position is genuinely NAKED on the broker for the entire gap — and invisible to `_check_naked_positions` (which only scans status='open' rows), so there was zero monitoring or alerting on the exposure window either. Separately, the re-adopt flap guard (RECONCILER_READOPT_GUARD_SECONDS, BL-20260618-RECONCILE-DUP) only matched rows with setup_type='adopted_orphan' — a phantom-closed STRATEGY-ATTRIBUTED row (still carrying its original strategy's setup_type) fell through with ZERO flap protection, not even the 300s SLV incidentally got via its own setup_type. Full investigation ordered by the operator ('do a full audit of the entire Alpaca pipeline... I don't want to keep fixing one bug just to create another') — four parallel read-only audit agents covered order placement/sizing, position/balance reads, close/flatten paths, and reconciler/re-adopt-guard logic; this entry captures the three findings judged tightly coupled enough to fix together. FIX (all three parts landed in one PR): (1) AlpacaClient.close() now polls positions() after the DELETE, bounded by new ALPACA_CLOSE_CONFIRM_S (default 6.0s, <=0 restores legacy accept-is-success), mirroring IB_CLOSE_CONFIRM_S exactly — and deliberately reuses the exact 'not confirmed flat' retMsg substring order_monitor.py's existing IB-built cooldown/retry/consecutive-failure-alert machinery already keys on generically (by account/symbol/direction, not by exchange), so no other file needed to change for that half. (2) _recently_closed_adopted_orphan (src/runtime/order_monitor.py) widened to also match a row closed via exit_reason IN ('exchange_flat_reconciled', 'exit_coverage_no_strategy') regardless of setup_type — deliberately NOT widened to every close reason (a genuine broker-confirmed close like Bybit's reconciler_filled or a normal sl_cross/tp_cross strategy exit must never suppress a legitimate new position). (3) AlpacaClient.balance()/buying_power() truthiness bugs fixed in the same PR (see BL-20260707-ALPACA-BALANCE-TRUTHINESS below) since they surfaced in the same audit pass and are the same bug shape as the original BL-20260707-RECONCILER-MASS-FALSE-CLOSE fix.
items.trigger_condition: Already fixed this session; re-open if a close is ever confirmed accepted-but-not-flat again after this deploys, or if a phantom-close/re-adopt double-row is observed for a close_reason not covered by the widened guard.
items.resolution_criteria: Fix ships, deploys to the live VM, and is verified via git_sha; ideally also observed live the next time an Alpaca close is attempted near/after market hours (the close should now retry with the naked-sweep/cooldown machinery instead of fabricating a phantom close).

updates.note: Deploy-verified. PR #5923 (close-confirm-flat poll + widened re-adopt guard, bundled with BL-20260707-ALPACA-BALANCE-TRUTHINESS since they're the same audit pass) merged to main and confirmed live via git_sha. Superseding PR #5924 (entry-side rejection confirm, BL-20260707-ALPACA-ENTRY-FILL-CONFIRM-GAP) branched from post-#5923 main and is also merged+deployed, so #5923's changes are necessarily included in its ancestry — live VM git_sha 07f2b3fa matches #5924's merge commit exactly, confirming this fix is live. Closing on ships+deploys+git_sha-verified per resolution_criteria; the 'ideally also observed live near/after market hours' clause is a soft/optional condition (same standard applied to BL-20260707-RECONCILER-MASS-FALSE-CLOSE) — re-open per trigger_condition if a phantom-close/re-adopt double-row is ever observed post-deploy.

items.title: AlpacaClient.balance()/buying_power() treated a genuine $0 reading the same as 'could not read' — same truthiness bug shape as the already-fixed account_open_positions bug, one layer up
items.description: balance(): `float(acct.get('equity') or acct.get('cash'))` — a real equity=0.0 is falsy, so the code silently substitutes cash instead, even though equity is the more authoritative figure. buying_power(): `if bp > 0: return bp` inside a fallback loop over (regt_buying_power, buying_power, cash) — a genuine regt_buying_power=0.0 (fully invested / no free margin, a real and common account state) is treated identically to an unparseable value and falls through to a LESS authoritative key; if all three read non-positive, the function returns None. Coordinator.multi_account_execute reads a None buying_power() as 'could not determine' and falls back to a MORE PERMISSIVE sizing basis (total_account_usd/balance_usd x leverage) — exactly backwards: the account has genuinely zero free margin and the sizer should be MOST conservative, not fall back to a laxer basis. Verified safe to fix: Coordinator's caller already does `if _bp is not None: available_usd = _bp`, and RiskManager.position_size's margin cap correctly computes max_qty_by_margin=0 when available_usd=0.0, safely refusing the trade rather than erroring.
items.trigger_condition: Already fixed this session (shipped in the same PR as BL-20260707-ALPACA-CLOSE-NOT-CONFIRMED-FLAT).
items.resolution_criteria: Fix ships and deploys to the live VM.

updates.note: Deploy-verified alongside BL-20260707-ALPACA-CLOSE-NOT-CONFIRMED-FLAT — same PR (#5923), same deploy, same git_sha confirmation (live VM git_sha 07f2b3fa, PR #5924's merge sha, which supersedes #5923 in main's ancestry). balance()/buying_power() truthiness fixes are live.

items.title: Order ENTRY has the same accept-vs-fill confirmation gap as the close side (BL-20260707-ALPACA-CLOSE-NOT-CONFIRMED-FLAT) — a bracket order journals status='open' the instant it's HTTP-accepted, with a fabricated entry price, no fill confirmation
items.description: `_submit_order`'s Alpaca branch (src/units/accounts/execute.py ~line 780) treats any retCode==0 (HTTP 2xx accept) from AlpacaClient.place() as success; execute_pkg then calls `_log_trade_to_journal` synchronously right after, writing status='open' with entry_price=the strategy's DECISION-TIME signal price, not a broker-confirmed fill price — no polling for an actual fill. Lower urgency than the close-side bug fixed today: Alpaca bracket orders are time_in_force:day MARKET orders, which fill almost instantly during RTH, and RECONCILER_SNAPSHOT_MIN_FILL_AGE_S (BL-20260622-ALPACA-SNAPSHOT-FALSECLOSE) already exists specifically as a grace period for a just-placed order that hasn't propagated to the positions() snapshot yet — i.e. the team already knew entries can sit apparently-unfilled for minutes. NOT yet confirmed whether a genuinely NEVER-filled entry (rejected post-accept, or placed outside RTH) converges to a correct state or fabricates a phantom trade with fake entry+exit+PnL for a position that never existed on the broker — this needs tracing before a fix is designed. Deliberately deferred out of today's close-side fix: the entry path is more sensitive (delaying/blocking a live entry has different risk than delaying a close) and deserves its own careful design, not a bolt-on.
items.trigger_condition: Next full-pipeline review, or if a phantom-entry (journaled open, never actually filled) is observed live.
items.resolution_criteria: Trace whether a never-filled Alpaca entry self-heals cleanly today; if not, design + ship an entry-side fill-confirmation step analogous to the close-side fix, sized to the entry path's different risk profile (do not just copy-paste the close-side poll loop without considering it delays a live entry, not a close).

updates.note: Operator directive: 'finish the fix for the entry side as well - I want full pipeline stability.' Fixed the REJECTION half only, deliberately NOT the fill-price half (scoped down from the original description after tracing the actual call path). AlpacaClient.place() (src/units/accounts/alpaca_client.py) now polls the order by id after a successful POST, bounded by new ALPACA_PLACE_CONFIRM_S (default 3.0s, matching IB_PLACE_CONFIRM_S; <=0 restores legacy behaviour): a terminal rejected/canceled/expired status within the window is surfaced as retCode!=0 so _submit_order raises and _log_trade_to_journal never writes a phantom open trade row for an order the broker never actually executed. Still-pending or filled at the deadline is reported as success unchanged. Deliberately did NOT attempt to capture/backfill the broker's real filled_avg_price into the journal: _submit_order's return contract is a bare str (order_id) shared byte-for-byte across bybit/IB/alpaca/oanda; threading a richer fill-price payload through it would be a materially larger, cross-exchange refactor and a separate design decision, not a bug fix — the decision-time entry price is already the documented, accepted convention for every exchange here (IB's own IB_PLACE_CONFIRM_S only checks non-rejection too, never fill price), and RECONCILER_SNAPSHOT_MIN_FILL_AGE_S remains the existing downstream grace period for a genuinely-slow-but-good fill. Tests: test_client_place_confirms_no_rejection_when_filled, test_client_place_detects_post_accept_rejection, test_client_place_still_pending_at_deadline_is_success, test_client_place_confirm_disabled_restores_legacy_behavior (tests/test_alpaca_wiring.py) — 34/34 in that file, 323 across the broader order-management/reconciler surface.

items.title: Minor / lower-priority findings from the full Alpaca-pipeline audit — logged, not blocking
items.description: Bundle of smaller findings from the four-way parallel audit (order placement/sizing, position/balance reads, close/flatten paths, reconciler/re-adopt-guards), none individually urgent enough to block the main close-confirm fix: (1) OandaClient.close() (src/units/accounts/oanda_client.py) has the IDENTICAL accept-is-success defect as the pre-fix Alpaca close — not live-impactful today since oanda_practice is dry-run-only, but will need the same confirm-flat treatment before any OANDA account goes live. (2) No client_order_id / idempotency key on AlpacaClient.place()/place_protective() — a lost HTTP response to a timeout could leave a real accepted broker order un-journaled; low probability today since there's no retry loop in execute.py/coordinator.py to trigger a double-submit, but worth hardening. (3) The naked-position re-arm paths (_rearm_broker_protection_after_recovery, _check_broker_naked_equity_positions in order_monitor.py) size the re-armed OCO from trades.position_size (the journal column) rather than a fresh broker-side qty read — suspected staleness risk, not confirmed as a live bug, needs a follow-up trace of whether position_size reliably stays broker-truth-synced across partial fills/closes. (4) AlpacaClient.place_protective() duplicates whole_unit_qty's rounding logic inline instead of calling the shared helper place() already uses — cosmetic DRY violation, functionally identical today but a latent drift risk if the rounding rule ever changes. (5) _PENDING_ORPHAN_NOSTRAT_CLOSE (order_monitor.py) is never popped on the 'absent' (closed via disappear-confirm) branch, only the 'recoverable' branch — an unbounded but harmless in-process dict leak (trade ids are never reused). (6) _recover_orphan_order_package searches order packages of ANY status (not just open) for a symbol/direction/entry-price(+-2%) match — theoretical risk of resurrecting an already-closed, unrelated order package on a coincidental price match; not observed live, worth a defensive exclusion (e.g. skip packages whose linked trade closed via exit_coverage_no_strategy/exchange_flat_reconciled within the readopt-guard window) as a follow-up.
items.trigger_condition: Next /health-review, or before any OANDA account goes live (item 1 becomes Tier-2 at that point).
items.resolution_criteria: Each item addressed individually as time/priority allows; item 1 must be resolved before oanda_practice (or any OANDA account) flips off dry_run.
